fields.Text: What elements are needed to characterize and evaluate pain ? {{c1::Localization}} {{c1::Commencement/onset}} {{c1::Intensity}} {{c1::Provocation and attenuation}} {{c1::Duration}}
fields.Extra: Can you point to the offending tooth ? When did the symptoms first occur ? How intense is the pain ? What produces or reduces the symptoms ? Do the symptoms subside shortly, or do they linger after they are provoked ?
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Isolated increased probing depth can be the result of what ? - {{c1::Infection of endodontic origin}} - {{c1::Vertical root fracture}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Chronic endodontic infections can lead to the formation an intraoral communication known as a {{c1::sinus tract}}
fields.Extra: They can extend extraorally
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A positive response to a percussion test is an indication of inflammation of what structure ? {{c1::periodontal ligament}}
fields.Extra: inflammation due to physical trauma, periodontal disease, or extension of pulpal disease into PDL
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In the presence of a root fracture , when is a bite test painful ? {{c1::On release of biting pressure}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does tooth mobility increase or decrease with increasing PDL inflammation ? {{c1::increases}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the clinical and histologic term denoting inflammation of the dental pulp ? {{c1::pulpitis}}
fields.Extra: Clinically described as reversible or irreversible Histologically described as acute, chronic, hyperplastic
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How do you treat irreversible pulpitis ? {{c1::Endodontic treatment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In 2D xrays, evidence of pathosis can only be observed once the lesion extends to the junction of what two structures ? Junction of {{c1::Cortical bone}} and {{c1::trabecular bone}}
fields.Extra: Radiographic changes from bone loss will not be detected if the loss is only in cancellous bone. However, the radiographic evidence of pathosis will be observed once this bone loss extends to the junction of the cortical and cancellous bone . The apices of most anterior and premolar teeth are located close to the cortical-cancellous bone junction. Therefore, periapical pathosis from these teeth is exhibited sooner on the radiograph. By comparison, the distal roots of mandibular first molars and both roots of mandibular second molars are generally positioned more centrally within the cancellous bone, as are maxillary molars, especially the palatal roots. Periapical lesions from these roots must expand more before they reach the cortical-cancellous bone junction and are recognized as radiographic pathosis. For these reasons, it is important not to exclude the possibility of pulpal pathosis in situations in which there are no radiographic changes.( https://doi.org/10.1097/00004770-200311000-00006 )
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What tests should be systematically be done when evaluating a symptomatic tooth ? - {{c1::Palpation}} - {{c1::Mobility}} - {{c1::Percussion}} - {{c1::Periodontal probing}} - {{c1::Pulp test}} - {{c1::Peri-apical Rx}}
fields.Extra: Bite test can also inform on eventual fractures
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does a tooth with a necrotic pulp respond to a pulp test ? {{c1::asymptomatic}}
fields.Extra: If only part of the pulp is necrotic, there may be some response (eg. multi-rooted teeth)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A tooth with a necrotic pulp may become hypersensitive to cold or heat ? {{c1::heat}}
fields.Extra: Relieved by applications of cold
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long do symptoms of a pulp test last on a normal pulp ? {{c1::a few seconds}}
fields.Extra: Symptoms are mild , cause no distress , result in a transient sensation that resolves in seconds.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does a tooth with reversible pulpitis respond to a pulp test ? {{c1::sharp quickly reversible pain}}
fields.Extra: Usually considered as lasting less than 30 seconds Pain can be in response to thermal, tactile, mechanical, osmotic, or chemical stimuli
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does a tooth with irreversible pulpitis respond to a pulp test ? {{c1::heightened and prolonged pain even after removal of stimuli}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is there intermittent or spontaneous pain in symptomatic irreversible pulpitis ? {{c1::Yes::Yes/No}}
fields.Extra: Pain can be sharp, dull, localized, diffuse, or referred
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is there intermittent or spontaneous pain in reversible pulpitis ? {{c1::No, only in response to a stimuli::Yes/No}}
fields.Extra: chemical, osmotic, thermal, tactile, mechanical
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does a tooth with asymptomatic ir reversible pulpitis respond to a pulp test ? {{c1::normal response to pulp test}}
fields.Extra: More or less, sometimes it can be painful
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How do you treat reversible pulpitis ? {{c1::Eliminate the causative irritating factor}}
fields.Extra: Causative factors include caries, exposed dentin, defective restoration
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How do you treat a necrotic pulp ? {{c1::Endodontic treatment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A painful tooth response on biting or percussion with/without pain on palpation is indicative of what periapical diagnosis ? {{c1::Symptomatic (acute) apical periodontitis}}
fields.Extra: - May or may not respond to pulp test - Usually exhibits a widenend PDL - May or may not show apical radiolucency (sign of chronic inflammation = acute exacerbation of asymptomatic apical periodontitis) aka acute apical periodontitis
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does a tooth with symptomatic apical periodontitis respond to pulp vitality tests ? {{c1::Sometimes, depending on whether or not the pulp is necrotic}}
fields.Extra: - May or may not respond to pulp test - Usually exhibits a widenend PDL - May or may not show apical radiolucency (sign of chronic inflammation = symptomatic phase of asymptomatic apical periodontitis) aka acute apical periodontitis
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When would a tooth with symptomatic apical periodontitis exhibit an apical radiolucency ? {{c1::Acute exacerbation of asymptomatic apical periodontitis}}
fields.Extra: - May or may not respond to pulp test - Usually exhibits a widenend PDL - May or may not show apical radiolucency (sign of chronic inflammation = acute exacerbation of asymptomatic apical periodontitis) aka acute apical periodontitis
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Both symptomatic and asymptomatic apical periodontitis are defined as the {{c1::inflammation}} of apical periodontium, though asymptomatic apical periodontitis also results in the {{c2::destruction}} of apical periodontium.
fields.Extra: Destruction visible as radiolucency in Rx An apical radiolucency in a tooth with symptomatic apical periodontitis is a sign of acute excacerbation of asymptomatic apical periodontitis.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An apical radiolucency in a tooth with symptomatic apical periodontitis is a sign of acute excacerbation of what condition ? {{c1::asymptomatic apical periodontitis}}
fields.Extra: An apical radiolucency in a tooth with symptomatic apical periodontitis is a sign of acute exacerbation of asymptomatic apical periodontitis.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An apical radiolucency is a sign of {{c1::chronic}} periapical inflammation that has led to destruction and the formation of granulation tissue or a radicular cyst.
fields.Extra: Destruction visible as radiolucency in Rx An apical radiolucency in a tooth with symptomatic apical periodontitis is a sign of acute exacerbation of asymptomatic apical periodontitis.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the periapical diagnosis of a tooth exhibiting no clinical symptoms and where an apical radiolucency is present in the Rx ? {{c1::asymptomatic apical periodontitis (chronic)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In the case of asymptomatic apical periodontitis is a tooth sensitive to biting or percussion ? {{c1::Generally not sensitive though it may "feel different"}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does a tooth with an acute apical abscess exhibit spontaneous pain ? {{c1::Yes}}
fields.Extra: Acutely painful to biting, percussion, palpation
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An acute apical abscess is the inflammatory response to pulpal infection and {{c1::necrosis}}.
fields.Extra: Pulp vitality test negative
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Will an acute apical abscess respond to a pulp vitality test ? {{c1::No, pulp is necrotic}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Will a chronic apical abscess respond to a pulp vitality test ? {{c1::No, pulp is necrotic}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Will an acute apical abscess present an apical radiolucency ? {{c1::Sometimes, during acute exacerbation of chronic apical abscess}}
fields.Extra: = Phoenix abscess
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What intra-oral sign may appear in an acute apical abscess ? {{c1::Swelling of adjacent tissue}}
fields.Extra: Only once infection escapes into the medullary cavity of the bone, oedema may develop in the soft tissues of the face
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What extra-oral signs may be associated with an acute apical abscess ? - {{c1::Fever}} - {{c1::Tender cervical and submandibular lymph nodes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What intra-oral element is always present in a chronic apical abscess ? {{c1::sinus tract}}
fields.Extra: Allows intermittent discharge of pus
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Will a chronic apical abscess present an apical radiolucency ? {{c1::Yes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Will a chronic apical abscess usually present clinical symptoms (pain/discomfort) ? {{c1::No, but tooth may "feel different"}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What intra-oral element allows you to distinguish between asymptomatic apical periodontitis and a chronic apical abscess ? {{c1::Sinus tract}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The acute exacerbation of a chronic apical abscess is called a {{c1::phoenix abscess}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How do teeth with a chronic apical abscess respond to biting and percussion ? {{c1::Not sensitive, but may "feel different"}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the granulation tissue around the opening of an intra-oral sinus tract called ? {{c1::parulis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Reactive bone formation in response to a chronic periapical lesion is called {{c1::condensing osteitis}}
fields.Extra: aka chronic focal sclerosing osteomylelitis
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: If an acute apical abscess does not form a sinus tract and become chronic, it can evolve into what other types of infections ? - {{c1::Osteomyelitis}} - {{c1::Cellulitis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 4 principal components of the periodontium ? - {{c1::gingiva}} - {{c1::periodontal ligament}} - {{c1::cementum}} - {{c1::alveolar bone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The oral mucosa consists of what three types of mucosa ? - {{c1::masticatory mucosa}} - {{c1::specialized mucosa}} - {{c1::lining mucosa}}
fields.Extra: masticatory = palate + gingiva (keratinized) specialized = dorsum of tongue lining = the rest of the oral cavity (non-keratinized)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In an adult, normal gingiva covers the alveolar bone and tooth root to a level just coronal to what dental landmark ? {{c1::cemento-enamel junction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 types of gingiva based on their anatomical location ? - {{c1::Attached gingiva}} - {{c1::Marginal gingiva}} - {{c1::Interdental or papillary gingiva}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In 50% of cases, the marginal gingiva is demarcated from the attached gingiva by the {{c1::free gingival groove}}
fields.Extra: aka marginal groove
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the maximum probing depth of a clinically normal gingival sulcus ? {{c1::3 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the demarcation between attached gingiva and the alveolar mucosa called ? {{c1::mucogingival junction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The width of the attached gingiva is the distance between what ? {{c1::mucogingival junction}} and {{c1::projection of the bottom of the gingival sulcus or periodontal pocket}}
fields.Extra: Not to be confused with width of keratinized gingiva (MGJ to top of marginal gingiva)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The width of the attached gingiva is greatest in the anterior or posterior regions ? {{c1::anterior region}}
fields.Extra: Anterior : 3.5 to 4.5 mm in the maxilla and 3.3 to 3.9 mm in the mandible Posterior : 1.9 mm in the maxillary first premolars and 1.8 mm in the mandibular first premolars
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does the location of the mucoginginval junction move throughout adult life ? {{c1::No::Yes/No}}
fields.Extra: Changes in the attached gingiva are caused by modifications in the coronal portion.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The interdental gingiva can either have a pyramidal shape or a {{c1:: col }} shape depending on the proximity to the contact point .
fields.Extra: Pyramidal : tip of papilla is located immediately beneath the contact point Col : valley-like depression that connects a facial and lingual papilla and conforms to the shape of the interproximal contact
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The oral mucosa is composed of what layers of tissue ? {{c1::oral epithelium}} and {{c1::lamina propria}}
fields.Extra: stratified squamous epithelium and connective tissue
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In what regions is the oral mucosa attached directly to the periosteum of underlying bone, with no intervening submucosa ? {{c1::gingiva }} and {{c1::hard palate}}
fields.Extra: = mucoperiosteum (masticatory mucosa)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Oral mucosa with no submucosa is called {{c1::mucoperiosteum}}.
fields.Extra: gingiva and hard palate
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The minor salivary glands are located in which layer of the oral mucosa ? {{c1::submucosa}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The nodules of lymphoid tissue are located in which layer of the oral mucosa ? {{c1::epithelium}}
fields.Extra: Form crypts of invaginated epithelium into the lamina propria. These areas are infiltrated extensively by lymphocytes and plasma cells
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of epithelium is the oral epithelium ? {{c1::stratified squamous epithelium:: ______ ________ epithelium}}
fields.Extra: Can be keratinized or not depending on the location
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 types of gingival epithelium ? {{c1::oral epithelium}}, {{c1::sulcular epithelium}}, and {{c1::junctional epithelium}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Remarks: JE = Junctional Epithelium SE = Sulculare Epithelium OE = Oral Epithelium
JE = Junctional Epithelium SE = Sulculare Epithelium OE = Oral Epithelium
JE = Junctional Epithelium SE = Sulculare Epithelium OE = Oral Epithelium

fields.Text: What is the principal cell type of the oral epithelium ? {{c1::keratinocyte}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A complete keratinization process leads to the production of a superficial epithelial layer described as {{c1::orthokeratinized}}
fields.Extra: orthokeratinized = no nuclei in the keratinized layer parakeratinized = absence of granular layer and presence of pyknotic nuclei
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Melanocytes are present in what layers of the oral epithelium ? {{c1::basal layer}} and {{c1::spinous layer}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Melanocytes synthesize melanin in what organelles ? {{c1::melanosomes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Langherans cells are located in which layers of the oral epithelium ? {{c1::all supra-basal layers}}
fields.Extra: = dendritic cells
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What cells in the oral epithelium are the principal antigen-presenting cells ? {{c1::Langherans cells}}
fields.Extra: Present in all supra-basal layers
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What cells in the oral epithelium act as tactile receptors ? {{c1::Merkel cells}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Keratinocytes are connected to each other by what cellular structures ? {{c1::desmosomes}} {{c1::adhesion belt}} {{c1::tight junctions}} {{c1::gap junctions}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Keratinocytes are connected to the basal lamina by what structures ? {{c1::hemidesmosomes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Gingival epithelium : Is the oral epithelium keratinized ? {{c1::Yes (keratizined or parakeratinized)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is the sulcular epithelium keratinized ? {{c1::No}}
fields.Extra: acts as a semipermeable membrane through which bacteria can enter and tissue fluid can seep out (it is less permeable than juncitonal epithelium)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Gingival epithelium : Is the junctional epithelium keratinized ? {{c1::No}}
fields.Extra: acts as an epithelial barrier against plaque bacteria and
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Gingival epithelium : The junctional epithelium attaches to enamel and cementum surfaces via what structure ? {{c1::internal basal lamina}}
fields.Extra: JE connected to the IBL by hemidesmosomes
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The majority of gingival fluid diffuses through which epithelium ? {{c1::junctional epithelium}}
fields.Extra: Roles : - cleanse sulcus - diffuse proteins that improve adhesion - antimicrobial - exert antibody activity
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The gingival fibers present in the connective tissue of the oral mucosa consists of type I {{c1::collagen}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 5 types of gingival fibers ? - {{c1::gingivodental}} - {{c1::circular}} - {{c1::transseptal}} - {{c1::semi-circular}} - {{c1::trans-gingival}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which gingival fibers extend from the cementum at the base of the sulcus and fan out externally ? {{c1::gingivodental fibers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which gingival fibers encircle the tooth through the marginal and interdental gingivae ? {{c1::circular fibers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which gingival fibers join appromixating teeth by the cementum located interproximally ? {{c1::transseptal fibers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which gingival fibers attach the proximal surfaces of a same tooth and go around the facial or lingual marginal gingiva ? {{c1::semicircular fibers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which gingival fibers attach interproximally on one tooth, contour the approximal tooth and attach interproximally to the following tooth ? {{c1::transgingival fibers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the preponderant cell type in the lamina propria of oral mucosa ? {{c1::fibroblast}}
fields.Extra: They play a role in the development, maintenance, and repair of the connective tissue They synthesize collagen and elastic fibers as well as glycoproteins and glycoaminoglycans Also present are mast cells, adipocytes, macrophages
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the main cell type responsible for immune response in the lamina propria of oral mucosa ? {{c1::mast cell (mastocyte)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The color of healthy attached and marginal gingiva is generally described as {{c1::coral pink}}
fields.Extra: Color varies among different persons and appears to be correlated with cutaneous pigmentation
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What color is healthy alveolar mucosa ? {{c1::red}}
fields.Extra: red, shiny, smooth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The texture of attached gingiva is described as {{c1::stippled}}
fields.Extra: marginal gingiva is smooth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is marginal gingiva stippled ? {{c1::No::Yes/No}}
fields.Extra: marginal gingiva is smooth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The contour of gingiva is generally described as {{c1::scalloped}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The consistency of healthy gingiva is generally described as {{c1::firm}}
fields.Extra: firm and resilient, and tightly bound to the underlying bone except for the marginal gingiva
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The process of apical shift of the gingival margin caused by different conditions or pathologies is called {{c1::recession}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What structure surrounds the tooth root and connects it to the inner wall of the alveolar bone ? {{c1::periodontal ligament}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of tissue is the periodontal ligament ? {{c1::connective tissue}}
fields.Extra: complex vascular and highly cellular that is continuous with the connective tissue of the gingiva
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The principle fibers of the PDL take root in what type of bone lining the alveolar socket ? {{c1::bundle bone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What collagen structures arranged in bundles within the PDL bridge alveolar bone and cementum ? {{c1::principal fibers}}
fields.Extra: Forms a continuous anastomosing network
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the terminal portions of the principal fibers inserted into the cementum and bone called ? {{c1::Sharpey fibers}}
fields.Extra: Once they embed in the alveolar bone or cementum they calcify to a sinificant degree
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The principal fibers are divided into what 6 groups ? - {{c1::transseptal}} - {{c1::alveolar crest}} - {{c1::horizontal}} - {{c1::oblique}} - {{c1::apical}} - {{c1::interradicular}}
fields.Extra: transseptal fibers can be considered part of the gingiva since they do not have any osseous attachment
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Approximately how thick is the periodontal ligament ? {{c1::0.2 mm}}
fields.Extra: varies with tooth function and age
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What connective tissue cells are present in the periodontal ligament ? {{c1::fibroblasts}}, {{c1::cementoblasts}}, {{c1::osteoblasts}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Periodontal ligament : What immature form of elastin runs parrallel to the root surface and attaches to cementum ? {{c1::oxytalan}}
fields.Extra: Present in the cervical third of the root. They are thought to regulate vascular flow.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Periodontal ligament : Oxytalan fibers runs {{c1:: parrallel ::perpendicular/parrallel}} to the root surface and attaches to cementum in the {{c2:: cervical }} third of the root.
fields.Extra: Most present in the cervical third of the root. They are thought to regulate vascular flow.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Periodontal ligament : Oxytalan fibers are thought to regulate {{c1::vascular}} flow in relation to tooth function.
fields.Extra: Most present in the cervical third of the root. They are thought to regulate vascular flow.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Periodontal ligament : The epithelial rests of Malassez are considered remnants of the {{c1::Hertwig root sheath}} which disintegrates during root development.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Periodontal ligament : Are the epithelial rests of Malassez closer to the alveolar bone or to the cementum ? {{c1::cementum}}
fields.Extra: Most numerous in the apical and cervical area When these epithelial cells proliferate they can form periapical cysts and lateral root cysts
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of epithelial cells are present in the periodontal ligament ? {{c1::Epithelial rests of Malassez}}
fields.Extra: Present as clusters or interlacing strands When these epithelial cells proliferate they can form periapical cysts and lateral root cysts
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the calcified masses within the PDL called ? {{c1::cementicles}}
fields.Extra: Adherent to or detached from root surface
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the functions of the periodontal ligament ? - {{c1::support/physical}} - {{c2::formative}} - {{c3::remodeling}} - {{c4::nutritional}} - {{c5::sensory}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does cementum have blood vessels ? {{c1::No}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is cementum calcified ? {{c1::Yes}}
fields.Extra: Inorganic content of cementum (hydroxyapatite; Ca10[Po4]6[OH]2) is 45% to 50%, which is less than that of bone (65%), enamel (97%), or dentin (70%)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Primary cementum is {{c1::acellular::(a)cellular}} whereas secondary cementum is {{c1::cellular::(a)cellular}}
fields.Extra: Primary cementum is formed first and is located in coronal portion of the root Both consist of calcified interfibrillar matrix and collagen fibrils
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cementum is classified according to what two criteria ? - {{c1::origin of the collagen fibers}} - {{c2::presence or absence of cells}}
fields.Extra: Both consist of calcified interfibrillar matrix and collagen fibrils
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of cementum provides attachment for the tooth ? {{c1::acellular cementum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of cementum is associated with adaptation and repair of periodontal tissues ? {{c1::cellular cementum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the extrinsic fibers of the cementum ? {{c1::sharpey fibers}}
fields.Extra: formed by fibroblasts in the PDL whereas intrinsic fibers are formed by cementoblasts
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What cells produce the intrinsic fibers of the cementum ? {{c1::cementoblasts}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Acellular cementum covers which half of the tooth root ? {{c1::coronal half}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cellular cementum covers which half of the tooth root ? {{c1::apical half}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cementogenesis : During root formation, ectomesenchymal cells of the dental follicle migrate through gaps in {{c1:: Hertwig’s epithelial root sheath }} and differentiate into {{c2:: cementoblasts }}
fields.Extra: They secrete cementoid (cementum matrix)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cementogenesis : Once cementoblasts become covered in cementum they are known as {{c1::cementocytes}} which reside in lacunae
fields.Extra: They secrete cementoid (cementum matrix)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cementum most closely resembles what other tissue ? {{c1::bone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why does the lack of a cementoenamel junction result in accentuated sensitivity in cases of gingival recession ? {{c1::exposed dentin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does the thickness of cementum increase or decrease with age ? {{c1::increases}}
fields.Extra: hard to distinguish between pathological thickening of the cementum (hypercementosis) and physiological thickening (No Tx for hypercementosis)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Fusion of the {{c1::cementum}} to alveolar bone with obliteration of the PDL is called {{c2::ankylosis}}
fields.Extra: May develop after chronic periapical inflammation, tooth replantation, occlusal trauma and around embedded teeth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Functions of the cementum : - {{c1::Support}} - {{c2::Protection from resorption}} - {{c3::Formation (continuous eruption)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Ankylosis results in the resorption of the root and its gradual replacement by what kind of tissue ? {{c1::bone tissue}}
fields.Extra: Replanted teeth that ankylose will lose their roots after 4 to 5 years and will be exfoliated. Clinically, ankylosed teeth lack the physiologic mobility of normal teeth, which is one diagnostic sign for ankylotic resorption. In addition, these teeth usually have a special metallic percussion sound ; if the ankylotic process continues, they will be in infraocclusion. However, the clinical diagnosis of ankylosis by mobility and percussion tests alone is only reliable when at least 20% of the root surface is affected.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Alveolar bone : The external plate of cortical bone is formed by what type of bone ? {{c1::haversian bone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the inner socket wall of thin compact bone that includes bundle bone called ? {{c1::alveolar bone proper}}
fields.Extra: Bundle bone is localized within the alveolar bone proper. aka lamina dura
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the bone structure between adjacent teeth and between roots called ? {{c1::septum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Radiographically, what is alveolar bone proper called ? {{c1::lamina dura}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the other name for trabecular or spongy bone ? {{c1::cancellous bone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Both cementocytes and osteocytes communicate to other cells through what structures ? {{c1::canaliculi}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most apical part of the marginal gingiva called ? {{c1::zenith}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is the alveolar bone proper trabecular bone or lamellated bone ? {{c1::lamellated bone}}
fields.Extra: Bundle bone is localized within the alveolar bone proper.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are isolated areas in which the root is denuded of bone and the root surface is covered only by periosteum and overlying gingiva called ? {{c1::fenestrations}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are areas in which the root is denuded of bone through the marginal bone and the root surface is covered only by periosteum and overlying gingiva called ? {{c1::dehiscence}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Helper T cells (CD4+) are so-called because they help produce an inflammatory response by producing {{c1::pro-inflammatory cytokines}}
fields.Extra: stimulate proliferation of more T cells. stimulate macrophages to become super alert and vigilant (macrophage activation). activation of mast cells. recruitment of neutrophils and monocytes. activate CD8+ T cells. activate B cells.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cytotoxic T cells (CD8+) are so-called because they recognize antigens by an infected cell and destroy the infected cell via proteins such as {{c1::perforins}} and {{c2::granzymes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When the bottom of a periodontal pocket is apical to the level of the adjacent alveolar bone it is termed an {{c1::intrabony pocket}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The pattern of bone destruction found in intrabony defects is {{c1::vertical/angular}}
fields.Extra: Suprabony pockets usually present horizontal bone destruction.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A pathologically deepened gingival sulcus is termed a {{c1::periodontal pocket}}.
fields.Extra: Deepening of the sulcus may occur due to gingival enlargement without destruction of underlying tissues --> pseudo-pocket
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A deepening of the gingival sulcus due to gingival enlargement without destruction of underlying periodontal tissues is called a {{c1::pseudo-pocket}}
fields.Extra: eg. some patients taking calcium channel blockers, cyclosporine, anticonvulsants
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A periodontal pocket leads to the destruction of {{c1::the underlying periodontal tissues}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Changes involved in the transition from the normal gingival sulcus to the pathologic periodontal pocket are associated with different proportions of {{c1::bacterial cells in dental plaque}}.
fields.Extra: Diseased gingiva is associated with increased numbers of spirochetes and motile rods , whereas healthy gingiva is mostly coccoid cells and straight rods.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is periodontal destruction in periodontitis a result of bacterial action ? {{c1::No! It's the host's immunoinflammatory response to persistent bacterial attack}}
fields.Extra: 1. Matrix-metalloproteinases 2. Fibroblast collagen phagocytosis at the ligament-cementum interface
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The rational for pocket reduction is based on the need to eliminate areas of {{c1::plaque accumulation}}.
fields.Extra: Stagnation of bacteria leads to chronic inflammation of the surounding tissues leading to perio destruction by the host.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The loss of attachment produced by periodontitis is characterized by periods of activity and inactivity or is it slow but continuously progressive ? {{c1::periods of activity and inactivity}}
fields.Extra: inactivity = low/no inflammation/bone loss activity = buildup of gram -, motile anaerobic bacteria leads to inflammation
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which classification is most often used to describe tooth mobility ? {{c1::Miller mobility index (1950)}}
fields.Extra: 0: No distinguishable movement (<0.2mm) 1: first distinguishable sign of movement greater than “normal” (>0.2mm) 2: movement of the crown up to 1 mm in any direction 3: movement of the crown more than 1 mm in any direction or vertical depression or rotation of the crown in its socket Physiologic mobility is movement up to 0.2 mm horizontally and 0.02 mm axially. Mobility beyond the physiologic range is termed abnormal or pathologic. It is pathologic in that it exceeds the limits of normal mobility values; however, the periodontium is not necessarily diseased at the time of examination.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does a Miller mobility index score of 0 signify ? {{c1::No distinguishable movement (<0.2mm mobility) = Physiologic}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does a Miller mobility index score of 2 signify ? {{c1::Movement of the crown up to 1 mm in any direction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does a Miller mobility index score of 1 signify ? {{c1::First distinguishable sign of movement greater than “normal” (>0.2mm)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does a Miller mobility index score of 3 signify ? {{c1::Movement of the crown more than 1 mm in any direction or vertical depression or rotation of the crown in its socket}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Physiologic mobility is movement up to {{c1::0.2}} mm horizontally and {{c1::0.02}} mm axially.
fields.Extra: 0: No distinguishable movement (<0.2mm) 1: first distinguishable sign of movement greater than “normal” (>0.2mm) 2: movement of the crown up to 1 mm in any direction 3: movement of the crown more than 1 mm in any direction or vertical depression or rotation of the crown in its socket Physiologic mobility is movement up to 0.2 mm horizontally and 0.02 mm axially. Mobility beyond the physiologic range is termed abnormal or pathologic. It is pathologic in that it exceeds the limits of normal mobility values; however, the periodontium is not necessarily diseased at the time of examination.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Tooth mobility beyond the physiologic range is termed {{c1::abnormal}} or {{c1::pathologic}}.
fields.Extra: 0: No distinguishable movement (<0.2mm) 1: first distinguishable sign of movement greater than “normal” (>0.2mm) 2: movement of the crown up to 1 mm in any direction 3: movement of the crown more than 1 mm in any direction or vertical depression or rotation of the crown in its socket Physiologic mobility is movement up to 0.2 mm horizontally and 0.02 mm axially. Mobility beyond the physiologic range is termed abnormal or pathologic. It is pathologic in that it exceeds the limits of normal mobility values; however, the periodontium is not necessarily diseased at the time of examination.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Uncomplicated enamel-dentin fracture of primary tooth {{c1::Restore}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Crown-root fracture of primary tooth if tooth is restorable with pulp exposure {{c1::Partial pulpotomy or RCT and restore}}
fields.Extra: Tx depends on size of exposed pulp, stage of root development, and level of the fracture
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Enamel fracture of primary tooth {{c1::Smooth sharp edges}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Alveolar fracture of a primary tooth {{c1::Reposition displaced segments + flexible splint (4 weeks)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Subluxation of primary tooth {{c1::None}}
fields.Extra: Widened PDL will be visible
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Extrusive luxation of primary tooth if tooth is excessively mobile or > 3mm extruded {{c1::Extraction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Crown-root fracture of a primary tooth if tooth is restorable without pulp exposure {{c1::Remove loose fragment and restore}}
fields.Extra: Needed to identify the extent and position of the fracture line
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Crown-root fracture of primary tooth if tooth is not restorable {{c1::Extract all loose fragments and if possible apical fragment}}
fields.Extra: Tx depends on size of exposed pulp, stage of root development, and level of the fracture
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Root fracture of primary tooth if coronal fragment not displaced {{c1::No treatment necessary}}
fields.Extra: establish baseline and identify shape/extent/positio of the fracture
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Root fracture of primary tooth if coronal fragment displaced and excessively mobile : - {{c1::Extract only the loose fragment}} OR - {{c1::Flexible splint for 4 weeks}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Root fracture of primary tooth if coronal fragment displaced but not excessively mobile {{c1::Spontaneous reposition}}
fields.Extra: Even if there is some (a little...) occlusal interference
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Complicated enamel-dentin fracture of primary tooth {{c1::Vital pulp therapy}}
fields.Extra: Small exposure or < 48h orients towards partial pulpotomy Large exposure or > 48h orients towards cervical pulpotomy
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Lateral luxation of primary tooth if there is minimal or no occlusal interference {{c1::Spontaneous repositioning or minimal grinding}}
fields.Extra: Increased PDL space
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Concussion of primary tooth {{c1::None necessary}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Extrusive luxation of primary tooth if no occlusal interference {{c1::Spontaneous reposition}}
fields.Extra: Identify increased PDL space and check for fractures
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Intrusive luxation of primary tooth {{c1::Tooth should be allowed to spontaneously reposition irrespective of direction of displacement}}
fields.Extra: Improvement in the position should occur within 6 months Note : previous recommendations (2012) indicated extraction if fragment oriented towards tooth germ or in absence of spontaneous eruption
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Avulsion of primary tooth {{c1::None}}
fields.Extra: /!\ Primary teeth should not be replanted !!
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Lateral luxation of primary tooth if there is severe displacement {{c1::Extraction (if ingestion or aspiration risk)}} OR {{c1::Reposition and flexible splint for 4 weeks}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Diagnosis of peri-implant health requirements : - {{c1::Absence of clinical signs of inflammation}} - {{c1::Absence of bleeding or suppuration on probing}} - {{c1::No increase in probing depth compared to previous examinations}} - {{c1::Absence of bone loss beyond initial remodeling}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Case definition of peri-implant mucositis : - {{c1::Presence of bleeding and/or suppuration on gentle probing with or without increased probing depths relative to baseline}} - {{c1::Absence of bone loss beyond baseline}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Case definition of peri-implantitis : - {{c1::Bleeding and/or suppuration on probing}} - {{c1::Increased probing depths relative to baseline}} - {{c1::Bone loss beyond inital remodeling}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Complicated crown-root fracture on a restorable mature permanent tooth {{c1::RCT and restore}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Alveolar fracture of a permanent tooth {{c1::Reposition and flexible splint for 4 weeks (monitor pulp)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Root fracture on a permanent tooth if fracture is in the cervical 1/3 {{c1::Reposition and flexible splint for 4 weeks (up to 4 months)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Intrusive luxation on a mature permanent tooth if < 3mm intrusion {{c1::spontaneous repositioning and if no eruption after 8 weeks, surgical reposition + RCT}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Extrusive luxation of a permanent tooth {{c1::Reposition and flexible splint for 2 weeks + endo re-evaluation }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Intrusive luxation of a mature permanent tooth if 3-7mm intrusion {{c1::surgical or orthodontic repositioning + root canal therapy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Root fracture on a permanent tooth if fracture is in the apical 2/3 {{c1::Reposition and flexible splint for 4 weeks}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Complicated crown-root fracture on a restorable immature permanent tooth {{c1::Vital pulp therapy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Uncomplicated crown-root fracture on a permanent tooth {{c1::Bond if feasible, or extract mobile fragment and restore}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Complicated crown-root fracture on a permanent tooth unrestorable through direct methods {{c1::Extrusion + RCT + restore}} OR {{c1::Extraction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Lateral luxation on a an immature permanent tooth {{c1::Reposition and 4 weeks splint (monitor pulp)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Subluxation on a permanent tooth {{c1::None or flexible splint for 2 weeks if mobile or tender}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Lateral luxation on a mature permanent tooth {{c1::Reposition and splint 4 weeks + RCT}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In descending order of preference, what storage mediums are suitable for an avulsed tooth ? 1. {{c1::Milk}} 2 .{{c1::Hank's Balanced Salt Solution (HBSS)}} 3. {{c1::Saliva}} 4. {{c1::Saline}} 5. {{c1::Water}}
fields.Extra: "In descending order of preference, milk, HBSS, saliva (after spitting into a glass for instance), or saline are suitable and convenient storage mediums. Although water is a poor medium, it is better than leaving the tooth to air-dry." - IADT 2020
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Uncomplicated enamel-dentin fracture on a permanent tooth {{c1::Restore}}
fields.Extra: Treatment depends on proximity to pulp and fragment availability
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For a permanent tooth avulsion replanted at site of injury , what is the timeframe for repositioning an inadequately replanted tooth ? {{c1::48 hours}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the expected outcome of delayed replantation (> 60 minutes extra-oral dry time) regarding the PDL ? {{c1::replacement resorption (ankylosis)}}
fields.Extra: - Expected outcome is replacement resorption (ankylosis) for both mature and immature teeth - replantation in these cases is to restore, at least temporarily, esthetics and function while maintaining alveolar bone contour, width, and height - Worth replanting because it keeps treatment options open (extraction, decoronation, autotransplantation) PDL = periodontal ligament
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Should a patient try and replant a permanent tooth immediately ? {{c1::Yes!}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What diameter of flexible wire for flexible splinting of an avulsed tooth ? {{c1::0.4 mm}}
fields.Extra: Nylon fishing line can also be used but only in absence of alveolar fracture
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Complicated enamel-dentin fracture on a permanent tooth {{c1::Vital pulp therapy}}
fields.Extra: For immature roots : < 1mm 2 pulp and < 24h : direct pulp cap < 1mm 2 pulp and 24-72h : partial pulpotomy > 1mm 2 pulp and > 72h : cervical pulpotomy For mature roots : attempt to preserve, but if > 48h since exposure, RCT
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Concussion on a permanent tooth {{c1::None}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Enamel only fracture on a permanent tooth {{c1::Smooth of edges and restore if needed}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Intrusive luxation on a mature permanent tooth if > 7mm intrusion {{c1::surgical repositioning + flexible splint + RCT}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is endodontic therapy systematic for avulsed teeth with closed apexes ? {{c1::Yes, within 2 weeks of replantation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: After what length of extra-oral dry time are PDL cells most likely non-viable ? {{c1::> 60 minutes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Correct replantation position of a tooth should be verified both {{c1::clinically}} and {{c1::radiologically}}.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: If extra-oral dry time is > 60 minutes, placing the tooth in a {{c1::2% sodium fluoride}} solution for 20 minutes before replantation may slow resorption .
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is administration of systemic antibiotics after replantation systematic ? {{c1::Yes}}
fields.Extra: Periodontal ligament often becomes contaminated by bacteria from the oral cavity, storage medium, or environment in which the avulsion occured. Systemic antibiotics are recommended to prevent infection-related reactions and to decrease the occurence of inflammatory root resorption
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What properties of doxycycline make it interesting after replantation of a tooth ? - {{c1::anti-microbial}} - {{c1::anti-resorptive}} - {{c1::anti-inflammatory}}
fields.Extra: Doxycycline has anti-microbial, anti-inflammatory and anti-resorptive effets and may be used in patients over 12 years of age. Animal studies have demonstrated effectiveness of tetracyclines.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long should a flexible splint be applied to an avulsed tooth ? {{c1::2 weeks, unless alveolar fracture in which case 4 weeks}}
fields.Extra: Immature teeth may require longer splinting
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What antibiotics are recommended after replantation of a tooth ? {{c1::Amoxicillin or Penicillin}}
fields.Extra: Doxycycline has anti-microbial, anti-inflammatory and anti-resorptive effets and may be used in patients over 12 years of age
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Treatment : Intrusive luxation on an immature permanent tooth {{c1::Spontaneous repositioning regardless of degree of intrusion for 4 weeks, and if no eruption then orthondontic extrusion + 2 week endo re-evaluation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Up till what length of extra-oral dry time are PDL cells most likely viable ? {{c1::15 minutes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When might replantation of a permanent tooth be contraindicated ? {{c1::Severe caries, periodontal disease, immunosuppression, endocarditis risk, uncooperative patient}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: After replantation of a tooth, the patient should be referred to their physician for evaluation of the need for what type of booster shot ? {{c1::tetanus}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Up to what length of extra-oral dry time may PDL cells be viable but compromised ? {{c1::15-60 minutes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Delayed replantation (> 60 minutes extra-oral dry time) results in {{c1::necrosis}} of the periodontal ligament.
fields.Extra: - Expected outcome is replacement resorption (ankylosis) - Still worth replanting because it keeps treatment options open (extraction, decoronation, autotransplantation)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the recommended minimum age for administering tetracyclines ? {{c1::12 years of age}}
fields.Extra: Risk of discoloration of permanent teeth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What phenomenon is possible in avulsed immature teeth that is not feasible in mature teeth ? {{c1::revascularization}}
fields.Extra: - Allows continued root development and maturation - Endo should not be initiated unless signs of necrosis
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Are cleft lips more often unilateral or bilateral ? {{c1::unilateral (80%)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cleft lips are due to the lack of fusion between which two embryological structures ? {{c1::medial nasal process}} and {{c1::maxillary process}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A cleft palate is due to the lack of fusion between what embryological structures ? {{c1::palatal shelves}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Are cleft palates or cleft lips more frequent ? {{c1::Cleft lips }}
fields.Extra: Cleft lip = 1/1000 births Cleft palate 1/2000 births
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Congenital invaginations at the labial commissures or near the midline of the lower lips are termed {{c1::congenital lip pits}}
fields.Extra: = sinus tracts that may extend to a depth of 1.5 cm and may express salivary secretions AKA : paramedian lip pits, congenital fistulas of the lower lip
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which syndrome associates cleft lip/palate with lip pits ? {{c1::Vand der Woude Syndrome}}
fields.Extra: = sinus tracts that may extend to a depth of 1.5 cm and may express salivary secretions AKA : paramedian lip pits, congenital fistulas of the lower lip
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Ectopic sebaceous glands that occur most commonly on the buccal or labial mucosa are termed {{c1::fordyce granules}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The yellow or white papules on buccal or labial mucosa, called fordyce granules, are ectopic {{c1::sebaceous glands}}.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The whitish-gray edematous mucosal condition found on buccal mucosa that disappears when the cheek is stretched is termed {{c1::leukoedema}}
fields.Extra: Found in 70-90% of black adults and due to intracellular edema of the spinous layer
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How do you clinically diagnose leukoedema ? {{c1::stretching the cheek causes it to diminish greatly}}
fields.Extra: Found in 70-90% of black adults and due to intracellular edema of the spinous layer
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A mass of thyroid tissue situated at the midline base of the tongue is called a {{c1::lingual thyroid}}
fields.Extra: At the foramen cecum and represents 90% of ectopic thyroids
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Lingual thyroids and thyroglossal duct cysts are both found down the embryonic path of {{c1::thyroid descent}}
fields.Extra: Lingual thyroids represent 90% of ectopic thyroids
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Midline neck swelling due to remnants of the thyroglossal duct epithelium is termed {{c1::thyroglossal duct cyst}}
fields.Extra: In most cases the thyroglossal duct epithelium atrophies and is obliterated
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Where is a thyroglossal duct cyst found ? {{c1::Midline of the neck (60-80% adjacent to the hyoid bone)}}
fields.Extra: In most cases the thyroglossal duct epithelium atrophies and is obliterated - - Present as soft, mobile, painless mass unless complicated by infection
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What two synonyms exist for geographic tongue ? {{c1::benign migratory glossitis}} and {{c1::erythema migrans}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Geographic tongue is described as {{c1::white::color}} annular lesions surrounding central {{c1::red::color}} islands that migrate over time.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What symptom can occasionally acompany geographic tongue ? {{c1::burning}}
fields.Extra: No curative treatment, only symptomatic.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When folds and furrows are found on the dorsum of the tongue it is identified as {{c1::fissured tongue}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which syndrome associates fissured tongue , granulomatous cheilitis , and facial paralysis ? {{c1::Melkerson-Rosenthal Syndrome}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Melkerson-Rosenthal Syndrome associates what 3 orofacial signs ? - {{c1::Fissured tongue}} - {{c2::Granulomatous cheilitis (enlargement of the lips)}} - {{c3::Facial paralysis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The family of tumors composed of blood or lymph vessels are termed {{c1::angiomas}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Red papules that can be found on the skin that are due to the benign proliferation of capillaries termed {{c1::cherry angiomas}}
fields.Extra: - Red papules due to the benign proliferation of capillaries - Occurence increases with age
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A congenital focal proliferation of capillaries that presents as red plaques and may disappear is termed a {{c1::hemangioma}}
fields.Extra: 2 types : - rapidly involuting congenital hemangioma (disappears in 9-14 months) - noninvoluting congenital hemangioma (grows proportionately to child)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Are most hemangiomas persistent or do they disappear ? {{c1::Most disappear after ~12 months}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Congenital focal proliferation of lymph vessels are termed {{c1::lymphangiomas}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A lymphangioma that occurs in the neck is termed a {{c1::cystic hygroma}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which syndrome associates angiomas of the leptomeninges and on the skin along the distribution of the trigeminal nerve ? {{c1::Sturge-Weber Syndrome}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The dermal capillary vascular malformations found in Sturge-Weber Syndrome are known as {{c1::port wine stains}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The product of excessive cortical bone growth is known as either {{c1::exostoses}} or {{c1::tori}}.
fields.Extra: tori mandibularis is most common on the lingual aspect of the mandible
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Exostoses or tori are due to the excesive growth of what type of bone ? {{c1::cortical bone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A doughy mass containing structures like hair and sebaceous glands that can present both on the floor of the mouth or on the neck is termed a {{c1::dermoid cyst}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A dermoid cyst has what type of consistency ? {{c1::doughy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The insertion of which muscle determines whether a dermoid cyst will present on the floor of the mouth or on the neck ? {{c1::Mylohyoid muscle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What developmental cyst develops from the remnants of the pharyngeal arches ? {{c1::Branchial cleft cysts}}
fields.Extra: Soft, fluctuant mass that can range from 1 to 10 cm in diameter = cervical lymphoepithelial cyst
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Are branchial cleft cysts located along the midline or the lateral parts of the neck ? {{c1::Lateral parts}}
fields.Extra: Soft, fluctuant mass that can range from 1 to 10 cm in diameter = cervical lymphoepithelial cyst
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The intraoral equivalent of the branchial cleft cyst is called an {{c1::oral lymphoepithelial cyst}}
fields.Extra: BCC = cervical lymphoepithelial cyst
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Oral lymphoepithelial cysts are found in lymphoid tissue found principally where ? {{c1::Waldeyer's Ring}}
fields.Extra: = Palatine tonsil, lingual tonsils, pharyngeal adenoids
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What intraoral developmental cyst found in lymphoid tissue presents as a small firm white/yellow nodule ? {{c1::Oral lymphoepithelial cyst}}
fields.Extra: = Palatine tonsil, lingual tonsils, pharyngeal adenoids
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What developmental condition presents as a radiolucency due to a lingual concavity of the mandible ? {{c1::Stafne bone defect}}
fields.Extra: Not usually present from birth (most cases found in middle-aged or older adults)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does a Stafne bone defect present above or below the mandibular canal ? {{c1::Below}}
fields.Extra: Does not appear to be present from birth (most cases found in middle-aged or older adults) Image source : Aparicio, CC BY-SA 2.5
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which developmental condition presents as a heart-shaped radiolucency between the maxillary central incisors ? {{c1::Nasopalatine duct cyst}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Nasopalatine duct cysts typically are described as having what shape ? {{c1::heart}}
fields.Extra: Swelling of the anterior palate, drainage, sometimes pain = incisive canal cyst
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Nasopalatine duct cysts are due to remnants of what structures that normally disappear during development ? {{c1::Nasopalatine duct}}
fields.Extra: Swelling of the anterior palate, drainage, sometimes pain = incisive canal cyst
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the clinical description of a radiolucency between the maxillary canine and lateral incisor ? {{c1::Globulomaxillary lesion}}
fields.Extra: NOT A DIAGNOSIS Most are consistent with periapical cysts, OKC, lateral periodontal cysts
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A globulomaxillary lesion occurs where ? {{c1::Between maxillary canine and lateral incisor}}
fields.Extra: NOT A DIAGNOSIS Most are consistent with periapical cysts, OKC, lateral periodontal cysts
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is a traumatic bone cyst falsely named ? {{c1::Not epithelium lined}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are synonyms for a traumatic bone cyst ? {{c1::Simple bone cyst}} and {{c1::Idiopathic bone cavity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Traumatic bone cysts are described as large radiolucencies that are {{c1::scalloped::shape}} around roots.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How do you diagnose a traumatic bone cyst ? {{c1::Aspiration reveals only blood}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A white line on the buccal mucosa due to chronic friction of the mucosa is called {{c1::linea alba}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A lesion that completely breaks through epithelium is termed an {{c1::ulcer}}
fields.Extra: Erosion remains in the mucosal lyer Ulccer extends to the submucosal layer
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A lesion that incompletely breaks through epithelium is termed an {{c1::erosion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What common substances can cause intraoral chemical burns ? - {{c1::Aspirin}} - {{c1::Hydrogen peroxide}} - {{c1::Silver nitrate}} - {{c1::Phenol}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Red dots on a white background due to tobacco smoking is termed {{c1::nicotine stomatitis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The red dots seen in nicotine stomatitis are due to what ? {{c1::inflamed salivary duct openings}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When is nicotine stomatitis considered premalignant ? {{c1::reverse smoking}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The traumatic implantation of amalgam particles into mucosa is called {{c1::amalgam tattoo}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Brown, diffuse melanotic macules due to tobacco stimulation of melanocytes is called {{c1::smoking-associated melanosis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What cells are stimulated by tobacco in smoking-associated melanosis ? {{c1::melanocytes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is smoking-associated melanosis typically seen in the anterior gingiva ? {{c1::path of smoke, and smokeless tobacco}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A benign brown/black/blue hyperpigmentation in the mucous membrane is called an {{c1::oral melanotic macule}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which syndrome associates melanotic macules and intestinal polyposis ? {{c1::Peutz-jeghers Syndrome}}
fields.Extra: 10-18x risk for certain cancers
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Peutz-Jeghers syndrome characterized by {{c1::melanotic macules (all over)}} and {{c2::intestinal polyposis}}
fields.Extra: 10-18x greater than normal cancer risk
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Elongated filiform papillae that may be present as a brown or black tongue is called {{c1::hairy tongue}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Hairy tongue is due to elongated {{c1::filiform}} papillae on the dorsum of the tongue
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Dentifrice-associated sloughing is due to what component of toothpaste ? {{c1::Sodium lauryl sulfate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How do you differentiate submucosal hemmorhage from telangiectasias or angiomas ? {{c1::they do not blanch on vitropression (diascopy)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Extravascular red lesions that do not blanch are grouped under the category of {{c1::submucosal hemmorrhage}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A 1-2mm, pin-point hemmorhage, on skin/oral mucous membrane is termed {{c1::petechiae}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A 3-10 mm hemmorhage, on skin/oral mucous membrane is termed {{c1::purpura}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A > 10 mm hemmorhage, on skin/oral mucous membrane is termed {{c1::ecchymosis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A solid mass of clotted blood within tissue that may be caused by trauma is called a {{c1::hematoma}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Primary HSV-1 infection in children most often presents as {{c1::acute herpetic gingivostomatitis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: After primary infection, HSV-1 remains latent predominantly in what ganglion ? {{c1::trigeminal ganglion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Recurrent HSV-1 infection occurs predominantly on which type of epithelium ? {{c1::Keratinized (hard palate, attached gingiva)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Recurrent HSV-1 infection that occurs on the vermillion border of the lips is called {{c1::herpes labialis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Recurrent HSV-1 infection that occurs on a finger is termed {{c1::herpetic whitlow}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment for recurrent HSV-1 infection and when ? {{c1::Acyclovir in prodromal period}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What 3 factors may trigger an HSV-1 reccurent infection ? - {{c1::Stress}} - {{c1::Immunosuppression}} - {{c1::Sunlight}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Chickenpox is the common term to denote the primary infection of which virus ? {{c1::Varicella Zoster Virus (VZV)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In which ganglion does VZV remain latent ? {{c1::Trigeminal ganglion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Recurrent infection by the Varicella Zoster Virus (VZV) is called {{c1::herpes zoster OR shingles}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which syndrome results from the reactivation of Varicella Zoster Virus in the geniculate ganglion ? {{c1::Ramsay Hunt Syndrome}}
fields.Extra: Source: Worme M, CC BY 2.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Ramsay Hunt syndrome results from the reactivation of the Varicella Zoster Virus in which ganglion ? {{c1::Geniculate ganglion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Ramsay Hunt syndrome may manifest with what clinical signs in addition to zoster reaction ? - {{c1::Facial paralysis}} - {{c2::Vertigo}} - {{c3::Deafness}}
fields.Extra: Source: Worme M, CC BY 2.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Herpes zoster reactivation manifests unilaterally or bilaterally ? {{c1::Unilaterally along one or more dermatomes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The facial paralysis, vertigo, and deafness seen in Ramsay Hunt Syndrome is due to reactivation of the VZV which affects which cranial nerves ? {{c1::VII, VIII}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which virus is responsible for hand-foot-and-mouth disease and herpangina ? {{c1::Coxsackie virus}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the primary lesions in hand-foot-and-mouth disease ? {{c1::vesicles}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which clinical pattern of the coxsackie virus presents as numerous ulcerations of the posterior oral cavity ? {{c1::Herpangina}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which part of the mouth do the clinical manifestations of Herpangina present ? {{c1::posterior oral cavity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which viral infection presents as buccal mucosa dot ulcers called Koplik's spots ? {{c1::Measles (Rubeola)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The intraoral blue-white dot ulcers that precede skin rash in Measles are called {{c1::Koplik's spots}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Squamous papillomas, verruca vulgaris, Heck's Disase and condyloma acuminatum are all due to what type of virus ? {{c1::Human papilloma Virus }}
fields.Extra: Lots of different types responsible for lots of different lesions.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Benign epithelial pedunculated or sessile proliferations exhibiting surface projections associated with HPV are termed {{c1::squamous papillomas}}
fields.Extra: HPV 6, 11
