fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Dentures that are retained by implants or tooth roots are called {{c1::overdentures}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Overdentures in the mandible usually involve {{c1::2}} implants whereas in the maxilla they usually require {{c1::4}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of implant retention system is more economical ? {{c1::cement-retained::cemented/screw}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of implant retention system allows more angle correction ? {{c1::cement-retained::cemented/screw}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of implant retention system allows for easier crown removal and maintenance ? {{c1::screw-retained::cemented/screw}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What periodontal condition may arise in cement-retained implants that may compromise implant prognosis ? {{c1::Peri-implantitis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the material of choice for taking diagnostic impressions ? {{c1::Alginate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Alginate impression material is composed of {{c1::sodium}} or {{c1::potassium}} salts of {{c2::alginic acid}} which react chemically with {{c3::calcium sulfate}} to produce calcium alginate
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What substance is added to alginate to increase strength ? {{c1::Diatomaceous earth}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which component in alginate controls the setting rate ? {{c1::Trisodium phosphate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the usual setting time for alginate impressions ? {{c1::3-4 minutes (from mixing)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An alginate impression should be poured within {{c1::15 minutes}} of taking impression
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long does it take for a cast to set after being poured ? {{c1::30-60 minutes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 2 main maxillo-mandibular relations ? {{c1::Centric relation and Maximal intercuspation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Centric relation is the position in which {{c1::condyles}} articulate with the thinnest avascular portion of their respective discs in the most {{c2::anterior-superior}} position against the articular eminences
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which maxillo-mandibular relationship is determined by the condyles ? {{c1::Centric relation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Maximum intercuspation is the position in which the {{c1::greatest}} number of teeth interdigitate
fields.Extra: It should be unique, stable, reproducible,
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Maximum intercuspation and centric relation only coincide in {{c1::10}}% of the population
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For small fixed prosthetics where MI is reproducible, should casts be mounted in CR or MI ? {{c1::MI}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For extensive rehabilitation and dentures, should casts be mounted in CR or MI ? {{c1::CR}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The most stable and reproducible maxillo-mandibular relation is {{c1::centric relation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is one of the most accurate methods to obtain accurate CR interocclusal records ? {{c1::Bimanual manipulation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In what position should the patient be in during bimanual manipulation to find CR ? {{c1::patient should be lying back}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A facebow record's objective is to duplicate on the articulator the relationship of the {{c1::maxillary arch}} to the skull
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A facebow record's objective is to duplicate on the articulator the relationship of the {{c1::mandible}} to the rotational axis of the TMJs
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of facebow orients the maxillary cast to the skull via the external auditory meatus ? {{c1::arbitrary facebow}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of facebow is placed on the hinge axis of the mandible ? {{c1::kinematic facebow}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The tool that allows us to relate the maxillary and mandibular casts is called an {{c1::articulator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In a nonadjustable articulator the distance between hinge and teeth is significantly {{c1::shorter}} than in the patient
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Due to the distance between hinge and teeth being shorter than in the patient, a nonadjustable articulator may result in {{c1::premature contacts}} and incorrect {{c2::ridge and groove}} direction of restorations
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A semiadjustable articulator allows you to set the {{c1::Bennet angle}} and the {{c2::horizontal condylar inclination}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In most cases the Bennet angle should be adjusted to {{c1::15}} degrees and the HCI should be adjusted to {{c1::30}} degrees
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In what type of semiadjustable articulator are the condyles a part of the lower member and the fossa a part of the upper member ? {{c1::Arcon}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In what type of semiadjustable articulator are the upper and lower members rigidly attached ? {{c1::Nonarcon}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of semiadjustable articulator is more anatomically accurate ? {{c1::Arcon }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In a fully adjustable articulator a {{c1::pantograph}} is used to follow the patient's {{c2::border movements}}.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Casts poured from alginate are more accurately mounted with what type of records ? {{c1::wax}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Casts poured from elastomeric materials are more accurately mounted with what type of records ? {{c1::elastomeric materials or ZOE paste}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which types of guidance are solicited in protrusive movements ? - {{c1::Condylar guidance}} - {{c1::Incisal guidance}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Condylar guidance is determined by the slope of the {{c1::articular eminence}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which articulator element represents the condylar guidance on an articulator ? {{c1::Horizontal Condylar Inclination}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Condylar guidance is a(n) {{c1::posterior::anterior/posterior}} determinant of occlusion
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Incisal guidance is a(n) {{c1::anterior::anterior/posterior}} determinant of occlusion
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which part of the articulator represents the incisal guidance ? {{c1::Pin and guide table}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Incisal guidance is determined by the {{c1::incisal}} edges of the mandibular incisors on the {{c1::palatal}} slopes of the maxillary incisors
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When in lateral movements all posterior teeth are immediately discluded and contact occurs only on canines, this is referred to as {{c1::canine guidance}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Anterior guidance refers to both {{c1::incisal}} and {{c1::canine}} guidance
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When in lateral movements more than one tooth contact before posterior disclusion this is referred to as {{c1::group function}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::Anterior}} guidance must be preserved when restorative procedures change the surfaces of any "guiding" teeth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Mutual protection is the idea that front teeth protect the back teeth during {{c1::protrusive}} and {{c1::lateral}} movements
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Mutual protection is the idea that back teeth protect the front teeth from {{c1::occlusal forces}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 4 phases of hemostasis ? 1. {{c1::Vascular phase}} 2. {{c1::Platelet phase}} 3. {{c1::Coagulation phase}} 4. {{c1::Fibrinolytic phase}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are 3 examples of syndromes that affect the vascular phase of hemostasis ? - {{c1::Marfan syndrome}} - {{c1::Ehlers-Danlos syndrome}} - {{c1::Osler-Weber-Rendu syndrome (HHT)}}
fields.Extra: These patients rarely have a bleeding problem after oral surgery.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of tissue do Marfan syndrome and Ehlers-Danlos syndrome affect that impacts the vascular phase of hemostasis ? {{c1::connective tissue}}
fields.Extra: These patients rarely have a bleeding problem after oral surgery.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What precautions should be taken in patients with vascular wall defects ? - {{c1::Consult with hematologist}} - {{c1::Minimally invasive dentistry}} - {{c1::Local hemostatic measures}} - {{c1::Avoid NSAIDs}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Endothelial damage causes the exposure of {{c1:: Von Willibrand Factor (vWF)}} which allows adhesion of platelets via {{c2:: GPIb ::which protein?}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: After binding vWF, platelets are activated and change {{c2::shape}} while releasing amplification signals such as {{c1:: TXA2 }} and {{c1:: ADP }} which recruits more platelets
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During hemostasis, as more platelets are activated, they begin to aggregate and circulating {{c1:: fibrinogen }} connects platelets by their respective {{c2:: GP IIb/IIIa }} complexes
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 phases of the platelet pathway or primary hemostasis ? 1. {{c1::Adhesion}} 2. {{c1::Activation}} 3. {{c1::Aggregation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does Von Willebrand disease impact hemostasis ? {{c1::impairs platelet adhesion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which disease results in a deficiency of Von Willebrand factor ? {{c1::Von Willebrand disease}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common congenital bleeding disorder ? {{c1::Von Willebrand disease}}
fields.Extra: - about 1% of the population
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A low platelet count is termed {{c1::thrombocytopenia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the normal lab values of platelets ? {{c1::150,000-450,000 platelets/microL}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Increased clinical bleeding can be noted starting at what platelet count ? {{c1::50,000 platelets/microL}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Spontaneous bleeding and bruising can be noted below what platelet count ? {{c1::20,000 platelets/microL}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does Aspirin affect primary hemostasis ? {{c1::Prevents synthesis of Thromboxane A2 which decreases platelet activation and aggregation }}
fields.Extra: Thromboxane A2 is needed to recruit platelets to the endothelial lesion
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Clopidogrel competes with {{c1:: ADP }} which decreases {{c2:: platelet activation }}
fields.Extra: ADP is needed to recruit platelets to the endothelial lesion
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Abciximab bonds to the {{c1:: GP IIb/IIIa complex }} on platelets and prevents {{c2:: aggregation }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What screening test is recommended for dentists to obtain a quantitative assessment of platelets ? {{c1::platelet count}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What 2 screening tests are recommended for dentists to obtain a qualitative assessment of platelets ? {{c1::Peripheral blood smear}} {{c1::Platelet aggregation test}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What precautions should be taken in a patient with a platelet disorder ? - {{c1::Consult hematologist}} - {{c1::Minimally invasive dentistry}} - {{c1::Local hemostatic measures}} - {{c1::Avoid NSAIDs}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Most coagulation factors are synthesized in which organ ? {{c1::liver}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Coagulation factor I is also called {{c1::fibrinogen}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Fibrinogen is activated and turned into soluble fibrin by {{c1::thrombin (IIa)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Soluble fibrin is turned into insoluble fibrin by {{c1::coagulation factor XIIIa}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the other name for coagulation factor III ? {{c1::Tissue factor}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Thrombin, or factor II is responsible for activating what other coagulation factors ? {{c1::V, VII, VIII, XI, XIII}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Von Willebrand disease leads to a reduction in which coagulation factor ? {{c1::VIII}}
fields.Extra: This disease affects primary hemostasis AND coagulation
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Hemophilia A is due to a deficiency of factor {{c1::VIII}} and carries an 80% chance of hematoma following IANB
fields.Extra: Unless factor VIII transfused pre-operatively
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Hemophilia B is due to a deficiency of factor {{c1::IX}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Hemophilia C is due to a deficiency of factor {{c1::XI}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: All hemophilia affect the intrinsic or extrinsic pathway ? {{c1::Intrinsic pathway}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which vitamin is required for the synthesis of coagulation factors II, VII, IX, and X ? {{c1::Vit K}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which coagulation factors depend on the presence of vitamin K ? {{c1::II, VII, IX, X}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How do vitamin K antagonists such as Warfarin and Coumadin function ? {{c1::blocks reduction recycling of vit K}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Heparin functions by binding and activating {{c1:: antithrombin }} which inactivates {{c2::thrombin (IIa)}} and {{c2::factor Xa}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Apixaban (Eliquis) functions by inhibiting factor {{c1::Xa}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Dabigatran (Pradaxa) functions by binding and directly inhibiting {{c1::thrombin (factor IIa)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The activated partial thromboplastin time (aPTT) is used to measure the intrinsic, extrinsic, or common coagulation pathway ? {{c1::Intrinsic and common pathway}}
fields.Extra: Number of seconds it takes for a clot to form given exposure to certain reagents Normal = 25-35 seconds
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which coagulation test is used to assess the intrinsic and common pathways ? {{c1::aPTT}}
fields.Extra: Number of seconds it takes for a clot to form given exposure to certain reagents Normal = 25-35 seconds
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The prothrombin time (PT/INR) is used to assess the intrinsic, extrinsic, or common pathway ? {{c1::Extrinsic}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the goal INR for a patient on vitamin K antagonist anticoagulant medication ? {{c1::< 3}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which drugs/families should be avoided in patients on VKA medications because they potentiate the anticoagulant effect ? {{c1::NSAIDs}} {{c1::Metronidazole}} {{c1::Erythromycin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which drugs/families should be avoided in patients taking VKA medications because of their antagonistic effects ? (Increase clotting) {{c1::Barbiturates}} {{c1::Steroids}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What local hemostatic measures should be taken for patients taking vitamin K antagonist drugs ? - {{c1::Compressive packing}} - {{c1::Sutures}} - {{c1::Topical thrombin}} - {{c1::4.8% tranexamic acid mouthwash}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What precautions should be taken in a patient with INR between 3-3.5 undergoing simple surgery ? {{c1::Local hemostatic measures}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What precautions should be taken in a patient with INR between 3-3.5 undergoing complex surgery ? {{c1::Defer treatment and refer to medical doctor}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What precautions should be taken in a patient with INR > 3.5 ? {{c1::Defer treatment and refer to medical doctor}}
fields.Extra: Takes 3-5 days for INR reduction to occur after dosage change
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long after a VKA dosage change does it take for INR to change ? {{c1::3-5 days}}
fields.Extra: INR test should be performed < 48h prior to surgery
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How close to surgery should an INR test be done ? {{c1::< 48h}}
fields.Extra: INR test should be performed < 48h prior to surgery
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some classic oral manifestations of bleeding disorders ? - {{c1::Spontaneous gingival bleeding}} - {{c1::Submucosal hemmorhage}} - {{c1::Hemarthrosis of TMJ (Hemophilia ++)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [1] represent ? {{c1::Labial frenum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [2] represent ? {{c1::Labial vestibule}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [3] represent ? {{c1::Buccal frenum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [4] represent ? {{c1::Buccal vestibule}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [6] represent ? {{c1::Alveolar ridge}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [7] represent ? {{c1::Maxillary tuberosity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [8] represent ? {{c1::Hamular notch}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [9] represent ? {{c1::Posterior palatal seal area}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [10] represent ? {{c1::Fovea palatini}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [11] represent ? {{c1::Median palatine raphe}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [12] represent ? {{c1::Incisive papilla}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [13] represent ? {{c1::Palatine ruggae}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The vibrating line runs from {{c1::hamular notch}} to {{c1::hamular notch}}, about 2mm away from the {{c2::fovea palatini}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How do you locate the posterior vibrating line ? {{c1:: Patient says "aah"}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The distal end of a complete maxillary denture should extend to the {{c1::posterior vibrating line}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How can you locate the anterior vibrating line (butterfly line) ? {{c1::Valsalva maneuver}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [5] represent ? {{c1::Coronoid notch}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What anatomical element connects the buccinator to the superior pharyngeal constrictor ? {{c1::Pterygomandibular raphe}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [1] represent ? {{c1::Labial frenum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What muscles are attached to the maxillary labial frenum ? {{c1::None!}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What muscles are attached to the maxillary buccal frenum ? {{c1::Orbicularis oris and the buccinator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What muscles are attached to the mandibular buccal frenum ? {{c1::Orbicularis oris and the buccinators}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What muscles are attached to the mandibular labial frenum ? {{c1::Orbicularis oris}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What muscles are attached to the mandibular lingual frenum ? {{c1::genioglossus}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [3] represent ? {{c1::Buccal frenum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [12] represent ? {{c1::Lingual frenum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [2] represent ? {{c1::Labial vestibule}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What muscle is inserted on the alveolar ridge and forms the labial vestibule of the mandible ? {{c1::mentalis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What muscle is inserted on the alveolar ridge and forms the buccal vestibule of the mandible ? {{c1::buccinator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [4] represent ? {{c1::Buccal vestibule}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [5] represent ? {{c1::Alveolar ridge}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [7] represent ? {{c1::Retromolar pad}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What anatomical element represents the distal extension of a mandibular complete denture ? {{c1::Retromolar pad}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The retromolar pad contains attachments from which muscles/structures ? - {{c1::Temporalis}} - {{c1::Buccinator}} - {{c1::Superior pharyngeal constrictor}} - {{c1::Pterygomandibular raphe}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The most distobuccal area on a mandibular edentulous impression is the {{c1::masseteric notch}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The most distobuccal area on a maxillary edentulous impression is the {{c1::coronoid notch}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Overextension at the masseteric notch risks dislodging the denture due to the action of which muscle ? {{c1::Masseter}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During border molding, what movement allows proper extension of the masseteric notch ? {{c1::Closing against resistance}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [10] represent ? {{c1::Alveololingual sulcus}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [8] represent ? {{c1::Masseteric notch (Pterygomandibular notch)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The flange in the anterior part of the mandibular alveololingual sulcus must be shorter because of the presence of what anatomical element ? {{c1::sublingual gland}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The flange in the middle part of the alveololingual sulcus is deflected {{c1::medially}} due to the prominence of the {{c2::mylohyoid ridge}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why can the lingual flange be longer in the posterior region of the mandible than in the middle region ? {{c1::mylohyoid fibers are directed more vertically than in the middle region}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In the posterior region of the alveololingual sulcus the denture flange is deflected {{c1::laterally}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Denture extension in the posterior alveololingual sulcus is limited by which muscles ? {{c1::Palatoglossus}} {{c1::Superior pharyngeal constrictor}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [6] represent ? {{c1::Buccal shelf}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: While the alveolar ridge resorbs in an edentulous patient, why doesn't the buccal shelf resorb as well ? {{c1::It's maintained by the muscle insertions (laterally and posteriorly)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Bisphosphonates prevent bone resorption by inducing {{c1::osteoclasts}} to undergo {{c2::apoptosis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do bisphosphonates increase or decrease bone density ? {{c1::Increase}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do bisphosphonates speed up or slow down tooth movement ? {{c1::slow down}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do bisphosphonates improve or impair bone healing ? {{c1::impair}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are 4 major indications for bisphosphonates use ? - {{c1::Osteoporosis}} - {{c1::Paget's disease}} - {{c1::Multiple myeloma}} - {{c1::Metastatic bone lesions of various cancers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What suffix denotes bisphosphonate drugs ? {{c1::-dronate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the approximate bioavailibility of oral bisphophonates ? {{c1::2% (compared to 100% IV)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is the bioavailability of oral bisphosphonates so much lower than IV ? {{c1::high binding affinity to metals in the intestinal tract --> low absorption}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Bisphosphonates have a high binding affinity to which elements ? - {{c1::Calcium}} - {{c1::Iron}} - {{c1::Magnesium}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do bisphosphonates concentrate more in trabecular or cortical bone ? {{c1::Trabecular bone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The half life of bisphosphonates can vary from {{c1::10 hours}} for ibandronate to {{c2::10 years}} for alendronate
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Bisphosphonate drugs are synthetic analogues of {{c1::inorganic pyrophosphate}} which has a high affinity for {{c2::calcium}} and inhibits mineralization
fields.Extra: Pyrophosphate is a natural circulating inhibtor of mineralization in the blood and urine which can't get inside the bones because the lining cells destroy it with alkaline phosphatase. Bisphophonates substitute a carbon for the oxygen and can have a high affinity for bone
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Bisphosphonates reduce both osteoclast {{c2::function}} and {{c1::number}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Increasing potency of bisphosphonates is due to {{c1::nitrogen}} side groups that inhibit farnesyl pyrophosphate synthase
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Prolonged high-dose use of bisphosphonates can lead to impaired bone healing due to impaired {{c1::angiogenesis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the cause of "bone death" in MRONJ ? {{c1::lack of blood supply}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Other than bisphosphonates , what other drugs can lead to MRONJ ? - {{c1::anti-VEGF drugs (bevacizumab)}} - {{c1::anti-RANKL drugs (denosumab)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is MRONJ more common in the maxilla or the mandible ? {{c1::mandible}}
fields.Extra: Less vascular than the maxilla
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is MRONJ more common in the posterior or anterior oral cavity ? {{c1::posterior}}
fields.Extra: More occlusal forces, more micro-fractures
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Concomitant use of {{c1:: estrogen }} or {{c2:: glucocorticoid }} drugs is considered a risk factor for MRONJ
fields.Extra: Risk factors : - Estrogen, glucocorticoids - > 65 yo - Nitrogen side-group drugs
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What age is considered a risk factor for MRONJ ? {{c1::> 65 years old}}
fields.Extra: Risk factors : - Estrogen, glucocorticoids - > 65 yo - Nitrogen side-group drugs
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Bisphophonate drugs with or without nitrogen side groups are more associated with MRONJ ? {{c1::With nitrogen side group}}
fields.Extra: Risk factors : - Estrogen, glucocorticoids - > 65 yo - Nitrogen side-group drugs
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What 3 conditions must be met for an MRONJ diagnosis ? - {{c1::Current or previous treatment with causative medication}} - {{c2::Bone exposure in the maxillofacial region that persists for more than 8 weeks}} - {{c3::No history of radiation therapy to the jaws}}
fields.Extra: eg. extraction, implant pacement, periodontal surgery - bone loses overlying blood supply (periosteum) and has insufficient perfusion to heal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long must bone be exposed to the oral cavity before it can be considered MRONJ ? {{c1::8 weeks}}
fields.Extra: eg. extraction, implant pacement, periodontal surgery - bone loses overlying blood supply (periosteum) and has insufficient perfusion to heal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For IV bisphophonates the average MRONJ case occurs how long after administration ? {{c1::1 year}}
fields.Extra: eg. extraction, implant pacement, periodontal surgery - bone loses overlying blood supply (periosteum) and has insufficient perfusion to heal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For oral bisphophonates the average MRONJ case occurs how long after administration ? {{c1::3 years}}
fields.Extra: eg. extraction, implant pacement, periodontal surgery - bone loses overlying blood supply (periosteum) and has insufficient perfusion to heal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For IV bisphophonates what is the prevalence of spontaneous MRONJ ? {{c1::1%}}
fields.Extra: eg. extraction, implant pacement, periodontal surgery - bone loses overlying blood supply (periosteum) and has insufficient perfusion to heal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For IV bisphophonates what is the prevalence of MRONJ after extraction ? {{c1::10%}}
fields.Extra: eg. extraction, implant pacement, periodontal surgery - bone loses overlying blood supply (periosteum) and has insufficient perfusion to heal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For oral bisphophonates what is the prevalence of MRONJ after extraction ? {{c1::0.5%}}
fields.Extra: eg. extraction, implant pacement, periodontal surgery - bone loses overlying blood supply (periosteum) and has insufficient perfusion to heal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For oral bisphophonates what is the prevalence of spontaneous MRONJ ? {{c1::0.1%}}
fields.Extra: eg. extraction, implant pacement, periodontal surgery - bone loses overlying blood supply (periosteum) and has insufficient perfusion to heal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In patients at risk of MRONJ is root canal therapy preferred to extraction ? {{c1::Yes!}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For patients at risk of MRONJ is antibiotic coverage during and after surgery warranted ? {{c1::Yes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During an extraction, why would alveolectomy be especially warranted in patients at risk of MRONJ ? {{c1::Eliminate sharp edges that can perforate mucosa and are poorly vascularized}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For active MRONJ cases, what course of action ? - {{c1::Chlorhexidine 0,12% rinse}} - {{c1::Surgical debridement (oral >> IV)}} - {{c1::Hyperbaric oxygen}} - {{c1::Aggressive systemic antibiotics}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment is possible when a frenum is attached too high on the alveolar ridge ? {{c1::frenectomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Most common frenectomies to least common : {{c1::labial}} > {{c1::buccal}} > {{c1::lingual}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the objective of a free gingival graft ? {{c1::Widen band of keratinized tissue around a tooth/implant}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of surgery is indicated when a widening of keratinized tissue is needed ? {{c1::Free gingival graft}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Hypermobile ridges are most common in what part of the mouth ? {{c1::Anterior maxilla}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For minor cases of hypermobile ridge what treatment can reduce inflammation ? {{c1::Tissue conditioning}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For major cases of hypermobile ridge what treatment can be indicated ? {{c1::electrosurgery/laser surgery to eliminate excess tissue}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What modifications need to be done when taking an impression of a hypermobile ridge ? - {{c1::Large relief}} - {{c1::Perforate the tray}}
fields.Extra: Avoids displacing the ridge during impression taking
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Epulis fissuratum is often due to an {{c1::overextended}} flange
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the first line treatment of epulis fissuratum ? {{c1::Tissue conditioner and adjusting the denture flange}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What descriptive term is used to denote large tuberosities that limit interarch space needed for denture construction ? {{c1::Fibrous (pendulous) tuberosity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Fibrous tuberosity (pendulous tuberosity) is common when {{c1::large tuberosities}} touch the retromolar pads.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How do you treat fibrous (pendulous) tuberosity ? {{c1::Surgical excision of fibrous tissue and/or bone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the risk factors for papillary hyperplasia ? {{c1::Local irritation (candidiasis)}} {{c1::Ill-fiting denture}} {{c1::Poor oral hygiene}} {{c1::Leaving dentures in all the time}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the main cause of papillary hyperplasia ? {{c1::Candidiasis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment for papillary hyperplasia ? {{c1::Oral hygiene instruction}} {{c1::Leave dentures out at night}} {{c1::1% bleach soak}} {{c1::Tissue conditioner}} {{c1::Brush the tissue}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The pattern of bone resorption in the anterior edentulous maxilla when opposing mandibular anterior teeth are present is called {{c1::combination syndrome}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the clinical manifestations of combination syndrome ? - {{c1::Papillary hyperplasia}} - {{c2::Overgrowth of tuberosities}} - {{c3::Extrusion of anterior lower teeth}} - {{c4::Bone resorption anterior maxilla and posterior mandible}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Residual root tips may be left alone under what conditions ? {{c1::Intact lamina dura}} {{c1::No radiolucency}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the "classic" presentation of a denture wearer with Paget's disease ? {{c1::Dentures and hats don't fit as bone expands and remodels}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The surgical reshaping of alveolar bone is called {{c1::alveoloplasty}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When would tori removal be necessary when creating a denture ? {{c1::Creates undercuts}} {{c1::Interferes with posterior palatal seal}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the treatment objective of vestibuloplasty ? {{c1::Increase the height of the alveolar process to increase denture base area}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is it easier to restore horizontal ridge width or vertical bone height ? {{c1::horizontal ridge width}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The distance between the nose and chin at rest is termed the {{c1::Vertical dimension of rest (VDR)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The state of equilibrium between elevator and depressor muscles used to determine the VDR is termed {{c1::physiologic rest position}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The distance between nose and chin when in maximal intercuspation is called the {{c1::vertical dimension of occlusion (VDO)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The difference between VDO and VDR is called the {{c1::interocclusal space}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Ideal interocclusal space is between {{c1::2}} and {{c1::4}} mm
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Clicking of the posterior teeth is a sign of {{c1::excessive::insufficient/excessive}} VDO
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Muscles of mastication fatigue is a sign of {{c1::excessive::insufficient/excessive}} VDO
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Strained appearance of the lips is a sign of {{c1::excessive::insufficient/excessive}} VDO
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An aging appearance of the lower third of the face is a sign of {{c1::insufficient::insufficient/excessive}} VDO
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Diminished occlusal force is a sign of {{c1::insufficient::insufficient/excessive}} VDO
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Angular cheilitis is a sign of {{c1::insufficient::insufficient/excessive}} VDO
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For an edentulous patient, the {{c1::centric relation}} record provides the ability to increase or decrease the VDO more accurately by establishing a radius of the mandible's arc of closure
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A {{c1::protrusive}} record registers the anterior-inferior condyle path in the translation movement of the condyles
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The space created between opposing occlusal surfaces in protrusion due to condyle movement along the articular eminence is called {{c1::Christensen's phenomenon}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The imaginary line from the ala of the nose to the tragus of the ear is called {{c1::Camper's line}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Camper's line extends from the {{c1::ala of the nose}} to the {{c1::tragus of the ear}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The imaginary line between the pupils of the eyes is called the {{c1::interpupillary line}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A maxillary occlusal wax rim should be parrallel to which lines ? {{c1::Camper's line}} {{c1::Interpupillary line}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What tool is used to assess the parallelism of an occlusal rim with Camper's line and the interpupillary line ? {{c1::Fox plane}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What denture occlusal scheme involves simultaneous anterior and bilateral posterior contacts in centric and eccentric movements ? {{c1::Balanced occlusion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of guidance should be avoided in balanced occlusion ? {{c1::anterior guidance (canine and incisal guidance)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which denture occlusion scheme involves contacts only on the palatal cusps of the maxillary posterior teeth ? {{c1::Lingualized occlusion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The angle obtained after the nonworking side condyle has moved anteriorly and medially relative to the sagittal plane is called the {{c1::Bennett angle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The lateral movement of the mandible toward the working side during lateral excursions is referred to as the {{c1::Bennet shift}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The lateral movement of both condyles toward the working side is referred to as the {{c1::Bennett movement}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does shallow or steep incisal guidance favor disclusion of posterior teeth in protrusive movements ? {{c1::steep}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does shallow or steep canine guidance favor disclusion of posterior teeth in lateral movements ? {{c1::steep}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does shallow or steep horizontal condylar inclination favor disclusion of posterior teeth in protrusive movements ? {{c1::steep}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does more or less Bennett movement favor disclusion of posterior teeth in lateral movements ? {{c1::less}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do short or long cusps favor disclusion of posterior teeth in lateral movements ? {{c1::short}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do steep or shallow cusp inclines favor disclusion of posterior teeth in lateral movements ? {{c1::shallow}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The anteroposterior curve that ensures loading along the long axis of each tooth is referred to as the {{c1::curve of Spee}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The mediolateral curve along posterior cusp tips that ensures loading along the long axis of each tooth is referred to as the {{c1::curve of Wilson}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does having more or less curve of Spee favor posterior disclusion during lateral movements ? {{c1::less}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does having more or less curve of Wilson favor posterior disclusion during lateral movements ? {{c1::less}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Would an occlusal plane permit faster protrusive disclusion if it was more parrallel or perpendicular to the condylar inclination ? {{c1::perpendicular}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the fricative or labiodental sounds ? {{c1::f, v, ph}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Fricative or labiodental sounds are due to the contact between {{c1::maxillary incisors}} and {{c1::the wet/dry line of the lower lip}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Fricative or labiodental sounds help determine the position of what denture teeth ? {{c1::incisal edges of maxillary anteriors}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The "f, ph, v" sounds are all examples of what types of sounds ? {{c1::fricative/labiodental}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some examples of sibilant or linguoalveolar sounds ? {{c1::s, z, sh, ch, j}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Sibilant or linguoalveolar sounds are due to contact between the {{c1::tongue}} and the {{c1::anterior palate or lingual surface of the teeth}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The "s, z, sh, ch, j" sounds are all examples of {{c1::sibilant/linguoalveolar}} sounds
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Sibilant or linguoalveolar sounds help determine what aspects of complete dentures ? {{c1::Vertical length of incisors}} {{c1::Overlap of anterior teeth}} {{c1::Width of arch form}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A whistling sound during wax rim try in is due to what ? {{c1::too narrow arch form}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A lisp where "s" becomes an "sh" sound during wax rim try in is due to what ? {{c1::too wide arch form}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During wax rim try-ins, the interincisal separation during pronunciation of "s" should be about {{c1::1-1.5 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The sound "th" is an example of which type of sound ? {{c1::linguodental}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is an example of a linguodental sound ? {{c1::"th"}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Linguodental sounds help determine the {{c1::anteroposterior}} position of the anterior teeth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: "B, P, M" are examples of which type of sounds ? {{c1::bilabial}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are examples of some bilabial sounds ? {{c1::"B, P, M"}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Bilabial sounds help evaluate what element of complete dentures ? {{c1::lip support}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some examples of guttural sounds ? {{c1::"G, K"}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The sounds produced when saying "G, and K" are examples of what type of sounds ? {{c1::guttural}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease associates what two pathologies ? {{c1::Chronic bronchitis}} {{c1::Emphysema}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common cause of COPD ? {{c1::Cigarette smoking}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the caricature used to denote chronic bronchitis patients ? {{c1::Blue bloater}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the caricature used to denote emphysema patients ? {{c1::Pink puffer}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the diagnostic criteria for chronic bronchitis ? {{c1::Daily productive cough for 3+ months in at least 2 consecutive years}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Chronic bronchitis patients have {{c1::elevated::decreased/elevated}} hemogloblin levels
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Chronic bronchitis is due to inflamed {{c1::bronchioles}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In chronic bronchitis, obstruction is present in inspiration, expiration, or both ? {{c1::both inspiration and expiration}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The general term for pulmonary disorders characterized by chronic airflow limitation from the lungs that is not fully reversible is {{c1::chronic obstructive pulmonary disease}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Chronic inflammation of the {{c2:: bronchioles }} with excessive mucus production and a persistent cough with sputum is called {{c1:: chronic bronchitis }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A permanent enlargement of the air spaces in the lung accompanied by destruction of the alveolar walls is called {{c1::emphysema}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In emphysema (COPD), inflammatory cells release which enzyme responsible for the destruction of the alveolar walls ? {{c1::elastase}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The destruction of alveoli in emphysema (COPD) results in {{c1::enlarged::reduced/enlarged}} air spaces distal to the terminal bronchioles and loss of elastic recoil .
fields.Extra: --> loss of elasticity and larger spaces makes it harder to push out air during expiration
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What instrument is used to measure the volume of air a person can breathe out ? {{c1::Spirometer}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A {{c2:: FEV 1 /FVC }} < {{c1:: 70 }}% is considered a COPD diagnosis
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A {{c1::pulse oximeter}} measures the percentage of {{c2::hemoglobin}} saturated with oxygen
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is a normal pulse oximeter reading ? {{c1::95-100%}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the therapeutic effects of anticholinergic medications used to treat COPD ? {{c1::Prevent bronchoconstriction (M3)}} {{c1::Decrease mucus production}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: β-adrenergic agonists relax smooth muscle of the airways by increasing {{c1::cyclic-AMP}} levels
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Phosphodiesterase inhibitors prevent the inactivation of {{c1::cyclic-AMP}} and cause bronchodilation
fields.Extra: = bronchodilation
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which COPD medication inhibits the inactivation of cyclic-AMP ? {{c1::Phosphodiesterase inhibitors}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What 4 categories of drugs are prescribed for COPD ? {{c1::Anticholinergics::A}} {{c1::Beta-adrenergic agonists::B}} {{c1::Corticosteroids::C}} {{c1::Phosphodiesterase inhibitors::D}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In patients with moderate to severe COPD , what chair positions facilitate breathing ? {{c1::Semisupine or upright}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most effective treatment intervention for COPD patients ? {{c1::Smoking cessation}}
fields.Extra: eg. Nicotine replacement, Bupropion therapy
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why would bilateral mandibular/greater palatine blocks be advised against in patients with COPD ? {{c1::May cause an uncomfortable airway constriction feeling}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What drugs should be avoided in COPD patients because of their respiratory depression effects ? {{c1::Barbiturates}} {{c1::Narcotics}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What drugs should be avoided when COPD patients are taking theophylline ? {{c1::Macrolids}} {{c1::Ciprofloxacin}}
fields.Extra: May reduce the metabolism of theophylline which may lead to toxicity
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of monitoring should be done continuously during treatment of a patient with COPD ? {{c1::Pulse oximetry}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Nitrous oxide induction and recovery times in patients with COPD are about {{c1::2}} times longer than normal.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What symptom in a COPD patient identifies them as unstable ? {{c1::dyspnea at rest}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Low flow oxygen (2L/min) is recommended if a person's oxygen saturation falls below {{c1::95%}}
fields.Extra: and asthma...
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An unstable or poorly controlled COPD patient is defined as having an O 2 Sat of < {{c1::91%}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What precautions should be taken in a patient with poorly controlled COPD ? {{c1::Defer and refer to physician}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In patients with COPD , poor oral hygiene can lead to what kind of lung infection ? {{c1::aspiration pneumonia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is a major side effect of anticholinergic drugs taken for COPD ? {{c1::xerostomia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which drug used to treat severe COPD has been linked to Stevens-Johnson syndrome ? {{c1::Theophylline}}
fields.Extra: = Phosphodiesterase inhibitor
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 4 categories of asthma triggers ? - {{c1::Extrinsic (allergic)}} - {{c2::Intrinsic (stress)}} - {{c3::Drug induced (NSAIDs)}} - {{c4::Exercise induced}}
fields.Extra: Extrinsic is the most common and accounts for 35% of adults cases
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which chronic inflammatory disease of the airways is characterized by reversible episodes of increased airway hyperresponsiveness ? {{c1::Asthma}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::Goblet cell}} hyperplasia may lead to excess mucus in the lungs and can be found in both asthma and COPD
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Measurements of lung function by {{c1::spirometry}} are done before and after β2-agonist administration to confirm the clinical diagnosis of asthma
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the most effective anti-inflammatory medications to treat asthma ? {{c1::Corticosteroids}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During an acute asthma attack , what kind of drug is administered ? {{c1::short-acting β2-agonist}}
fields.Extra: Can be paired with O2
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: If an acute asthma attack does not respond to fast-acting β2 agonists or supplemental oxygen , what do you do ? {{c1::IM Epinephrine}} {{c1::Activate EMS}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common fast-acting β2-agonist that comes in nebulizer form ? {{c1::Albuterol}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When are antihistamines prescribed for asthmatic patients ? {{c1::allergies trigger asthma attacks}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Decongestants such as {{c2:: pseudoephedrine }} are {{c1:: α 1 selective }} agonists that have a vasoconstricting effect.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the suffix used to denote leukotriene receptor antagonists ? {{c1::-lukast}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Patients with chronic asthma are classified by severity based on what criteria ? - {{c1::Frequency of diurnal symptoms}} - {{c1::Frequency of nocturnal symptoms (per month)}} - {{c1::FEV 1 and FEV 1 /FVC}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For an asymptomatic asthmatic patient, what drugs should be avoided ? {{c1::Narcotics}} {{c1::Barbiturates}} {{c1::NSAIDs (can precipitate an attack)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What precautions can be taken to prevent triggering an asthma attack in the dental office ? {{c1::Stress management}} {{c1::Excellent isolation}} {{c1::Avoid NSAIDs}} {{c1::Patient has taken meds and has rescue meds on hand}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Xerostomia in asthmatic patients is most often cause by {{c1::inhaler}} use.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Inhalers used by asthmatic patients have what common side effect ? {{c1::xerostomia}}
fields.Extra: Caries risk+++
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Oral candidiasis occurs in about 5% of asthmatic patients taking what type of treatment ? {{c1::inhaled corticosteroids}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Can asthma patients be treated with nitrous oxide ? {{c1::Yes}}
fields.Extra: Non-irritating and sedative effects
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, should COPD patients be treated with nitrous oxide ? {{c1::No}}
fields.Extra: Contraindicated in severe COPD Relative contraindication in mild and moderate COPD
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why would nasal obstruction be a relative contraindication to nitrous oxide treatment ? {{c1::N 2 O is administered through nasal masks}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is pregnancy a relative contraindication to nitrous oxide treatment ? {{c1::N 2 O readily enters fetal circulation and can be toxic to cells undergoing mitosis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is otitis media a contraindication to nitrous oxide treatment ? {{c1::concerns over pressure buildup and gas diffusion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is sickle cell disease a relative contraindication to nitrous oxide treatment ? {{c1::Chronic N 2 0 use decreases Vit B12}}
fields.Extra: Sickle cell patients are already B12 deficient
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are 2 relative contraindications to nitrous oxide AND supplemental oxygen ? {{c1::Bleomycin use}} {{c1::Paraquat poisoning (herbicide)}}
fields.Extra: Bleomycin can induce sensitivity to oxygen toxicity
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The resistance to vertical seating forces is called {{c1::support}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What structures in the maxilla provide the most amount of support ? {{c1::Palate}} {{c1::Alveolar ridge}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What structures in the mandible provide the most amount of support ? {{c1::Buccal shelf}} {{c1::Retromolar pad}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What part of the denture provides support ? {{c1::denture base}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The resistance to horizontal dislodging forces is called {{c1::stability}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What anatomical elements provide stability in the maxilla and the mandible ? {{c1::Ridge height}} {{c1::Depth of vestibule}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What part of the denture provides stability ? {{c1::Denture flange}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The resistance to vertical dislodging forces is called {{c1::retention}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What part of the complete denture provides retention ? {{c1::Peripheral seal}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The attraction of unlike molecules is called {{c1::adhesion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The attraction of like molecules is termed {{c1::cohesion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What kind of saliva is favorable to complete denture retention ? {{c1::thin and watery}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What kind of saliva is unfavorable to complete denture retention ? {{c1::thick and ropy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The combination of {{c1:: adhesion }} and {{c1:: cohesion }} forces that maintains film integrity is called {{c2:: surface tension }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the soft-tissue consequences of an overextended denture flange ? {{c1::Sore spot or ulcer}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment is necessary for an overextended flange creating sore spots ? {{c1::relieve denture in that area and re-evaluate in a few weeks}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why shouldn't denture teeth be set on the ramus of the mandible ? {{c1::occlusal forces would dislodge them}}
fields.Extra: Would also develop sore spots
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A denture flange that is too short (underextension) would lead to a lack of {{c1::retention}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The best indicator for denture success is the {{c1::alveolar ridge}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What mode of curing do most denture acrylics depend on ? {{c1::heat-curing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The monomer in denture acrylic liquid is called {{c1::methyl methacrylate (MMA)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The inhibtor in denture acrylic liquid is called {{c1::hydroquinone}}
fields.Extra: Prevents MMA from polymerizing with itself
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the role of hydroquinone in denture acrylic liquid ? {{c1::inhibits MMA from polymerizing with itself}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The cross-linking agent in denture acrylic liquid is called {{c1::Glycol dimethacrylate}}
fields.Extra: Forms cross links in the cured resin to increase rigidity and resistance
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the role of glycol dimethacrylate in denture acrylic liquid ? {{c1::cross-linking agent}}
fields.Extra: Forms cross links in the cured resin to increase rigidity and resistance
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the activator in denture acrylic liquid called ? {{c1::Dimethyl-p-toluidine}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the role of Dimethyl-p-toluidine in denture acrylic liquid ? {{c1::breaks down benzoyl peroxide into its radical form}}
fields.Extra: Benzoyl peroxide is the initiator
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The bulk of denture acrylic powder is composed of {{c1::polymethyl methacrylate (PMMA)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The initiator in denture acrylic powder is called {{c1::benzoyl peroxide}} and it is broken down by dimethyl-p-toluidine
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What function does benzoyl peroxide serve in denture acrylic powder ? {{c1::initiator of polymerization reaction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The pigments in denture acrylic powder are salts of {{c1::iron}}, {{c1::cadmium}} or {{c1::organic dyes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the result when excessive monomer is used in denture processing ? {{c1::more shrinkage}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the ideal ratio of monomer to polymer for denture acrylics ? {{c1::1:3}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What effect does heating denture acrylics too quickly result in ? {{c1::porosity}}
fields.Extra: Monomer evaporates before polymerization
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What effect does underpacking denture acrylics result in ? {{c1::porosity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the two most common materials used to make denture teeth ? {{c1::Acrylic}} {{c1::Porcelain}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why do acrylic teeth have better retention to the denture base ? {{c1::bonds to acrylic resin of denture base}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why are porcelain denture teeth more esthetic than acrylic teeth ? {{c1::more stain and wear resistant}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Mechanical retention of porcelain denture teeth is achieved with {{c1::pins}} for the anteriors or {{c1::diatorics}} for the posteriors .
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The top half of the denture flask is called the {{c1::cope}} while the bottom half is called the {{c1::drag}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the first steps to undertake given an unconscious person ? 1. {{c1::Appraise scene safety}} 2. {{c1::Assess responsiveness}} 3. {{c1::Alert for help}} 4. {{c1::Assess breathing and pulse}} 5. {{c1::Activate EMS}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Given an unresponsive patient that is breathing normally and has a pulse , what course of action ? {{c1::Monitor till EMS arrives}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Given an unresponsive patient that is breathing abnormally and has a pulse , what course of action ? {{c1::Maintain patent airway and begin rescue breathing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Given an unresponsive patient that is breathing abnormally and does not have a pulse , what course of action ? {{c1::Begin CPR}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Given an unresponsive patient that is not breathing and does not have a pulse , what course of action ? {{c1::Begin CPR}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During CPR where do you perform chest compressions ? {{c1::lower half of the sternum between the nipples}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During CPR , how deep should compressions be ? {{c1::2 inches}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During CPR , what rate of compressions should be administered ? {{c1::100-120 compressions per minute}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During CPR , how many compressions should be administered per cycle ? {{c1::30}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During CPR what technique is used to obtain a patent airway ? {{c1::Head tilt-chin lift}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During CPR what technique is used to obtain a patent airway if cervical spine injury is suspected ? {{c1::jaw thrust}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During CPR , how many rescue breaths should be administered per cycle ? {{c1::2}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During CPR , how long should each rescue breath last ? {{c1::1 second}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the purpose of an automated external defibrillator ? {{c1::stop an abnormally beating heart}}
fields.Extra: Depolarizes the entire heart = reset button
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During CPR, where should AED pads be placed ? {{c1::Upper right and lower left of the chest}}
fields.Extra: Depolarizes the entire heart = reset button
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: If you did not witness cardiac arrest how many cycles of CPR should you administer before using an AED ? {{c1::5 cycles}}
fields.Extra: Depolarizes the entire heart = reset button
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Where should you check for a pulse in children and adults ? {{c1::Carotid artery}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Where should you check for a pulse in a baby ? {{c1::Brachial artery}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When doing CPR on a small child, what modifications relative to an adult ? - {{c1::Use 1 hand}} - {{c1::15 compressions for every 2 breaths}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When doing CPR on an infant, what modifications relative to an adult ? - {{c1::Use 2 fingers}} - {{c1::15 compressions for every 2 breaths}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When doing CPR on an infant or small child, how deep should chest compressions be ? {{c1::1/3 of chest cavity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When performing rescue breathing, 1 breath should be administered every {{c1::3}} seconds for children and every {{c1::5}} seconds for adults .
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What do you do if someone is choking and coughing ? {{c1::Encourage coughing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What do you do if a conscious person is choking and not coughing ? {{c1::5 back slaps then 5 abdominal thrusts and repeat }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Where should abdominal thrusts be administered when someone is choking ? {{c1::between the xiphoid process and the navel}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Once a choking person becomes unconscious , what do you do ? {{c1::Retrieve any visible mouth obstruction and begin CPR}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long can the brain survive without oxygen ? {{c1::6 minutes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In emphysema obstruction is present in inspiration, expiration, or both ? {{c1::expiration}}
fields.Extra: collapse of the unsupported alveoli traps air
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What Kennedy class is this edentulism ? {{c1::Class I}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What Kennedy class is this edentulism ? {{c1::Class II}}
