fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What Kennedy class is this edentulism ? {{c1::Class III}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What Kennedy class is this edentulism ? {{c1::Class IV}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which Kennedy class is composed of a bilateral distal extension ? {{c1::Class I}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which Kennedy class is composed of a unilateral distal extension ? {{c1::Class II}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which Kennedy class is composed of a unilateral bounded edentulous space ? {{c1::Class III}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which Kennedy class is composed of a bilateral bounded edentulous space that crosses the midline ? {{c1::Class IV}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Should a Kennedy classification be assigned before or after extractions ? {{c1::After}}
fields.Extra: Teeth than cannot be part of the treatment plan
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Are missing third molars considered as part of the Kennedy classification ? {{c1::No}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Are present third molars considered as part of the Kennedy classification ? {{c1::Yes}}
fields.Extra: Teeth than cannot be part of the treatment plan
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Are missing second molars considered as part of the Kennedy classification if they won't be replaced ? {{c1::No}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The most {{c1::posterior::anterior/posterior}} edentulous area determines the Kennedy classification.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In the Kennedy classification, other edentulous areas (not described by the class) are called {{c1::modifications}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: By definition, class IV edentulisms cannot have any {{c1::modifications}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What Kennedy classification ? {{c1::Class III mod 1}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What Kennedy classification ? {{c1::Class III mod 2}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What Kennedy classification ? {{c1::Class II mod 3}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What Kennedy classification ? {{c1::Class I mod 1}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the primary function of a major connector in a RPD ? {{c1::provide rigidity to the framework}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most rigid maxillary major connector ? {{c1::Complete palatal plate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What maxillary major connector is indicated in bilateral edentulous cases (class I) ? {{c1::Complete palatal plate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What maxillary major connector is indicated in the case of periodontally compromised teeth ? {{c1::Complete palatal plate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What maxillary major connector is indicated in the case of shallow palatal vaults ? {{c1::Complete palatal plate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What maxillary major connector is indicated in the case of flat or flabby ridges ? {{c1::Complete palatal plate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the least rigid maxillary major connector ? {{c1::Horseshoe}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When would a horseshoe major connector be indicated ? {{c1::large palatal torus}}
fields.Extra: In the case where the patient doesn't want surgery
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What maxillary major connector is indicated in short-span class III edentulous patients ? {{c1::palatal strap}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: All maxillary major connectors should cross the midline at a {{c1::right (90 degree)}} angle
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Beading of major connectors is exclusive to major connectors of which arch ? {{c1::maxillary arch}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The scribing of a 0.5mm rounded groove in the cast at the borders of the major connector is called {{c1::beading}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Beading is the scribing of a {{c1:: 0.5 }} mm rounded groove in the cast at the borders of the major connector
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the purpose of beading of a maxillary major connector ? {{c1::Add strength}} {{c1::Prevent food impaction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of mandibular major connector is used when the depth of the lingual vestibule is > 7mm ? {{c1::lingual bar}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A lingual bar is indicated when the lingual vestibule is greater than or equal to {{c1::7}} mm
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common mandibular major connector ? {{c1::Lingual bar}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What mandibular major connector is indicated when the lingual vestibule is < 7mm ? {{c1::Lingual plate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What mandibular major connector is indicated when additional tooth loss is anticipated ? {{c1::Lingual plate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What mandibular major connector is indicated in the presence of lingual tori ? {{c1::Lingual plate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What mandibular major connector is indicated in a class I edentulism (bilateral extension) ? {{c1::Lingual plate}}
fields.Extra: Lingual bar with cingulum bar?
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A lingual plate is indicated when the lingual vestibule is less than {{c1::7}} mm
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A labial bar may be indicated when key abutment teeth such as {{c1::canines}} are missing
fields.Extra: Allows for retention and stability to be evenly distributed on the remaining anteriors
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which mandibular major connector may be indicated given extreme labioversion of anterior teeth or vast tissue undercuts ? {{c1::labial bar}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which part of the denture framework joins the major connector, the rests and retainers ? {{c1::minor connectors}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The rigid extension of an RPD framework that contacts the occlusal, lingual, or incisal surface of an abutment tooth is called a {{c1::rest}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the purpose of an RPD rest ? {{c1::provide support}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The tooth preparation that allows an abutment tooth to receive and support a rest is called the {{c1::rest seat}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the mesio-distal width of an occlusal rest ? {{c1::1/3 MD width}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the linguofacial width of an occlusal rest ? {{c1::1/2 intercuspal width}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How deep should an occlusal rest be ? ? {{c1::1.5mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The occlusal rest floor should form what angle with the vertical minor connector ? {{c1::< 90 degrees}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What shape is a cingulum rest ? {{c1::inverted V or U}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What length should a cingulum rest be (mesio-distally) ? {{c1::2.5-3 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What width should a cingulum rest be (labiolingually) ? {{c1::2 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How deep should a cingulum rest be ? {{c1::1.5 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cingulum rests are contraindicated in which teeth ? {{c1::mandibular incisors}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What shape is an incisal rest ? {{c1::Rounded notch at the incisal angle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the mesio-distal length of an incisal rest ? {{c1::2.5mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How deep is an incisal rest ? {{c1::1.5mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which rest type has the least favorable leverage profile ? {{c1::Incisal rest}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The metal plate that contacts proximal surfaces of abutment RPD teeth is referred to as the {{c1::proximal plate}}
fields.Extra: Follows the guide plane
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The flat parallel surfaces of abutment teeth that provide a path of insertion and removal are termed {{c1::guide planes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Guide planes provide a reproducible path of {{c1::insertion}} and {{c1::removal}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How wide should guide planes be ? {{c1::1/3 of the buccolingual width}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How far down should guide planes extend ? {{c1::2-3mm from the vertical}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The rotational movement of a distal extension RPD is centered around a line drawn through the {{c1::most distal rests}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Where should indirect retainers be placed to maximally brace rotational movements of distal extensions ? {{c1::perpendicular and anterior to the fulcrum line}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A direct retainer is composed of what 4 elements ? - {{c1::Rest}} - {{c1::Minor connector}} - {{c1::Retentive clasp arm}} - {{c1::Reciprocal clasp arm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The {{c1:: retentive }} clasp arm provides {{c2::retention}} while the {{c1:: reciprocal }} clasp arm provides {{c2::stability}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Extracoronal retainer clasps should encircle at tooth at least {{c1::180}} degrees
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What more esthetic alternative exists to RPD clasps ? {{c1::intracoronal retainers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The female part of an intracoronal retainer is built into the {{c1::survey crown}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The tip of a retentive clasp contacts tooth in the gingival third below the {{c1::height of contour/survey line}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which part of the retentive clasp should be below the height of contour ? {{c1::just the tip}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The tip of a retentive clasp is designed to resist dislodging forces by engaging an {{c1::undercut}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When is a retentive clasp "active" ? {{c1::When dislodging forces are applied. Otherwise passive}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, the retentive clasp is situated on the {{c1::buccal}} surface of the tooth while the reciprocal clasp is on the {{c1::lingual}} surface
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The reciprocal clasp contacts tooth {{c1::above::below/above}} the height of contour/survey line
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: RPD clasps that originate above the survey line are classified as {{c1::suprabulge}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: RPD clasps that originate below the survey line are classified as {{c1::infrabulge}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most commonly used RPD clasp ? {{c1::circumferential (Akers) clasp}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of RPD clasp is indicated when an undercut is adjacent to a bound edentulous space ? {{c1::Ring clasp}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The retentive part of a a ring clasp on an isolated molar is situated where ? {{c1::mesial (near the BES)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In a class III RPD , what type of clasp is indicated to gain retention on the dentate side ? {{c1::Embrasure clasp}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are 2 contraindications to I-bars and T-bar clasps ? - {{c1::Insufficient vestibule depth}} - {{c1::Soft tissue undercuts}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 components of an RPI clasp ? - {{c1::Mesial rest}} - {{c1::I bar}} - {{c1::Proximal plate (distal)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 components of an RPC clasp ? - {{c1::Mesial rest}} - {{c1::Circumferential clasp}} - {{c1::Proximal plate (distal)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In an RPI clasp assembly, a class {{c1:: II }} lever system is obtained by placing the rest on the {{c2:: mesial }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why use wrought wire when placing clasps on periodontally compromised or endo treated teeth ? {{c1::more flexible}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of clasp would you place on periodontally compromised or endo treated teeth ? {{c1::wrought wire}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What clasp is indicated in a bound edentulous space ? {{c1::Akers clasp (circumferential)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In order of preference, what clasps should be used in distal extension RPDs ? {{c1::RPI}} > {{c1::RPA}} > {{c1::wrought wire}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What amount of shrinkage is expected when making cobalt-chrome frameworks ? {{c1::2.3%}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Most RPD frameworks are made of what alloy ? {{c1::Cobalt-chromium}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the main reason cobalt-chromium clasps break ? {{c1::cold-working when seated and dislodged}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [1] represent ? {{c1::Axial wall}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [3] represent ? {{c1::Occlusal reduction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [4] represent ? {{c1::Functional cusp bevel}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does [2] represent ? {{c1::Margin/finish line}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The filling that restores missing tooth structure in order to prepare for a crown is called a {{c1::core (foundation restoration)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 guiding principles of tooth preparation ? - {{c1::Biology}} - {{c1::Mechanics}} - {{c1::Esthetics}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The thinnest gingival tissue is found in what 2 spots ? {{c1::Lingual molars}} {{c1::Facial premolars}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When preparing teeth, how can you minimize the risk of thermal injury to the pulp ? {{c1::Water spray}} {{c1::Use sharp instruments}} {{c1::Apply intermittent light pressure}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The features that prevent removal of a crown along the long axis of the tooth are called the {{c1::retention form}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The features that prevent removal of a crown along by apical, horizontal or oblique forces are called the {{c1::resistance form}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The inner surface of a denture or crown that is in contact with oral mucosa or dental tissue is referred to as the {{c1::intaglio}} surface
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The angle of convergence formed between two axial crown surfaces is referred to as the {{c1::taper}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which feature of crown preparation is under the most operator control ? {{c1::Axial wall taper}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: As the taper or angle of convergence of axial crown walls get smaller , how does retention change ? {{c1::Retention increases}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The ideal taper or angle of convergence for a crown prep is between {{c1::6}} and {{c1::10}} degrees
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The height , or length of a crown prep is measured from the {{c1::occlusal/incisal}} surface to the {{c1::crown margin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the minimum axial wall height needed to provide retention in incisors and premolars ? {{c1::3 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the minimum axial wall height needed to provide retention in molars ? {{c1::4 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The minimum height to base ratio of a crown prep is {{c1::0.4}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does prep width or height contribute more to retention and resistance form ? {{c1::height}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Given a short clinical crown preparation, what additional prep features can be added to provide more retention ? {{c1::Buccal grooves}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Given a short clinical crown preparation, what additional prep features can be added to provide more resistance ? {{c1::Proximal grooves}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For a full coverage metal crown what is the minimum thickness at the margin ? {{c1::0.5mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For a full coverage metal crown what is the minimum thickness at the non-contact areas ? {{c1::1 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For a full coverage metal crown what is the minimum thickness at the contact areas ? {{c1::1.5 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For a full coverage porcelain crown what is the minimum thickness in all areas ? {{c1::1.5 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For a full coverage PFM crown what is the minimum thickness in non-contact areas ? {{c1::1.5 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For a full coverage PFM crown what is the optimal thickness in contact areas ? {{c1::2 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The amount of occlusal tooth structure removed during preparation is called {{c1::reduction }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The amount of space left between tooth and opposing teeth after preparation is called {{c1::clearance}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the ideal amount of clearance needed between tooth preparation and opposing teeth ? {{c1::1.5-2mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: While {{c1::supragingival}} margins are better for periodontal health , {{c1::subgingival}} margins may be more esthetic .
fields.Extra: Subgingival margins can lead to inflammation which is inesthetic...
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of preparation margin provides the best marginal seal ? {{c1::Featheredge}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 2 major drawbacks to featheredge margins ? {{c1::Insufficient clearance for most materials}} {{c1::Difficult to visualize margins (lab)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of margin preparation is recommended for gold crowns ? {{c1::Light chamfer}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How thick is a light chamfer margin ? {{c1::0.3-0.5mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of preparation margin is used for the lingual of PFM crowns and some ceramic crowns ? {{c1::Heavy chamfer}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How thick is a heavy chamfer prep margin ? {{c1::1.0 -1.5 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: If a preparation margin is under-reduced the lab is forced to {{c1::overcontour}} the crown to attain minimal thicknesses
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the #1 lab complaint regarding crown preparations ? {{c1::under-reduced preps}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of prep margin is used for all-ceramic crowns ? {{c1::Shoulder margin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How thick are shoulder margins ? {{c1::1.0-1.5mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What iatrogenic result may occur when cutting an aggressive shoulder margin ? {{c1::pulp exposure/pulp irritation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What partial coverage restoration remains within the cusps ? {{c1::Inlay}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What partial coverage restoration covers some cusps ? {{c1::Onlay}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What partial coverage restoration covers all cusps ? {{c1::Overlay}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the main advantage of partial coverage restorations relative to full coverage crowns ? {{c1::conserve tooth structure}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To prevent wear , occlusal point contacts of restorations on antagonist teeth are preferred to be {{c1::broad}} and {{c1::flat}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What occlusal scheme is ideal in Class I occlusion ? {{c1::Cusp-marginal ridge}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What occlusal scheme is accepted in Class II maloclussions ? {{c1::Cusp-fossa}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of pontic allows good hygiene but poor esthetics and so is often used in the posterior mandible ? {{c1::hygienic/sanitary}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of pontic should never be used because it makes hygiene very difficult ? {{c1::Saddle/ridge-lap}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of pontic is very similar to the hygienic pontic but with marginally better esthetics ? {{c1::Conical}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of pontic allows good hygiene and good esthetics , and is used in anterior restorations ? {{c1::Modified ridge-lap}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most esthetic pontic shape used for anterior cases only and often requires mucogingival surgery ? {{c1::Ovate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When would a nonrigid connector be indicated in a bridge restoration ? {{c1::impossible to obtain common insertion path of both abutments}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Connectors for PFM bridges should have a minimum height of {{c1::3 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Doubling connector height increases connector strength by a factor of {{c1::8}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the most common hemostatic agents used to impregnate retraction cord ? - {{c1::AlCl (Hemodent)}} - {{c1::FeSO 4 (Viscostat)}} - {{c1::Epinephrine}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Electrosurgery for tissue management before impressions is contraindicated in patients with what medical devices ? {{c1::Pacemakers}} {{c1::Insulin pumps}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Agar is a {{c1::reversible}} hydrocolloid
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What highly-accurate impression material is a reversible hydrocolloid ? {{c1::Agar}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Agar changes between {{c1:: sol }} and {{c1:: gel }} states based on {{c2:: temperature }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the least accurate impression material ? {{c1::Alginate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the approximate setting time of alginate ? {{c1::3-4 minutes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When preparing alginate , the {{c1::powder}} should be added into the {{c1::water}} to avoid bubble formation and to obtain a smoother product.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The primary ingredient of alginate that provides resistance is {{c1::diatomaceous earth}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The active ingredient of alginate is {{c1::potassium alginate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does water temperature affect alginate setting time ? {{c1::hotter water sets faster and inversely}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does water quantity affect alginate setting time ? {{c1::less water sets faster and inversely}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The absorption of water by hydrocolloids is called {{c1::imbibition}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The loss of water by hydrocolloids is called {{c1::syneresis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What types of impression materials are affected by BOTH water imbibition and syneresis ? {{c1::Hydrocolloids}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The setting reaction of polysulfide rubber creates what byproduct ? {{c1::water}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is polysulfide rubber hydrophilic or hydrophobic ? {{c1::hydrophobic}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A polysulfide rubber impression should be poured within {{c1::30}} to {{c1::45}} minutes of impression
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Apart from hydrocolloids , what other impression material is affected by syneresis ? {{c1::Polysulfide rubber}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Condensation silicone creates what byproduct when it sets ? {{c1::alcohol}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The alcohol byproduct of condensation silicone causes {{c1::shrinkage}} of the impression when evaporated
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A condensation silicone impression should be poured within {{c1::30}} minutes of impression
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is polyether impression material hydrophilic or hydrophobic ? {{c1::hydrophilic}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Other than hydrocolloids , what impression material is prone to imbibition ? {{c1::Polyether}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A polyether impression should be poured within {{c1::60}} minutes of impression.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the main drawback of polyether impression materials ? {{c1::Very stiff}}
fields.Extra: Hard to remove, unexpected avulsions...
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What byproducts are created by addition silicones (PVS) ? {{c1::None!}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which impression material has the best fine detail, elastic recovery, and dimensional stability ? {{c1::PVS (addition silicone)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Addition silicone is inhibited by the {{c1::sulfur}} in the {{c2::latex}} of gloves and rubber dams
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long after impression should a PVS (addition silicone) impression be poured ? {{c1::whenever... extremely stable}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The functional cusp bevel is located on the {{c1::palatal}} cusp of maxillary teeth and on the {{c1::buccal}} cusp of mandibular teeth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Gypsum is mined as {{c1::calcium-sulfate dihydrate}} and it is manufactured with heat to become {{c2::calcium-sulfate hemihydrate}} which is used to make impression plaster and stone
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Type I gypsum is referred to as {{c1::impression plaster}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Type II gypsum is referred to as {{c1::model plaster}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Type III gypsum is referred to as {{c1::dental stone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Type IV gypsum is referred to as {{c1::high-strength, low expansion dental stone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Type V gypsum is referred to as {{c1::high-strength, high expansion dental stone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The extra water needed to obtain a workable mix of gypsum that does not react with the gypsum is called the {{c1::gauging water}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does increasing the amount of water in gypsum affect the strength of the final product ? {{c1::decreases strength}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does increasing the amount of water in gypsum affect the porosity of the final product ? {{c1::increases porosity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does increasing the amount of water in gypsum affect the expansion of the final product ? {{c1::decreases expansion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does increasing the amount of water in gypsum affect the setting time of the final product ? {{c1::increases setting time}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Impression plaster exhibits {{c1::low::high/low}} expansion
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is impression plaster used for ? {{c1::mounting casts in articulator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is model plaster used for ? {{c1::mouth guards and essex retainers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is type III dental stone used for ? {{c1::removable prostheses and diagnostic casts}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is is type IV stone used for ? {{c1::die fabrication}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of gypsum product exhibits the least expansion ? {{c1::Type IV dental stone}}
fields.Extra: Followed by impression plaster (Type I)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which dental stone has the greatest abrasion resistance and uses the least gauging water ? {{c1::Type IV stone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is type V dental stone used for ? {{c1::Die fabrication}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long should gypsum be mixed in a vacuum mixer ? {{c1::20 seconds}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long should gypsum be mixed if hand mixing ? {{c1::30 seconds}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Approximately how long is the setting time of gypsum ? {{c1::45 to 60 minutes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What solutions can be used to disinfect a cast after the stone has set ? - {{c1::1:10 bleach solution}} - {{c1::Glutaraldehyde}} - {{c1::Iodophor spray}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does temperature affect gypsum setting time ? {{c1::hotter water decreases setting time}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does water quantity affect gypsum setting time ? {{c1::less water decreases setting time}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does using a slurry water affect gypsum setting time ? {{c1::decreases setting time}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does longer spatulation time affect gypsum setting time ? {{c1::decreases setting time}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The most porous gypsum material is {{c1::type I}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 noble metals used in dentistry ? - {{c1::Gold}} - {{c1::Platinum}} - {{c1::Palladium}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In a metal alloy, what property does gold provide ? {{c1::tarnish resistance}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In a metal alloy, what properties does platinum provide ? {{c1::strength and increased melting temperature}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In a metal alloy, what property does palladium provide ? {{c1::increased strength}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why are silver alloys not used in dentistry ? {{c1::they cause greening of porcelain}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A high noble alloy is ≥ {{c1::60}}% noble, of which at least {{c2::40}}% is {{c1::gold}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A noble alloy is ≥ {{c1::25}}% noble metal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A base metal alloy is < {{c1::25}}% noble metal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Type I gold is used for what kind of restorations ? {{c1::Class V}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Type II gold is used for what kind of restorations ? {{c1::Inlays}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Type III gold is used for what kind of restorations ? {{c1::Crowns}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Type IV gold is used for what kind of restorations ? {{c1::RPD castings, bridges}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Type {{c1::I}} gold alloys are the softest while type {{c1::IV}} is the strongest
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Type {{c1::IV}} gold alloys have the least gold content while type {{c1::I}} have the most gold content
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The ability of a material to resist fracture during compression is termed {{c1::compressive strength}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The ability of a material to resist fracture during pulling is termed {{c1::tensile strength}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The ability of a material to resist fracture during bending is termed {{c1::flexural strength}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The ability of a material to resist the propagation of a crack is called {{c1::fracture toughness}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which dental material has the greatest fracture toughness ? {{c1::Zirconia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The physical parameter of a material's ability to sustain deformation without permanent change in size or shape is termed the {{c1::elastic modulus}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Mathematically, the modulus of elasticity is the {{c1::stress}} divided by {{c1::strain}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The modulus of elasticity is a measure of a material's {{c1::stiffness}} or {{c1::rigidity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A material that fractures easily without substantial dimension change is described as {{c1::brittle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the classic example of a brittle dental material ? {{c1::porcelain}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A material that deforms easily under tensile strength but does not return to its original shape is described as {{c1::ductile}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A material that deforms easily under tensile strength and returns to its original shape is described as {{c1::elastic}}
fields.Extra: eg. Rubber band
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Give an example of a ductile dental product : {{c1::orthodontic wire}}
fields.Extra: eg. Rubber band
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A material's ability to deform under compressive stress is termed {{c1::malleability}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What dental material is very malleable ? {{c1::Gold}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The force that can be applied to an object before it begins permanently deforming is called the {{c1::yield strength}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The ability of a material to be burnished is best described by what value ? {{c1::percentage elongation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Burnishing occurs when contact stress locally exceeds the {{c1::yield strength}} of a material
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What dental material is easily burnished ? {{c1::Gold}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The fractional change in size per degree change in temperature is the {{c1::coefficient of thermal expansion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Rank from highest coefficient of thermal expansion to lowest : {{c1::Composite > Metal > Tooth > Ceramic::Metal, Tooth, Composite, Ceramic }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which dental material has the closest coefficient of thermal expansion to tooth ? {{c1::gold}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A provisional restoration that is made in the patient's mouth is said to employ a {{c1::direct}} method
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A provisional restoration that is made on a cast is said to employ an {{c1::indirect}} method
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of prefabricated crown materials exist ? - {{c1::Polycarbonate}} - {{c1::Aluminum}} - {{c1::Stainless steel}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which material is most often used to create provisional restorations using an indirect method ? {{c1::PMMA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is PMMA not the ideal material to make provisional crowns using a direct method ? {{c1::Exothermic polymerization can irritate pulp}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of methylacrylate resin is used to create provisional restorations and has a less exothermic setting reaction but also less resistance than PMMA ? {{c1::PEMA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which provisional crown material is often used in the direct method and is more brittle than PMMA but shrinks less and is less irritating ? {{c1::Bis-acryl composite}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What substance in provisional cements inhibits polymerization of resin ? {{c1::Eugenol}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The bonding of porcelain to metal in PFM crowns is done via the {{c1::monomolecular oxidative}} layer
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What color is the monomolecular oxidative layer ? {{c1::grey/black}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of porcelain is placed directly over the metal part of a PFM crown to mask the monomolecular oxidative layer ? {{c1::Opaque porcelain}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of porcelain is placed directly over the opaque layer and contains most of the shade of the crown ? {{c1::body/dentin porcelain}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of porcelain is placed directly over the body layer and is the most translucent ? {{c1::incisal/occlusal porcelain}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of margin is used on the buccal face of a PFM crown ? {{c1::shoulder}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of margin is used on the lingual face of a PFM crown ? {{c1::chamfer}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Occlusal contacts on a PFM crown must be ≥ {{c1::1.5}}mm away from the porcelain-metal junction
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of failure occurred when the porcelain separates from the metal in a PFM crown ? {{c1::Adhesive failure}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of failure occurred when the porcelain chips or breaks in a PFM crown ? {{c1::Cohesive failure}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Porcelain-porcelain cohesive failures in PFM crowns can occur if there are what imperfections in the porcelain ? {{c1::inclusions}} or {{c1::voids}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Metal-metal cohesive failures in PFM crowns can occur if the oxide layer is too {{c1::thick}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When bonding a glass-infiltrated ceramic, etching is done with what kind of acid ? {{c1::hydrofluoric acid}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Bonding of glass-infiltrated ceramics requires etching with {{c1:: hydrofluoric acid }} then {{c1:: silane }} treatment.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the role of silane in bonding a glass-infiltrated ceramic ? {{c1::coupling agent}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Are zirconia and alumina crowns more often bonded or cemented ? {{c1::cemented}}
fields.Extra: You absolutely can bond zirconia.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What amount of reduction is necessary for a porcelain veneer in the gingival third ? {{c1::0.3mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What amount of reduction is necessary for a porcelain veneer in the facial third ? {{c1::0.5mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What amount of reduction is necessary for a porcelain veneer in the incisal portion ? {{c1::1-2mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Ideally, the preparation of veneers must stay within {{c1::enamel}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the main drawback of resin-bonded bridges ? {{c1::They may debond}}
fields.Extra: Of note, cantilever designs have a better prognosis than 2 retainer designs
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 components of the Munsell color system ? - {{c1::Hue}} - {{c1::Chroma}} - {{c1::Value}}
fields.Extra: Image courtesy : Jacobolus, CC BY-SA 3.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The color family of a color is given by its {{c1::hue}}
fields.Extra: Image courtesy : Jacobolus, CC BY-SA 3.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The saturation or intensity of a color is given by its {{c1::chroma}}
fields.Extra: Image courtesy : Jacobolus, CC BY-SA 3.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The lightness or darkness of a color is given by its {{c1::value}}
fields.Extra: Image courtesy : Jacobolus, CC BY-SA 3.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The phenomenon that color appears different under different lighting is termed {{c1::metamerism}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the ideal light temperature for shade matching ? {{c1::5500K}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The phenomenon that obects emit visible light when exposed to UV light is called {{c1::fluorescence}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The light effect of a translucent material appearing blue in reflected light and red-orange in transmitted light is called {{c1::opalescence}}
fields.Extra: Image courtesy : Annielogue, CC BY-SA 2.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An opalescent translucent material will appear {{c1:: blue }} in {{c2:: reflected }} light
fields.Extra: Image courtesy : Annielogue, CC BY-SA 2.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In what order do you look at Chroma, Hue and Value when shade selecting ? 1. {{c1::Value}} 2. {{c1::Chroma}} 3. {{c1::Hue}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Staining a crown results in a loss of {{c1:: fluorescence }} and increases {{c2:: metamerism }} while decreasing {{c3:: value }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the first thing to check when getting a crown back from the lab ? {{c1::Shade}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: After checking shade , what do you check on a newly received crown ? {{c1::Proximal contacts}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which temporary cement has pulp soothing properties ? {{c1::Zinc oxide eugenol}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which component of zinc phosphate cement causes pulpal irritation ? {{c1::phosphoric acid}}
fields.Extra: Zinc oxyde + phosphoric acid
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the historic "gold standard" luting cement ? {{c1::Zinc phosphate}}
fields.Extra: Zinc oxyde + phosphoric acid
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does zinc phosphate cement chemically bond to tooth structure ? {{c1::No}}
fields.Extra: Zinc oxyde + phosphoric acid
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How is zinc phosphate cement mixed and why ? {{c1::Chilled glass slab due to exothermic reaction}}
fields.Extra: Zinc oxyde + phosphoric acid
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does zinc polycarboxylate cement chemically bond to tooth structure ? {{c1::Yes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does zinc polycarboxylate cement bond to tooth structure ? {{c1::chelates to calcium}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why does zinc polycarboxylate provoke less pulpal irritation than zinc phosphate ? {{c1::Weaker acid}}
fields.Extra: Polyacrylic acid with carboxyl groups + zinc oxyde
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which luting cements serves as a fluoride reservoir ? {{c1::Glass ionomers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does glass ionomer adhere to tooth structure ? {{c1::chelates calcium}}
fields.Extra: Polyacrylic acid + FAS
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: RMGIC has {{c1::greater::greater/lower}} strength than regular glass ionomer
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: RMGIC has {{c1::lower::greater/lower}} solubility than regular glass ionomer
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Resin-modified glass ionomer cements should not be used with what type of crowns ? {{c1::all-ceramic crowns (except zirconia)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why shouldn't RMGIC be used with all-ceramic crowns ? {{c1::water absorption can cause fracture}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of cement has the greatest compressive strength ? {{c1::Resin cement}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which category of resin cement doesn't need adhesive application but has a weaker bond to tooth structure ? {{c1::autoadhesive resin cements}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which category of resin cement needs adhesive application and has excellent bond strength to tooth structure ? {{c1::adhesive resin cements}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of resin cement is most color stable ? {{c1::Light cured}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, zirconia crowns can be cemented with what luting cements ? {{c1::GIC or RMGIC}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, metal crowns are cemented with what luting cements ? {{c1::GIC or RMGIC}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, lithium disilicate crowns can be cemented with what luting cement ? {{c1::dual-cure resin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, feldspathic porcelain crowns can be cemented with what luting cement ? {{c1::light-cure resin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the purpose of ditching a die ? {{c1::Exposes the margin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Scraping and cleaning away stone below a prep margin on a stone die is called {{c1::ditching}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the purpose of adding die spacer to a prep ? {{c1::provides space for luting cement}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the lab steps involved in making a crown ? 1. {{c1::Waxing}} 2. {{c2::Spruing}} 3. {{c3::Investing}} 4. {{c4::Burnout}} 5. {{c5::Casting}} 6. {{c6::Recovery and quenching}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the purpose of spruing when making a metal crown ? {{c1::makes a path for molten metal to go into}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What investing material is used for a gold restoration ? {{c1::Gypsum-bonded investments}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What investing material is used for a PFM restoration ? {{c1::Phosphate-bonded investments}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What investing material is used for a base metal restoration ? {{c1::Silica-bonded investments}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The lab step of melting out the wax positive to leave room for the metal is called {{c1::burnout}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The lab step of melting metal into the investment is called {{c1::casting}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the purpose of quenching a cast metal restoration ? {{c1::Makes it more malleable for finishing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Inadequate condensing of porcelain during crown fabrication can lead to {{c1::porosity}} of the porcelain
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Heating of acrylic too quickly can lead to {{c1::porosity}} of the acrylic
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A sprue that is too thin can lead to {{c1::shrinkage porosity}} of the metal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A sprue that is too short can lead to {{c1::back-pressure porosity}} of the metal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An opalescent translucent material will appear {{c1:: red-orange }} in {{c2:: transmitted }} light
fields.Extra: Image courtesy : Annielogue, CC BY-SA 2.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 major types of cells in the pulp ? - {{c1::Fibroblasts}} - {{c1::Odontoblasts}} - {{c1::Undifferentiated mesenchymal cells}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What 2 anatomic factors make the pulp very susceptible to inflammation and infection ? - {{c1::Surrounded by hard dentin (incompressible)}} - {{c1::Lacks collateral circulation (terminal vascularization)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of dentin is very hard and occurs in response to aging and slowly advancing caries ? {{c1::Sclerotic/secondary dentin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of dentin is deposited in response to minor damage ? {{c1::Reactionary dentin (tertiary)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of dentin is deposited in response to major damage ? {{c1::Reparative dentin (tertiary)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Secondary dentin is produced by what cells ? {{c1::Odontoblasts}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Reactionary dentin is produced by what cells ? {{c1::Odontoblasts}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Reparative dentin is produced by what cells ? {{c1::Odontoblast-like cells}}
fields.Extra: Odontoblasts are dead and undifferentiated mesenchymal cells differentiate into OB-like cells and deposit a mineralized matrix (not actually dentin, but we call it that)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Pulpal death as a result of rapidly advancing caries or other damage is called {{c1::pulpal necrosis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What part of the pulp-dentin complex does [1] represent ? {{c1::Predentin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What part of the pulp-dentin complex does [2] represent ? {{c1::Odontoblastic layer}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What part of the pulp-dentin complex does [3] represent ? {{c1::Cell-free zone of Weil}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What part of the pulp-dentin complex does [4] represent ? {{c1::Cell-rich zone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What part of the pulp-dentin complex does [5] represent ? {{c1::Pulp core}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which nerve fibers are triggered by cold ? {{c1::Aδ}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Sharp transient dentinal pain is transmitted by which nerve fibers ? {{c1::Aδ}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Where are Aδ fibers situated in the pulp ? {{c1::Coronally}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Where are C fibers situated in the pulp ? {{c1::Centrally}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Aδ fibers are {{c1::large::small/large}} {{c2::myelinated::(un)myelinated}} afferent nerves
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: C fibers are {{c1::small::small/large}} {{c2::unmyelinated::(un)myelinated}} afferent nerves
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Dull, throbbing pulpitis pain is due to which nerve fibers ? {{c1::C}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which nerve fibers are more solicitted when heat is applied ? {{c1::C fibers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The medical term for a heightened response to pain is {{c1::hyperalgesia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The medical term for a pain due to a stimuli that is not normally painful is {{c1::allodynia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is a non-dental example of allodynia ? {{c1::Sunburn}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Pain that is felt in one part of the body but is caused by another part of the body is called {{c1::referred pain}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Preauricular pain is often referred from which teeth ? {{c1::Mandibular molars}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An endodontic diagnosis is composed of a {{c1::pulpal}} diagnosis and a {{c1::periapical}} diagnosis
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 2 most common tests used to establish a pulpal diagnosis ? {{c1::Cold test}} {{c1::Electric pulp test}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long is a chilled pellet applied when doing a pulp test ? {{c1::5 seconds}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which part of the tooth do you apply the chilled pellet to when doing a cold test ? {{c1::middle third of facial surface}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The pulpal diagnosis from a cold test is based on the {{c1::intensity}} and {{c1::duration}} of response to cold
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the least reliable pulp test ? {{c1::EPT}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why are electric pulp tests not ideal for establishing pulpal diagnoses ? {{c1::indicates if vital sensory fibers are present but doesn't inform on vascular supply}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Electric pulp tests are contraindicated in patients with {{c1::cardiac pacemakers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What visual change may accompany pulp necrosis ? {{c1::discoloration}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the objective of access preparation in endodontics ? {{c1::obtain straight-line access to root canal and apex}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is a rubberdam mandatory for endodontics ? {{c1::Yes. It is standard of care}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What shape are the endodontic access cavities of maxillary incisors ? {{c1:: Triangular (sometimes ovoid for lateral)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What shape are the endodontic access cavities of mandibular incisors ? {{c1::ovoid}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why aren't maxillary central incisor access preparations oval ? {{c1::Need to eliminate pulp horns}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Maxillary central and lateral incisors have how many canals ? {{c1::almost 100% have 1 canal}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Mandibular central incisors most often have how many canals ? {{c1::1 canal}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Mandibular lateral incisors most often have how many canals ? {{c1::1 canal (70%) and 2 canals (30%)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Maxillary and mandibular canines most often have how many canals ? {{c1::1 canal}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The access preparations for maxillary and mandibular premolars is what shape ? {{c1::Ovoid}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which premolar is most likely to have 2 roots and 2 canals ? {{c1::Maxillary first premolar}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Maxillary first premolar most often have how many root canals ? {{c1::2}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Maxillary second premolars and mandibular premolars most often have how many root canals ? {{c1::1 canal, though 2 is possible}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The access preparation for maxillary molars is what shape ? {{c1::blunted triangle/ rhomboid}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How many root canals do maxillary molars most often have ? {{c1::4 canals, and occasionally 3}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How many root canals do mandibular molars most often have ? {{c1::3 canals and sometimes 4}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What shape is the access preparation in mandibular molars ? {{c1::Trapezoidal}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 2 major types of materials used to make endodontic files ? - {{c1::Stainless steel}} - {{c1::Nickel titanium}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Stainless steel hand files have what taper ? {{c1::.02}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: NiTi rotary instruments usually have what tapers ? {{c1::.04}} or {{c1::.06}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The core of a K-file is what shape ? {{c1::twisted square}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The core of an H-file is what shape ? {{c1::spiral cone}}
fields.Extra: "teardrop cross-section"
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A K-file is used with what movement ? {{c1::watch winding}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An H-file is used with what movement ? {{c1::retraction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the ISO color order of hand-file sizes ? {{c1::White, Yellow, Red, Blue, Green, Black}}
fields.Extra: File tip increases by 0.05 between each color
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A size 0.06 hand file is what color ? {{c1::Pink}}
fields.Extra: File tip increases by 0.05 between each color
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A size 0.08 hand file is what color ? {{c1::Grey}}
fields.Extra: File tip increases by 0.05 between each color
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A size 0.10 hand file is what color ? {{c1::Purple}}
fields.Extra: File tip increases by 0.05 between each color
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A size 0.15 hand file is what color ? {{c1::White}}
fields.Extra: File tip increases by 0.05 between each color
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Where is D1 on an endodontic file ? {{c1::tip}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long are the cutting flutes on endodontic hand files ? {{c1::16mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the formula for finding endo file dimension based on taper, length, and tip size ? {{c1::[tip size] + [taper]x[length]}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which endodontic instrument is used to open orifices or to create straight-line access ? {{c1::Gates-Glidden drills}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which endodontic instrument is used to entangle and remove pulp ? {{c1::Barbed broach}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The core of an reamer file is what shape ? {{c1::twisted triangle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The cleaning and shaping technique that involves using larger files first then bringing smaller files to the apex is called the {{c1::crown-down}} technique
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The cleaning and shaping technique that involves using smaller files to the apex first then larger files coronally is called the {{c1::step-back}} technique
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What must be done between each pass of an endodontic file in a canal ? {{c1::Irrigation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the effect of sodium hypochlorite in endodontics ? {{c1::dissolves organic material}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the effect of EDTA in endodontics ? {{c1::dissolves inorganic/mineralized material}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the effect of chloroform in endodontics ? {{c1::dissolves gutta percha}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of bacteria are most often present in primary endodontic infection ? {{c1::Bacteroides (gram negative obligate anerobes)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of bacteria are most often present in secondary endodontic infections ? {{c1::Enterococcus faecalis (gram positive facultative anerobe)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the purpose of endodontic obturation ? {{c1::Seal the canal system and remaining bacteria}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What endodontic obturation material is most commonly used ? {{c1::Gutta percha}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of endodontic sealer material is most commonly used ? {{c1::ZOE sealer}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What material do endodontic sealer and gutta-percha both contain ? {{c1::Zinc oxyde eugenol}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Historically, what are the 2 most common obturation methods ? -{{c1::Warm vertical condensation}} -{{c1::Cold lateral condensation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: If the "problem" with an endodontic treatment is in the canal, what treatment is indicated ? {{c1::Orthograde retreatment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: If the "problem" with an endodontic treatment is only at the apex, what treatment may be indicated ? {{c1::Surgical retreatment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A surgical opening in soft tissue to release exudate and pressure is called {{c1::incision and drainage}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Localized fluctuant swelling can be first treated with {{c1::incision and drainage}}
fields.Extra: If endo origin, then RCT or extraction is indicated.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A surgical opening in hard tissue to release exudate and pressure is called {{c1::trephination}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How much root is resected in periapical microsurgery ? {{c1::3mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The resection of the apex of a tooth is termed {{c1::apicoectomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of instruments are used to instrument canals when performing periapical microsurgery ? {{c1::ultrasonic instruments}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What angle of bevel is now preferred when performing periapical microsurgery ? {{c1::0-10 degrees}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What length of root canal is obturated in periapical microsurgery ? {{c1::3mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What obturation material is most used to obturate during periapical microsurgery ? {{c1::MTA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An artificial irregularity created on the surface of a root canal wall is termed a {{c1::ledge}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Ledges are favored when canals are : {{c1::Curved::straight/curved}} {{c1::Small diameter::small/large diameter}} {{c1::Long::short/long}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The tendency of endodontic files to straighten out curved canals leads to {{c1::transportation}} or {{c1::zipping}} of the apical foramina
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What approach when confronted with a ledge during endodontic treatment {{c1::use a smaller precurved file to bypass}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Endodontic files made of what material are less likely to ledge ? {{c1::NiTi}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Endodontic files made of what material are most likely to fracture/separate ? {{c1::NiTi}}
fields.Extra: More flexible but if they bind they are fragile (true in rotary motion, hence why reciprocal movement is beneficial since they don't bind)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: NiTi files are {{c2::heat}} treated which makes them {{c1::superelastic}} and thus extremely flexible
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the two main causes of NiTi file separation ? - {{c1::Cyclic fatigue (metal fatigue)}} - {{c1::Torsional strain (locking in canal)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is it preferable to have an endodontic instrument separate early or late in the treatment ? {{c1::Later -- more canal disinfection has taken place}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What kind of perforation is due to excessive coronal flaring ? {{c1::strip perforation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep
