fields.Text: Strip perforation most often involves which side of a root canal ? {{c1::furcal side}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A root perforation has a better prognosis if it is situated in which portion of the root ? {{c1::more apically}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During endodontic treatment how are perforations treated ? {{c1::internal repair with MTA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When performing endo, what clinical sign is indicative of a perforation ? {{c1::immediate hemorrhage}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In the case of indirect pulp cap , calcium hydroxyde stimulates {{c1::odontoblast-like cells}} to form a dentinal bridge
fields.Extra: OB-like cells = secondary odontoblasts Note : dentinal bridge is not actually dentin, but an unclassified mineralized tissue
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the pH of calcium hydroxyde ? {{c1::12.5}}
fields.Extra: OB-like cells = secondary odontoblasts Note : dentinal bridge is not actually dentin, but an unclassified mineralized tissue
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the effect of calcium hydroxyde on living tissues and bacteria ? {{c1::Stimulates mineralization}} {{c1::Antimicrobial (very alkaline)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: MTA when used for apexification stimulates {{c1::cementoblasts}} to produce a mineralized tissue barrier
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What material is the opacifier used in MTA ? {{c1::Bismuth oxyde}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long is the setting time for MTA ? {{c1::3-4 hours}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 4 major properties of Mineral Trioxide Aggregate ? - {{c1::Sets in the presence of moisture}} - {{c1::Antimicrobial}} - {{c1::Nonresorbable}} - {{c1::Stimulates mineralization}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 minerals that make up MTA ? {{c1::Calcium}} {{c1::Silicon}} {{c1::Aluminum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which vital pulp therapy involves placing CaOH or RMGI on a thin partition of remaining dentin ? {{c1::Indirect pulp cap}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why use an indirect pulp cap instead of removing all carious dentin ? {{c1::Avoid pulp exposure of healthy pulp}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which vital pulp therapy involves placing a material directly on otherwise healthy pulp after exposure ? {{c1::direct pulp cap}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment for a traumatically exposed pulp if the elapsed time is less than 24h ? {{c1::direct pulp cap}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A direct pulp cap can be performed when carious or mechanical exposure is < {{c1::2}} mm
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: About how long does it take for a hard tissue barrier to form after direct pulp cap ? {{c1::6 weeks}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A partial pulpotomy is also called a {{c1::Cvek pulpotomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The removal of a small portion of coronal diseased pulp is termed {{c1::partial pulpotomy / Cvek pulpotomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment for a traumatically exposed pulp if the elapsed time is greater than 24h ? {{c1::Partial pulpotomy/Cvek pulpotomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: if a carious or mechanical exposure is > 2mm what treatment is indicated ? {{c1::partial pulpotomy/Cvek pulpotomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The removal of the entire coronal pulp is termed a {{c1::pulpotomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment for a traumatically exposed pulp if the elapsed time is greater than 72h ? {{c1::Pulpotomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In case of pulp exposure during caries excavation of a primary tooth, what treatment is indicated ? {{c1::pulpotomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the properties of Buckley's formocresol ? {{c1::Bactericidal}} {{c1::Fixative}}
fields.Extra: Mutagenic -- though "safe" for this indication it is no longer used in Europe
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The removal of vital coronal and radicular pulp is termed {{c1::pulpectomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The removal of nonvital organic and inorganic material in the pulp chamber and root canal is termed {{c1::root canal debridement}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What emergency treatment of irreversible pulpitis can be done to relieve symptoms while waiting for full RCT ? {{c1::pulpectomy}}
fields.Extra: Though sometimes just pulpotomy is done to relieve intracameral pressure
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the objective of vital pulp therapy on immature permanent teeth ? {{c1::apexogenesis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the objective of apexification ? {{c1::Obtain mineralized apical barrier}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Root canal debridement of an immature permanent tooth followed by induction of a mineralized apical barrier is called {{c1::apexification}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For all permanent tooth traumatic injuries except concussions, avulsions and crown fractures what radiographs are recommended ? - {{c1::Periapical radiograph}} - {{c1::2 additional PA with different angulations}} - {{c1::Occlusal radiograph}}
fields.Extra: For the others one PA is acceptable
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For almost all primary tooth traumatic injuries what radiographs are recommended ? {{c1::Periapical or occlusal}}
fields.Extra: Not necessary for concussion (only on primary teeth), enamel fracture
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: External resorption is due to damage of which layer of the periodontium ? {{c1::cementoblastic layer of the PDL}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of resorption is ankylosis ? {{c1::Replacement resorption}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Ankylosis is due to the fusion of bone to the {{c1::cementum}} with obliteration of the PDL
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of resorption is due to subepithelial sulcular infection from trauma or nonvital bleaching ? {{c1::Cervical resorption}}
fields.Extra: No longer an issue with internal bleaching. Resorption was most likely due to the catalytic activation of the bleaching medium.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A necrotic tooth that forms ragged margins due to bacteria and their byproducts is undergoing {{c1::inflammatory root resorption}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do external resorptions "move" with angled radiographs ? {{c1::Yes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A pink spot on a tooth is associated with what type of resorption ? {{c1::Cervical resorption}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Internal resorption is due to damage to what dental tissue layer ? {{c1::odontoblastic layer}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does internal resorption "move" with angled radiographs ? {{c1::No}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment is indicated for all types of root resorption ? {{c1::if restorable, RCT associated or not with local surgical debridement of resorption space}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Calcific metamorphosis is also termed {{c1::pulp canal obliteration}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A tooth that has suffered a trauma and now appears yellowish has most likely undergone what process ? {{c1::Pulp canal obliteration}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is a tooth that has undergone pulp canal obliteration vital ? {{c1::Yes! PCO is only possible if the pulp is alive }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is it recommended to use very little ZOE sealer when obturating with a warm vertical technique ? {{c1::It resorbs and leaves voids in the obturation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Teeth that fail to erupt within the expected developmental window are termed {{c1::impacted}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 most likely teeth to be impacted ? {{c1::Mandibular third molars}} > {{c1::Maxillary third molars}} > {{c1::Maxillary canines}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the primary etiology of impacted teeth ? {{c1::Inadequate arch length}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What teeth are most likely to be congenitally missing ? {{c1::third molars}} > {{c1::mandibular second premolars}} >{{c1::maxillary lateral incisors}} > {{c1::maxillary second premolars}}
fields.Extra: = aplasia
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When the height of contour of the tooth is above the level of bone and gingiva is completely or partially covering the tooth, it is referred to as a {{c1::soft-tissue}} impaction
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When the height of contour of the tooth is below the level of bone it is referred to as a {{c1::partial bony}} impaction
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When the whole tooth is below the level of bone it is referred to as a {{c1::full bony}} impaction
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Based on the nature of overlying tissue what are the easiest and hardest type of impacted tooth extractions ? Easiest : {{c1::Soft-tissue impaction}} Most difficult : {{c1::Full bony impaction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What classification is used to describe the position of impacted third molars based on the axis of the tooth ? {{c1::Winter's classification}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which Winter's classification extraction is most difficult to perform ? {{c1::Distoangular}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which Winter's classification extraction is the easiest to perform ? {{c1::Mesioangular}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which classification is used for impacted mandibular 3rd molars and is based on the vertical and sagittal position of the tooth ? {{c1::Pell and Gregory classification}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The level in the Pell and Gregory classification refers to the {{c1::vertical::vertical/anteroposterior}} position of the tooth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The class in the Pell and Gregory classification refers to the {{c1::anteroposterior::vertical/anteroposterior}} position of the tooth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: According to the Pell and Gregory classification, what would the most difficult mandibular third molar extraction be classified as ? {{c1::Level C Class III}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What local infectious complication is possible whenever a mucoperiosteal flap is elevated for surgical extraction ? {{c1::Subperiosteal abscess}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What perioperative precautions should be taken to avoid subperiosteal abscess after elevation of a mucoperiosteal flap ? {{c1::thorough irrigation (saline/CHX/betadine)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Oro-antral communication is most likely after extraction of which tooth ? {{c1::maxillary first molar}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why are antibiotics are prescribed after iatrogenic oro-antral communication (>2 mm) ? {{c1::sinus is sterile under physiologic conditions}}
fields.Extra: Augmentin (amoxicillin + clavulanic acid)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Oro-antral communications smaller than {{c1::2mm}} may heal spontaneously
fields.Extra: If the sinus is not already infected
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When an oro-antral communications is between 2-6mm what local treatment is advised ? {{c1::Figure 8 suture (and usually collagen sponge or similar) }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When an oro-antral communication is greater than 6mm what local treatment is advised ? {{c1::Flap surgery }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of drugs should systematically be prescribed after an iatrogenic oro-antral communication ? - {{c1::Antibiotics (Augmentin/Clindamycin)}} - {{c1::Decongestants (spray/drops such as Afrin)}} - {{c1::Analgesics}} - {{c1::Antihistamines}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An oro-antral communication can lead to what complications ? {{c1::Sinusitis}} {{c1::Oro-antral fistula (epithelialized OAC)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Premature loss of a blood clot after extraction may lead to what acutely painful condition ? {{c1::Alveolar osteitis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment for non-suppurated alveolar osteitis ? {{c1::irrigation and local/systemic analgesics}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What happens if non-suppurated alveolar osteitis is not treated ? {{c1::Self-limiting, it will heal by itself}}
fields.Extra: It will be painful but it will heal. Treatment is symptomatic. ZOE and Alvogyl dressings relieve pain but actually delay socket healing.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What teeth are most associated with iatrogenic nerve injury due to extraction ? {{c1::Mandibular 3rd molar extractions}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What immediate treatment should be started following nerve injury after extraction ? {{c1::Medrol (Methylprednisolone)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Patients with lingering dysesthesia/paresthesia/anesthesia that lasts more than {{c1::4 weeks}} after extraction should be referred for microneurosurgical evaluation .
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During extraction, maxillary first and second molars may be displaced into the {{c1::maxillary sinus}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During extraction, maxillary third molars may be displaced into the {{c1::infratemporal fossa}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During extraction, mandibular third molars may be displaced into the {{c1::submandibular space}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During extraction, if the patient swallows that tooth, what protocol should be implemented ? {{c1::Send to ER for chest/abdominal x-ray}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Bite blocks stabilize the mandible and provides comfort for the patient while improving operator {{c1::visibility}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of suction tip has a vented plastic tip and is ideal for soft-tissue suction ? {{c1::Yankauer suction tip}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of suction tip is metal and can be adequately used on hard and soft tissue ? {{c1::Frazier suction tip}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The instrument used to hold drapes around a patient is a {{c1::towel clip}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which tissue retractor forms a right angle and is used for small flaps ? {{c1::Austin retractor}}
fields.Extra: Source : Hu-Friedy
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which tissue retractor has a broad offset curve and is used for retracting the cheek or a flap ? {{c1::Minnesota retractor}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which tissue retraction has a broad heart-shaped tip and is used for retracting the tongue during mandibular lingual surgery ? {{c1::Weider (Sweetheart) retractor}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which tissue retractor is long and flat and is used for retracting flaps down to the floor of the mouth ? {{c1::Seldin retractor}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which tissue retractor is this ? {{c1::Austin retractor}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which tissue retractor is this ? {{c1::Minnesota retractor}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which tissue retractor is this ? {{c1::Weider (Sweetheart) retractor}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which tissue retractor is this ? {{c1::Seldin retractor}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The most commonly used periosteal elevator is the {{c1:: #9 Molt periosteal }} which is a larger version of the {{c2:: Woodson periosteal }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What instrument is used to elevate and reflect a flap and sever soft-tissue fibers ? {{c1::Periosteal elevator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which dental elevator has a concave blade that is oriented towards the surface of the tooth and is used as a lever ? {{c1::301 Straight elevator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which dental elevator has a triangular blade that is oriented towards the surface of the tooth and is used as a wheel and axle ? {{c1::Triangular (Cryer) elevator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The triangular (Cryer) elevator is excellent when used to remove a {{c1::broken root}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The Pick elevator is used to remove {{c1::retained or broken roots}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The large version of a pick elevator is called the {{c1::crane pick}} while the small version is called a {{c1::root tip pick}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which elevator is used to remove retained or broken roots and is used as a wedge ? {{c1::Pick elevator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which elevator is this ? {{c1::#301 straight elevator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which elevator is this ? {{c1::Triangular (Cryer) elevator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which elevator is this ? {{c1::Pick elevator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of instrument is used to luxate the tooth ? {{c1::Elevator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which extraction forceps are considered "universal" for the lower teeth ? {{c1::151}}
fields.Extra: Beak is angled slightly more down than the 150 (universal upper)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which forceps have curved beaks designed to engage the bifurcation of lower molars ? {{c1::#23 Lower cowhorn forceps}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which forceps have curved beaks to engage the furcation of maxillary teeth ? {{c1::#88 R/L upper cowhorn forceps}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which forceps are designed to extract mandibular premolars ? {{c1::#74 Ash forceps}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which forceps are designed to extract upper roots ? {{c1::#65 upper root forceps}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which blade is this ? {{c1::#15}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which blade is this ? {{c1::#10}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which blade is this ? {{c1::#11}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which blade is this ? {{c1::#12}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which surgical blade is most commonly used in intraoral surgery ? {{c1::15 blade}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which surgical blade is used for large skin incisions ? {{c1::10 blade}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which surgical blade is used for stab incisions ? {{c1::11 blade}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which surgical blade is used for distal wedges and mucogingival surgery ? {{c1::12 blade}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why use irrigation during surgical bone removal and after surgery ? {{c1::Prevents heat generation}} {{c1::Increases efficiency of surgical bur}} {{c1::Washes away inflammatory tissue and surgical debris}}
fields.Extra: *monoject syringe
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which surgical instrument is used for scraping away soft tissue after an extraction ? {{c1::curette}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why curette a socket after extraction ? {{c1::Remove granulation tissue and other soft tissue debris}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What surgical instrument is used to trim interradicular bone ? {{c1::Rongeur}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What surgical instrument is used to smooth bony contours ? {{c1::Bone file}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What surgical instrument is used to remove tori or section teeth and is tapped on by a mallet ? {{c1::Osteotome (bone chisel)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of bur should be used to section teeth ? {{c1::straight fissure bur}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of bur should be used to remove bone ? {{c1::round bur}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A bone file removes bone in the push or pull stroke ? {{c1::Pull stroke}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the surgical risk of using an air-driven handpiece during surgery ? {{c1::subcutaneous emphysema}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which surgical instrument is designed for hemostasis but is useful for blunt dissection of soft tissue ? {{c1::Hemostat}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The internal face of a needle holder has what pattern that allows grasping of suture needle ? {{c1::Crosshatch}}
fields.Extra: Source : Newmed instruments
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the primary purpose of a suture ? {{c1::Immobilize a flap or soft tissue}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Suture should be placed from {{c1::movable::non-movable/movable}} tissue to {{c1::non-movable::non-movable/movable}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which suture knot is the easiest and most common technique ? {{c1::simple interrupted suture}}
fields.Extra: Source : Olek Remesz, CC BY-SA 2.5
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which suture material has wicking properties that allow bacterial migration ? {{c1::Silk. It must be removed within a few days}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of forceps are used to grasp soft-tissue ? {{c1::Adson tissue forceps}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of forceps are used to pick up instruments or packing materials but are not designed for handling soft-tissues ? {{c1::Utility forceps}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which surgical scissors are used to cut sutures ? {{c1::Dean scissors}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which surgical scissors are used to cut fascia and dissect soft tissue ? {{c1::Mayo scissors}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What radiograph is necessary before 3rd molar extraction ? {{c1::Panoramic}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the minimum radiograph that is necessary before any extraction other than 3rd molars ? {{c1::Periapical}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which fibers are severed before beginning to luxate a tooth ? {{c1::Gingival and superficial PDL fibers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During luxation , the back of the elevator is placed against the {{c1::alveolar crest}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During luxation , the fulcrum of the lever is the {{c1::alveolar bone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the purpose of luxation ? {{c1::Tearing of the PDL}} {{c1::Expansion of alveolar bone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When using an elevator or forceps , why use slow and deliberate forces ? {{c1::Give time for expansion of bone and avoid tooth/ridge fracture}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The initial movement of the tooth with forceps for most permanent teeth is oriented {{c1::bucally}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The initial movement of the tooth with forceps for most primary teeth is oriented {{c1::palatally/lingually}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The initial movement of the tooth with forceps for conical teeth is {{c1::rotational}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why apply apical pressure when using dental forceps for an extraction ? - {{c1::Expand alveolar socket}} - {{c1::Displace center of rotation apically (minimize root fracture)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The recurrent use of a substance over the past 12 months with subsequent adverse consequences is termed {{c1::substance abuse}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The uncontrollable need for use of a substance despite adverse consequences is termed {{c1::dependence}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The need for increased amount of a substance to achieve the desired effect is termed {{c1::tolerance}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The group of symptoms that emerge upon abstinence of a habitually used substance is termed {{c1::withdrawal}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some examples of withdrawal symptoms ? {{c1::Irritability}} {{c1::Insomnia}} {{c1::Difficulty concentrating}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: About what proportion of the US population are illicit drug users ? {{c1::8.7%}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Most drugs of abuse disrupt the endogenous reward system , predominantly via {{c1::dopamine}} circuits
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Marijuana is manufactured from what plant ? {{c1::Cannabis plant}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the major psychoactive ingredient in marijuana ? {{c1::Delta-9-THC}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The primary effects of marijuana consumption are {{c1::pain}} reduction, {{c1::seizure}} alleviation, and altered {{c1::perception}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Abuse of marijuana consumption can lead to what general negative side-effects ? - {{c1::Chronic bronchitis}} - {{c1::Airway obstruction}} - {{c1::Orthostatic hypotension}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Opioids are manufactured from which plant ? {{c1::Poppy plant}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The primary effects of opioids are {{c1::pain}} reduction, modest {{c1::sedation}}, and {{c1::euphoria}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some general negative side effects of recreational opioids ? - {{c1::Respiratory depression}} - {{c1::Nausea}} - {{c1::Constipation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What common reversal agent exists for opioids ? {{c1::Naloxone (Narcan)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The electronic database that tracks opioid prescriptions and patient behaviors is called the {{c1:: PDMP (prescription drug monitoring program) }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To prescribe opioids a dentist must have a {{c1::DEA number}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Morphine and codeine are both examples of which type of opioids ? {{c1::Natural opiates}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Heroin, oxycodone and hydrocodone are examples of which type of opioids ? {{c1::Semi-synthetic opioids}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Fentanyl, methadone, and tramadol are examples of which type of opioids ? {{c1::Synthetic opioids}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Percocet and Vicodin are examples of which type of opioids ? {{c1::Combination analgesics}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cocaine is manufactured from which part of which plant ? {{c1::Coca leaf}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cocaine blocks the reuptake of {{c1::dopamine}}, {{c1::serotonin}}, and {{c1::norepinephrine}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cocaine is hard on the heart and can lead to what side effects ? - {{c1::Tachycardia}} - {{c1::Arrythmias}} - {{c1::Hypertension}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is mixing cocaine and heroin more dangerous than if they are consumed separately ? {{c1::Inverse effects may mask signs of overdose}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Amphetamines cause the release of {{c1::dopamine}} stores into the synapse
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Amphetamine type drugs are used to treat what variety of pathologies/conditions ? - {{c1::ADHD}} - {{c1::Narcolepsy}} - {{c1::Obesity (appetite suppressant)}} - {{c1::Depression}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Abuse of amphetamines can cause symptoms that are very similar to {{c1::acute schizophrenia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the first and second most abused sedative-hypnotics ? 1. {{c1::Benzodiazepines}} 2. {{c1::Barbiturates}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are a few signs of sedative-hypnotics withdrawal ? - {{c1::Nausea/vomiting}} - {{c1::Anxiety}} - {{c1::Loss of appetite}} - {{c1::Delirium}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What suffix denotes benzodiazepines ? {{c1::-am}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What suffix denotes barbiturates ? {{c1::-barbital}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Chronic alcohol use can lead to what physiologic effects ? - {{c1::Cirrhosis}} - {{c1::Hepatic encephalopathy}} - {{c1::Arrythmia}} - {{c1::Ascites}} - {{c1::GI bleeding}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Severe alcohol withdrawal that manifests as tremors and hallucinations is termed {{c1::delirium tremens}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What drugs may be administered in decreasing doses to treat alcohol withdrawal ? {{c1::Benzodiazepines}} {{c1::Beta-blockers}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which DEA scheduling category cannot be prescribed ? {{c1::Schedule I}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Heroin, LSD, marijuana, and ecstasy are schedule {{c1::I}} drugs
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Vicodin, cocaine, methylphenidate, oxycodone are schedule {{c1::II}} drugs
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Tylenol #3, ketamine, and anabolic steroids are schedule {{c1::III}} drugs
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Robitussin, Lyrica, Ibuprofen are schedule {{c1::IV}} drugs
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What kind of prescriptions are needed for schedule II drugs ? {{c1::Handwritten prescription (or called in)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What DEA schedule drugs cannot be refilled without a new prescription ? {{c1::Schedule II}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What patient precautions should be taken when a patient comes for treatment intoxicated ? {{c1::Defer and refer to PCP}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What special patient considerations when treating marijuana users ? - {{c1::Slow chair movements (avoid orthostatic hypertension)}} - {{c1::Oral cancer screening}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What special patient considerations for opioid users ? - {{c1::Have Naloxone on hand}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What special patient considerations for cocaine and meth users ? - {{c1::No treatment < 24h after drugs use }} - {{c1::No epinephrine 24h after drug use}} - {{c1::Monitor BP and pulse}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What special patient considerations for alcoholics ? - {{c1::Brief intervention/discussion}} - {{c1::Evaluate bleeding risk (liver)}} - {{c1::Oral cancer screening}} - {{c1::Avoid acetaminophen}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some non-specific manifestations of drug abuse ? - {{c1::Oral neglect (OH and appointments)}} - {{c1::Bloodborne infections in IV users }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: IV drug users are most at risk of developing what bloodborne infections ? - {{c1::Hepatitis B}} - {{c1::Hepatitis C}} - {{c1::HIV}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some oral side effects of marijuana consumption ? - {{c1::Xerostomia (and all associated problems)}} - {{c1::Leukoplakia (dysplasia to OSCC)}} - {{c1::Leukoedema}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some oral or dental-related side effects of opioid consumption ? - {{c1::Dental phobia}} - {{c1::Bruxism}} - {{c1::Caries/Perio/Candidiasis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some oral side effects of cocaine consumption ? - {{c1::Gingival recession and erosion on facial surfaces}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Meth mouth is characterized by what characteristics ? - {{c1::Xerostomia}} - {{c1::Bruxism}} - {{c1::Rampant caries, perio}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some oral manifestations excessive alcohol consumption ? - {{c1::OSCC}} - {{c1::Spontaneous gingival bleeding, and submucosal hemmorhage}} - {{c1::Sialadenitis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What do you do if a patient has severe pain and an opioid is indicated ? {{c1::Check PDMP to ensure to history of misuse/abuse}} {{c1::Prescribe the smallest efficient dose for the shortest interval likely needed}}
fields.Extra: Can always represcribe
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What pain medications can be prescribed for patients with a history of opioid abuse ? {{c1::NSAID + Acetaminophen}}
fields.Extra: Synergistic effect is even more effective than most opioids
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which extraction forceps are considered "universal" for the upper teeth ? {{c1::150}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why should a flap have a wider base than the free margin ? {{c1::maintain adequate blood supply to the flap}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When the base of the flap is narrower than the free margin, what is the risk ? {{c1::Ischemic necrosis of the whole/part of the flap}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Can flap incisions be placed over bony defects or eminences ? {{c1::No, only over intact bone (and bone that will stay intact)}}
fields.Extra: Ideally the incision should be 6-8mm from removed bone
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What 2 major structures must be avoided when raising a flap in the mandible ? {{c1::Mental foramen}} {{c1::Lingual nerve}}
fields.Extra: Ideally the incision should be 6-8mm from removed bone
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What major structure should be avoided when raising a flap in the maxilla ? {{c1::Greater palatine artery}}
fields.Extra: Ideally the incision should be 6-8mm from removed bone
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Vertical releases of flaps should be positioned at the {{c1::line angle}} of the teeth
fields.Extra: Vertical incision should not be midfacial or on the papilla
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What thickness of flap is indicated when extracting teeth ? {{c1::Mucoperiosteal flap (full-thickness)}}
fields.Extra: Vertical incision should not be midfacial or on the papilla
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of mucoperiosteal flap has 0 vertical releases ? {{c1::Envelope flap}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of mucoperiosteal flap has 1 vertical release ? {{c1::Three-cornered flap (Triangular)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of mucoperiosteal flap has 2 vertical release ? {{c1::Four-cornered flap (Trapezoidal)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of flap is often used for buccal access apicoectomy surgery ? {{c1::Semilunar incision}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A semilunar incision is done apical to the {{c1::mucogingival junction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In what part of the mouth is an apically displaced flap impossible to do ? {{c1::palatal of maxillary teeth}}
fields.Extra: Gingiva is too adherent to the bone
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of flap is often used for palatal torus removal ? {{c1::Double Y incision (or Y incision)}}
fields.Extra: Avoids the greater palatine arteries
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A sharp bend or angulation in a tooth root is referred to as a {{c1::dilaceration}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the purpose of removing buccal bone during a mandibular 3rd molar extraction ? {{c1::create purchase point}} {{c1::create a pathway for delivery}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The removal of interradicular bone during an extraction moves the {{c1::center of resistance}} apically and facilitates tooth removal
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When sectioning a maxillary molar , the tooth is split into how many pieces? {{c1::3}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When sectioning a mandibular molar , the tooth is split into how many pieces? {{c1::3}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What instrument is often used to complete the break when sectioning a molar ? {{c1::Elevator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What 3 strategies can be used to remove a root tip ? - {{c1::Gouge adjacent bone}} - {{c1::Remove buccal bone}} - {{c1::Bone window to push the root out}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why are uncontrolled diabetes and immunocrompromised patients relative contraindications for implants ? {{c1::Infection risk}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is bisphosphonate therapy a relative contraindication for implants ? {{c1::MRONJ risk}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is bruxism a relative contraindication for implants ? {{c1::implant/bone fracture risk}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is smoking a relative contraindication for implants ? {{c1::compromised oral wound healing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is a history of head/neck radiation a relative contraindication for implants ? {{c1::ORNJ risk}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is adolescent age a relative contraindication for implants ? {{c1::residual dentoalveolar growth leads to implant malposition}}
fields.Extra: Implants don't move as the dental arches grow!
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is old-age a contraindication to implants ? {{c1::No!}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Implants that are placed into alveolar or basal bone while maintaining the body of the implant within bone are referred to as {{c1::endosseus/endosteal}} implants
fields.Extra: = blade implants and root form implants
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When placing an implant, why sequentially enlarge the osteotomy ? - {{c1::Reduce heat generated (preserve bone cells)}} - {{c1::Maintain stable axis and correct if needed}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What part of the implant is [1] ? {{c1::Abutment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What part of the implant is [2] ? {{c1::Abutment screw}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What part of the implant is [3] ? {{c1::Implant body}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of implant abutment has a conical shape and a screw that is part of the abutment ? {{c1::One-piece abutment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of implant abutment needs an abutment screw ? {{c1::Two-piece abutment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of implant abutment needs an antirotation component ? {{c1::Two-piece abutment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of implant crown retainer is better for restricted restorative spaces ? {{c1::screw-retained}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why are cement-retained implant crowns more afflicted by peri-implantitis ? {{c1::Unremoved subgingival cement causes inflammation}}
fields.Extra: Source : Coronation Dental Specialty Group, CC BY-SA 3.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the major drawback of a one-piece implant ? {{c1::Cannot correct the angle between the implant and abutment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The direct histologic contact between bone and an implant surface is called {{c1::osseointegration}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Failure of osseointegration that results in the presence of a fibrous tissue between the implant and bone is termed {{c1::fibrous integration}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Primary stability of an implant is due to what ? {{c1::mechanical/frictional retention of the implant macrostructure in bone}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Secondary stability of an implant is due to what ? {{c1::osseointegration}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, Misch type I bone is found where in the oral cavity ? {{c1::anterior mandible}}
fields.Extra: Type I is best (for the INBDE) while type IV is the worst for implant placement
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, Misch type II bone is found where in the oral cavity ? {{c1::posterior mandible}}
fields.Extra: Type I is best (for the INBDE) while type IV is the worst for implant placement
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, Misch type III bone is found where in the oral cavity ? {{c1::anterior maxilla}}
fields.Extra: Type I is best (for the INBDE) while type IV is the worst for implant placement
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, Misch type IV bone is found where in the oral cavity ? {{c1::posterior maxilla}}
fields.Extra: Type I is best (for the INBDE) while type IV is the worst for implant placement
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Type I bone has the {{c1::most::least/most}} cortical bone and the {{c1::greatest::least/greatest}} cortical density while type IV has the {{c1::least::least/most}} cortical bone and the {{c1::least::least/greatest}} cortical density
fields.Extra: Type I is best (for the INBDE) while type IV is the worst for implant placement
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An implant must be placed at least 1mm from what landmarks ? - {{c1::Buccal/lingual plate}} - {{c1::Inferior border of the mandible}} - {{c1::Maxillary sinus}} - {{c1::Nasal cavity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An implant must be placed at least {{c1::1}} mm from the buccal/lingual plates, the inferior border of the mandible , the maxillary sinus , and the nasal cavity
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An implant must be placed at least {{c1::1.5}} mm from adjacent natural teeth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An implant must be placed at least {{c1::2}} mm from the inferior alveolar nerve
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An implant must be placed at least {{c1::3}} mm from adjacent implants
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When doing one-stage implant surgery, a {{c1::healing abutment}} is placed over the implant
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When doing two-stage implant surgery, a {{c1::cover screw}} is placed over the implant
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What indications might necessitate two-stage implant surgery ? - {{c1::Poor primary stability}} - {{c1::Bone graft}} - {{c1::Immunocompromised patient}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The component used to transfer location and angulation of an implant from the mouth to a master cast is called the {{c1::impression coping}}
fields.Extra: Source : Neoss
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The component used to represent the implant in the master cast is called the {{c1::analog}}
fields.Extra: Source : Baumgarten et al., PPAD. 17. 735-40.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The fake gingiva used to create implant emergence profiles on a cast is called {{c1::gingival moulage}}
fields.Extra: Source : Baumgarten et al., PPAD. 17. 735-40.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The most important component of socket preservation is {{c1::atraumatic extraction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When performing socket preservation and primary closure is not obtained, what must be done ? {{c1::Place membrane}}
fields.Extra: Prevents epithelial migration into the socket and loss of grafted material
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Gingival fibers next to implants are oriented in which direction ? {{c1::Parallel to abutment surface}}
fields.Extra: There is not connective tissue attachment to the tooth (only epithelial attachment) This leads to increased normal probing depth (4mm)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A surgical stent for guided implant therapy can give what information on implant position ? {{c1::Location}} {{c1::Angulation}} {{c1::Depth}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The traditional criteria for implant success are : - {{c1::Immobile}} - {{c1::No peri-implant radiolucency}} - {{c1::Bone loss < 0.2mm/year after first year}} - {{c1::Absence of pain}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of bacteria are associated with implant failure ? {{c1::Gram negative anaerobic rods and filaments }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Implant failure due to compromised osseointegration may occur when bone is heated to {{c1::47}} °C for {{c2::1}} minute or {{c1::40}} °C for {{c2::5}} minutes
fields.Extra: --> necrosis of bone cells
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What radiograph is best suited to evaluate mandibular fractures ? {{c1::Panoramic}}
fields.Extra: Source : Coronation Dental Specialty Group, CC BY 3.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What mandibular fractures are most common in order of most to least common ? {{c1::Condylar}} > {{c1::Angle}} > {{c1::Body/Symphysis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A bone fracture that does not go all the way through the bone is termed a {{c1::greenstick}} fracture
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A bone fracture that involves multiple fracture lines and where bone is crushed is termed a {{c1::comminuted}} fracture
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A bone fracture that is closed to the oral cavity is termed {{c1::simple}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A bone fracture that is open to the oral cavity is termed {{c1::compound}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Midface fractures are best evaluated with what radiograph ? {{c1::CBCT}}
fields.Extra: Source : James Heilman, MD, CC BY-SA 3.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A midface fracture that extends horizontally across the maxilla is termed a {{c1::Lefort I}} fracture
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A midface fracture that extends to the nasal bone and orbit is termed a {{c1::Lefort II}} fracture
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A midface fracture that extends completely from left to right and represents a complete craniofacial disjunction is termed a {{c1::Lefort III}} fracture
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A zygomaticomaxillary complex fracture involves fractures at the {{c1::lateral orbit}}, {{c1::maxilla}}, and {{c1::zygoma}}
fields.Extra: Source : I enjoy sandwiches, CC BY-SA 4.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A direct blow to what structure may result in zygomaticomaxillary complex fracture ? {{c1::Malar eminence}}
fields.Extra: Source : I enjoy sandwiches, CC BY-SA 4.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What common clinical sign is seen in zygomaticaomaxillary complex fractures ? {{c1::Bleeding under the conjunctiva}}
fields.Extra: Source : I enjoy sandwiches, CC BY-SA 4.0
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The process of returning bone fracture fragments to their normal position without surgical exposure is termed {{c1::closed reduction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The process of returning bone fracture fragments to their normal position with surgical exposure is termed {{c1::open reduction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The use of titanium bone plates to hold bone together after fracture is termed {{c1::internal fixation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The use of wires, archbars and elastics to apply forces to fracture sites is termed {{c1::intermaxillary fixation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Ideally, how are mandibular fractures treated ? {{c1::Open reduction and internal fixation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A prognathic mandible is associated with what skeletal class ? {{c1::Class III}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A retrognathic mandible is associated with what skeletal class ? {{c1::Class II}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The adjective to describe a skeletal anterior open bite is {{c1::apertognathic}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A patient with a gummy smile due to a maxilla that is too long is said to exhibit {{c1::vertical maxillary excess}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A skeletal horizontal crossbite is also called a {{c1::horizontal transverse discrepancy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A chin that is too big is referred to as {{c1::macrogenia}}, while if it's too small it's called {{c1::microgenia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What main radiograph is used during treatment planning of orthognathic surgery ? {{c1::Lateral cephalometric radiograph }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of surgery aims to correct severe skeletal discrepancies ? {{c1::Orthognathic surgery}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What surgical procedure is used to move the maxilla ? {{c1::Le Fort I osteotomy}}
fields.Extra: Source : Lai et al, Open Access (MDPI)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What surgical procedure is used to move the mandible ? {{c1::Bilateral sagittal split osteotomy (BSSO)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What surgical procedure is used to move the chin ? {{c1::genioplasty}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the general indications for a Le Fort I osteotomy ? - {{c1::Retrusive maxilla}} - {{c1::Vertical maxillary excess}}
fields.Extra: Source : Lai et al, Open Access (MDPI)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the general indications of a bilateral sagittal split osteotomy ? {{c1::Retrusive or protrusive mandible}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common post-op complication of bilateral sagittal split osteotomy ? {{c1::nerve damage (IAN)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What structures should be unaltered during a bilateral sagittal split osteotomy ? {{c1::condyle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 phases of distraction osteogenesis ? 1. {{c1::Osteotomy}} 2. {{c1::Latency}} 3. {{c1::Distraction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In distraction osteogenesis how long after the latency phase is the appliance activated ? {{c1::1 week}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Distraction osteogenesis is used to add width or length to the mandible ? {{c1::Length}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common "model" of pain ? {{c1::Biopsychosocial Model of Pain}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Axis I of the biopsychosocial model of pain refers to acute pain from {{c1::nociceptive}} input from somatic tissue
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Axis II of the biopsychosocial model of pain refers to chronic pain from the interaction of the {{c1::thalamus}}, {{c1::cortex}}, and {{c1::limbic}} structures
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Chronic pain is usually defined as lasting more than {{c1::3}} months
fields.Extra: Source : Treede RD, Rief W, Barke A, et al. A classification of chronic pain for ICD-11. Pain 2015;156(6):1003-7.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 4 major steps of the pain pathway ? 1. {{c1::Transduction}} 2. {{c1::Transmission}} 3. {{c1::Modulation}} 4. {{c1::Perception}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The part of the pain pathway where information travels from the PNS to the CNS is termed {{c1::transduction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The part of the pain pathway where information travels from the CNS to the thalamus and higher cortical centers is termed {{c1::transmission}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The part of the pain pathway that involves the limitation of flow of pain information is termed {{c1::modulation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The part of the pain pathway that represents the human experience of pain and includes the physiologic and psychological factors is termed {{c1::perception}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c2::Somatic}} pain is due to increased {{c1::stimulus}} of nociceptors
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c2:: Neuropathic }} pain is independent of stimulus {{c1::intensity}} and results from damage or alteration to the pain pathways
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Tic douloureux is another name for {{c1::trigeminal neuralgia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Trigeminal neuralgia mostly affects which population category ? {{c1::postmenopausal women (> 50y/o)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Trigeminal neuralgia pain is described as a unilateral {{c1::sharp}}, {{c1::paroxysmal}}, {{c1::electrical}}, {{c1::shooting}} pain followed by a {{c2::refractory period}}
fields.Extra: paroxysmal = violent attack/episodic
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of orofacial pain is described as unilateral sharp , paroxysmal , electrical , shooting pain followed by a refractory period ? {{c1::Trigeminal neuralgia}}
fields.Extra: paroxysmal = violent attack/episodic
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is trigeminal neuralgia unilateral or bilateral ? {{c1::Unilateral}}
fields.Extra: paroxysmal = violent attack/episodic
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the first-line treatment for trigeminal neuralgia ? {{c1::Anticonvulsants (eg. Carbamazepine)}}
fields.Extra: paroxysmal = violent attack/episodic
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the new name for atypical odontalgia ? {{c1::Painful post-traumatic trigeminal neuropathy (PPTTN)}}
fields.Extra: IASP-2016
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Atypical odontalgia or PPTTN is secondary to {{c1::deafferentation}} of nerves as a result of endodontic therapy or extraction
fields.Extra: deaffertation = "the interruption or destruction of the afferent connections of nerve cells" PPTN = painful post-traumatic trigeminal neuropathy
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Postherpetic trigeminal neuropathy is a potential sequela of which infection ? {{c1::herpes zoster}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Though most people heal completely after herpes zoster (within 3-4 weeks) without sequelae, some may have irreversible damage to skin and sensory disturbances that manifest as {{c1::postherpetic trigeminal neuralgia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatments may be used to treat postherpetic trigeminal neuralgia ? - {{c1::Anticonvulsants}} - {{c1::Antidepressants}} - {{c1::Sympathetic blocks}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Burning mouth syndrome mostly affects what demographic group ? {{c1::Postmenopausal women}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What comorbidities are associated with burning mouth syndrome ? - {{c1::Type II diabetes}} - {{c1::Malnutrition}} - {{c1::Xerostomia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Chronic headaches are also referred to as {{c1::neurovascular}} pain
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of chronic headache manifests as unilateral pulsating pain that may be accompanied by nausea , photophobia , and phonophobia ? {{c1::Migraine}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What symptoms may accompany migraines ? - {{c1::Nausea/vomiting}} - {{c1::Photophobia}} - {{c1::Phonophobia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of chronic headache manifests as a bilateral , dull ache or non-pulsating pain of mild to moderate intensity ? {{c1::Tension-type headache}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Are tension-type headaches unilateral or bilateral ? {{c1::bilateral}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What chronic headache pain manifests as excruciating unilateral periorbital pain ? {{c1::Cluster headache}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What symptomatic treatment is often prescribed for migraines ? {{c1::Triptans}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the mechanism of action of triptans ? {{c1::Selective serotonin receptor agonist}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What broad category of pain includes conversion reaction, psychotic delusion, and malingering ? {{c1::Pyschogenic pain}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Facial pain of unknown cause or while a diagnosis is pending is classified as {{c1::atypical pain}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What movement occurs in the lower joint space, as demarcated by the articular disc ? {{c1::Rotation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What movement occurs in the upper joint space, as demarcated by the articular disc ? {{c1::Translation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which muscle of mastication opens the mandible ? {{c1::Lateral pterygoid muscle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which muscles of mastication close the mandible ? - {{c1::Masseter}} - {{c1::Temporalis}} - {{c1::Medial Pterygoid}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which ligament surrounds the temporomandibular joint ? {{c1::Capsular ligament}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which ligaments maintain the articular disc in contact with the condyle during movement? {{c1::Discal/collateral ligaments}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which ligament prevents anterior displacement of the articular disc ? {{c1::Posterior ligament}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which ligament prevents posterior displacement of the articular disc ? {{c1::Lateral ligament}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which main arteries supply blood to the temporomandibular joint ? - {{c1::Maxillary}} - {{c1::Ascending pharyngeal}} - {{c1::Deep auricular}} - {{c1::Superficial temporal}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of disc displacement makes a clicking sound ? {{c1::With reduction }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Given a disc displacement without reduction , how is the mandible affected on opening ? {{c1::Limited opening and ipsilateral deflection}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Given a disc displacement with reduction , how is the mandible affected on opening ? {{c1::Ipsilateral deviation and clicking on open and closing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A shift of the mandibular midline during opening that disappears with continued opening is termed {{c1::deviation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A shift of the mandibular midline during opening that becomes greater with opening and does not disappear with continued opening is termed {{c1::deflection}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What term refers to when the condyle gets stuck anterior to the articular eminence and requires mechanical manipulation to achieve reduction ? {{c1::Condyle dislocation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment for recurrent mandibular dislocation ? - {{c1::Botox of the lateral pterygoid}} - {{c1::Surgery}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What term refers to a bony or fibrous union between the condyle and the skull ? {{c1::Ankylosis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common cause of TMJ ankylosis ? {{c1::Trauma}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How is the mandible affected when the condyle is ankylosed ? {{c1::Severely restricted range of motion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Clenching and/or grinding of teeth during the day is referred to as {{c1::diurnal bruxism}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Clenching and/or grinding of teeth during the night is referred to as {{c1::nocturnal bruxism}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What appliance may be prescribed to treat bruxism ? {{c1::Occlusal guard}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the role of an occlusal guard for treating bruxism ? - {{c1::Distribute occlusal forces}} - {{c1::Relax muscles}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The flushing out of the superior joint space is termed {{c1::arthrocentesis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What categories of nonsurgical therapy exist for treating TMJ pain ? - {{c1::Counseling (habits)}} - {{c1::Drugs}} - {{c1::Physical therapy}} - {{c1::Address occlusion}} - {{c1::Arthocentesis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Surgical disc repositioning for persistent clicking or a closed lock is termed {{c1::arthroplasty}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The surgical repair or removal of the articular disc when it is severely damaged is termed {{c1::discectomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A vertical ramus osteotomy to reposition the condyle and disc is termed a {{c1::condylotomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which nerve is most commonly damaged during TMJ surgery ? {{c1::Facial nerve (VII)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common cause of masticatory pain ? {{c1::Myofascial pain syndrome (MPS)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Myofascial pain syndrome is characterized by {{c1::trigger points}} located in the muscles of mastication
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Myofascial pain syndrome is characterized by diffuse pain in which region? {{c1::preauricular region}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A biopsy is indicated if a lesion of concern is persistent and unresponsive to treatment for how long ? {{c1::2 weeks}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which biopsy technique involves repeatedly scraping a lesion with a kit brush and immediately fixing and observing the tissue under a microscope ? {{c1::Cytology/brush biopsy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the drawback of a cytology/brush biopsy ? {{c1::Many false positives}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which biopsy technique involves the use of a needle and syringe to aspirate contents of a lesion before fixing and observing under a microscope ? {{c1::Fine needle aspiration}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Incisional biopsies are usually performed on lesions of what size ? {{c1::> 1 cm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Incisional biopsies are particularly indicated in what circumstances ? {{c1::> 1cm potentially malignant lesions}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Describe the shape and extent of an incisional biopsy : {{c1::Deep, narrow wedge that incorporates normal tissue}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why should necrotic tissue be avoided when performing a biopsy ? {{c1::No diagnostic value}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Excision biopsies are usually performed on lesion of what size ? {{c1::< 1cm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How much normal tissue should be included in periphery to excisional biopsies ? {{c1::2-3mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the typical shape of an excisional biopsy ? {{c1::Elliptical}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of biopsy is indicated for a small most likely benign lesion ? {{c1::Excisional biopsy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of biopsy is indicated for a large potentially malignant lesion ? {{c1::Incisional biopsy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why use block anesthesia instead of local infiltration when performing a biopsy ? {{c1::Avoid tissue distortion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: After excision , the biopsy sample should be stored in what medium ? {{c1::10% formalin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What surgical options may be used to remove cysts ? - {{c1::Enucleation +/- curettage}} - {{c1::Marsupialization +/- enucleation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Enucleation of a tumor may be associated with {{c1::curettage}} for non-invasive benign tumors or {{c1::resection}} for malignant or aggressive tumors
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep
