fields.Text: The surgical removal of a mass without cutting into or rupturing it is termed {{c1::enucleation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The surgical technique of slitting an abscess or cyst and suturing the edges to allow drainage is termed {{c1::marsupialization}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The surgical removal of a cyst or tumor as well as a margin of normal tissue is termed {{c1::resection}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The SPORT acronym after identifying medical emergencies signifies what ? {{c1::Stop treatment::S}} {{c1::Position patient::P}} {{c1::Oxygen::O}} {{c1::Reassure everyone::R}} {{c1::Take vitals::T}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common medical emergency in the dental chair ? {{c1::Syncope (vasovagal ++)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The most common syncope, related to a stress response is called {{c1::vasovagal syncope}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When a patient has a syncope what position helps alleviate symptoms ? {{c1::Trendelenburg position}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In a pregnant patient that has a syncope what position helps alleviate symptoms ? {{c1::Left lateral decubitus}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why place a pregnant patient in left lateral decubitus during syncope ? {{c1::Relieves pressure on the inferior vena cava}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What kind of syncope is due to a decrease in blood pressure when standing up too fast ? {{c1::Orthostatic hypotension}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the second most common type of syncope ? {{c1::Orthostatic hypotension}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Repeated rapid or intravascular anesthesia injection can lead to what medical emergency ? {{c1::Epinephrine overdose}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What differentiates stable angina from unstable angina ? {{c1::Chest pain at rest}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What emergency treatment for a patient experiencing chest pain/angina ? 1. {{c1::Oxygen}} 2. {{c1::Nitrogen (x3)}} 3. {{c1::Aspirin}} 4. {{c1::Activate EMS}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How many nitroglycerin doses should be administered before activating EMS for a patient experiencing angina ? {{c1::3 doses (separated by 5 mins)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which coronary artery is most often occluded during myocardial infarction ? {{c1::Left anterior descending artery}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Physiologically, what differentiates unstable angina from myocardial infarction ? {{c1::Ischemic necrosis of cardiac tissue}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment for a conscious patient presenting hypoglycemia ? {{c1::administer glucose orally}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment for an unconscious patient presenting hypoglycemia ? {{c1::IV dextrose or IM glucagon}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What treatment when a patient is hyperventilating ? - {{c1::Sit them upright}} - {{c1::Breathe into a brown paper bag}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why not administer oxygen to a hyperventilating patient ? {{c1::Slows the return of blood gases to normal}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Wheezing is a high pitch upon {{c1::exhalation::inhalation/exhalation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the emergency treatment of an asthma attack ? {{c1::2 puffs from an emergency inhaler}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What precautions must be taken faced with a seizing/convulsing patient ? - {{c1::Protect them from injury}} - {{c1::Do not restrain them}} - {{c1::Do not attempt to put anything intraorally}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long does a tonic-clonic seizure (grand mal) usually last ? {{c1::2-3 minutes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A seizure that lasts longer than 5 minutes is termed {{c1::status epilepticus}} and is a medical emergency
fields.Extra: if over 5 minutes, activate EMS
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What drugs can be administered during a grand mal seizure ? {{c1::Dilantin (phenytoin)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What drugs can be administered during status epilecticus ? {{c1::Diazepam (Valium)}}
fields.Extra: if convulsions last over 5 minutes, activate EMS immediately
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 2 categories of strokes ? - {{c1::Transient ischemic attack (TIA)}} - {{c1::Cerebrovascular accident (CVA)}}
fields.Extra: CVA --> hemorrhagic or ischemic
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the emergency treatment for a stroke ? - {{c1::Oxygen}} - {{c1::Activate EMS}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What blood finding is often found (and may cause) a stroke ? {{c1::Hyponatremia (low sodium)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the cardinal signs of a stroke ? - {{c1::Face droop}} - {{c1::Arm drift}} - {{c1::Speech slur}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the emergency treatment for anaphylactic shock ? - {{c1::Albuterol}} - {{c1::Epinephrine (0.3mg 1:1000)}} - {{c1::IM antihistamine}} - {{c1::Oxygen}} - {{c1::Activate EMS}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What blood test might you order to assess a patient's anemia, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia ? {{c1::Complete blood count (CBC)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What blood test might you order to assess a patient's platelet function (eg. antiplatelet drugs) ? {{c1::Bleeding time}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What blood test might you order to assess a patient on anticoagulants or with liver damage or a vitamin K deficiency ? {{c1::Prothrombin time (PT)}}
fields.Extra: or INR
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What blood test should you order to assess a patient vitamin K antagonist drugs ? {{c1::International normalized ration (INR)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What blood test might you order to assess a patient on heparin , on renal dialysis or who is a hemopheliac ? {{c1::Partial thromboplastin time (PTT)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What herbal supplements have anticoagulant properties ? - {{c1::Garlic}} - {{c1::Ginger}} - {{c1::Ginkgo}} - {{c1::Ginseng}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The written expression of American dentistry's "implied contract with society" is the {{c1::ADA's Prinicples of Ethics and Code of Conduct (ADA Code)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 5 principles of ethics ? 1. {{c1::Autonomy}} 2. {{c2::Nonmaleficence}} 3. {{c3::Beneficence}} 4. {{c4::Justice}} 5. {{c5::Veracity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For each ethical principle, what are the 3 sections of the ADA code ? - {{c1::Principles of ethics}} - {{c1::Code of professional conduct}} - {{c1::Advisory opinion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does the principle of self-determination mean that the dentist has to undertake whatever treatment the patient wants ? {{c1::No! We determine the bounds of acceptable treatment }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What response to a patient who wants treatment outside the bounds of acceptable treatment ? {{c1::Express professional opinion and refer for second opinion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which law is meant to safeguard patient confidentiality ? {{c1::HIPAA}}
fields.Extra: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: If a dentist does not obtain {{c1:: informed consent }} before treatment they could be charged with {{c2:: assault and battery }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Informed consent is based on which ethical principle ? {{c1::Patient autonomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Informed consent of a procedure requires what complementary explanations ? - {{c1::Benefits}} - {{c1::Risks}} - {{c1::Alternative treatments}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Minors younger than 18 can give what type of consent ? {{c1::implied consent OR assent}}
fields.Extra: implied consent = informal agreement assent = formal agreement ( ≠ consent)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Under what conditions can minors provide informed consent ? - {{c1::Emancipation}} - {{c1::Emergency situation}} - {{c1::Married}} - {{c1::Parent/Pregnant}} - {{c1::Military}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Who must provide informed consent for a minor ? {{c1::Legal guardian or parent}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Originals of charts and x-rays should be kept by whom ? {{c1::Treating dentist}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long should a dental office keep patient records and charts ? {{c1::As long as possible}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which ethical principle requires continuing education ? {{c1::Nonmaleficence}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which ethics principle requires knowing your limits and referring difficult cases ? {{c1::Nonmaleficence}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which ethics principle requires acting for the benefit of others ? {{c1::Beneficence}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which ethical principle prohibits refusing patients based on race, religion, gender identification etc ? {{c1::Justice}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which ethical principle prohibits slander or disparaging comments against other dentist's work ? {{c1::Justice}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which ethical principle prohibits misrepresenting care , advertising , or fees ? {{c1::Veracity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The laws that set the maximum time after an event within which legal proceedings may be initiated are called the {{c1::statute of limitations}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of statute of limitations law starts running immediately after the injury or malpractice occurs ? {{c1::Occurrence rule}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of statute of limitations law starts running after discovery of the injury or malpractice occurs ? {{c1::Discovery rule}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An expert testimony must testify to the existing {{c1::standard of care}} and how it was breached by the defendant
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In court, a person who was present during the events is called a {{c1::fact witness}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which law provides legal protection to individuals who provide reasonable assistance to individuals who are injured, ill, in peril, incapacitated ? {{c1::Good samaritan law}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What active listening technique involves repeating back what the patient says in your own words ? {{c1::Paraphrasing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The mutual sense of trust and openness between patient and dentist is termed {{c1::rapport}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The ability to understand and share the feelings of another is termed is termed {{c1::empathy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common nonverbal reaction to discomfort ? {{c1::eye and eyebrow movement}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Instead of saying phrases like "don't worry" and "it'll be fine," what should you communicate ? {{c1::Give clear expectations}}
fields.Extra: eg. during anesthesia, or for a patient who worries about post-op pain
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of question should be asked if you need to gather broad information and explain what is important to them ? {{c1::open-ended question}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of question should be asked if you need to gather specific information ? {{c1::closed-ended question}}
fields.Extra: eg. When did the pain start ?
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of question asks a patient to respond from a list of choices ? {{c1::laundry list question}}
fields.Extra: eg. Do you take X,Y,Z medications ?
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Should treatment alternative be presented in ascending or descending order of desirability ? {{c1::Descending}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What method can you employ to verify patient understanding of treatment options ? {{c1::Teach-back method}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 5 stages of change as described by Prochaska & DiClemente ? 1. {{c1::Precontemplation}} 2. {{c2::Contemplation}} 3. {{c3::Preparation}} 4. {{c4::Action}} 5. {{c5::Maintenance}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which theory describes change behavior as influenced by self-efficacy , behavorial modeling , and social reinforcement ? {{c1::Social cognitive theory}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: According to social cognitive theory , behavior change is influenced by what 3 factors ? - {{c1::Self-efficacy}} - {{c1::Behavioral modelling}} - {{c1::Social reinforcement}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which factor in social cognitive theory involves having the cognitive perception that you can execute behaviors necessary for a given situation ? {{c1::Self-efficacy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The health belief model proposes that motivation to change is influenced by what factors ? - {{c1::Perceived susceptibility}} - {{c1::Perceived costs and benefits}} - {{c1::Cues to action}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Behavioral learning by classical conditioning is based on a {{c1::stimulus}}
fields.Extra: eg. local anesthesia at every visit makes kids associate the dentist with pain
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Behavioral learning by operant conditioning is based on {{c1::consequences}}
fields.Extra: eg. positive reinforcement, negative punishment etc.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Behavioral learning by observational learning is based on {{c1::modelling}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the classic example of classical conditioning ? {{c1::Pavlov's dogs}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The process of changing a conditioned response by altering the link between the stimulus and the reaction is called {{c1::classical extinction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of operant conditioning involves doing a good thing and getting rewarded ? {{c1::Positive reinforcement}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of operant conditioning involves doing a good thing and removing a bad stimulus ? {{c1::Negative reinforcement}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of operant conditioning involves doing a bad thing and getting punished ? {{c1::Positive punishment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of operant conditioning involves doing a bad thing and removing a good stimulus ? {{c1::Negative punishment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When a behavior learned by operant conditioning is changed by altering the stimulus or reward/punishment, this is termed {{c1::operant extinction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The acquisition of a skill by observing someone else is termed {{c1::observational learning}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which behavioral strategy involves setting small attainable goals and rewarding yourself after each step ? {{c1::Shaping}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The {{c1:: Premack Principle }} involves making a behavior that has a {{c2:: high ::low/high}} probability of happening contingent on a behavior that has a {{c2:: low ::low/high}} probabillty of being performed.
fields.Extra: Eg. Getting a bed time story (high probability) if you floss your teeth (low probability)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The person-centered counseling style to assist the resolution from ambivalence to change is called {{c1::motivational interviewing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 4 components of the Oars model of motivational interviewing ? {{c1::Open questions::O}} {{c2::Affirmations::A}} {{c3::Reflective listening::R}} {{c4::Summarizing::S}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The feeling of emotional or physical tension in reaction perceived threat to one's well being is called {{c1::stress}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The subjective experience of stress is called {{c1::anxiety}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Once in the dental chair, are anxious patients rather fidgety and talkative or subdued and quiet ? {{c1::Subdued and quiet}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do anxious patient typically require more or less interpersonal distance to be comfortable ? {{c1::More}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To help manage a patient's stress, what can you do before starting ? {{c1::give clear expectations and provide information}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To help manage a patient's stress, what signal should be used to ask for a break ? {{c1::Raised hand}}
fields.Extra: Don't want them touching your hands or instruments or moving their head
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What breathing technique helps trigger a physiologic relaxation response ? {{c1::Diaphragmatic breathing}}
fields.Extra: Activates the PNS
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To help ease tension, what technique can help an anxious patient relax ? {{c1::Progressive muscle relaxation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What stress management techniques involves exposing a patient to progressively increasing feared stimuli ? {{c1::Systematic desensitization (graded exposure)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Distraction with music, TV, or video games is least effective for what type of patients ? {{c1::Hypervigilant anxious patients}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The decrease in response that occurs as a results of repeated or prolonged exposure to a conditioned stimulus is called {{c1::habituation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What coping strategy can help patients rationalize and reframe their experience ? {{c1::rational response/cognitive coping/reframing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 4 factors of cognitive appraisal of a threat ? - {{c1::Contrallability}} - {{c1::Familiarity}} - {{c1::Predictability}} - {{c1::Imminence}}
fields.Extra: These factors influence a patient's subjective perception of a threat
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Should asking a child about their fears be avoided ? {{c1::No, it can help relieve their anxity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Are anxious patients more or less likely to report pain ? {{c1::More likely}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which visual scale can be used to assess pain in children ? {{c1::Wong-Baker Faces Pain Rating Scale}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, for mild pain what analgesic drugs would be prescribed ? {{c1::APAP or ibuprofen}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, for moderate pain what analgesic drugs would be prescribed ? {{c1::APAP and ibuprofen (synergistic)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, for severe pain what analgesic drugs would be prescribed ? {{c1::APAP and/or ibuprofen and an opioid}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Preventing disease before it occurs is called {{c1::primary}} prevention
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Preventing a disease from getting worse after it has started is called {{c1::secondary}} prevention
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Rehabilitation to improve quality of life and reduce symptoms after disease is called {{c1::tertiary}} prevention
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Broadly, {{c1::topical}} fluoride acts on teeth already in the mouth , whereas {{c1::systemic}} fluoride acts on teeth that are actively developing
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does systemic fluoride act on teeth that are already in the mouth ? {{c1::Fluoride in saliva}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most cost effective and practical preventive measure to prevent caries ? {{c1::community water fluoridation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the optimal amount of fluoride in water ? {{c1::1 ppm (1mg/L)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When in the range of {{c1:: 0.7 }} ppm to {{c1:: 1.2 }} ppm, fluoride is {{c2::odorless}}, {{c2::colorless}}, and {{c2::tasteless}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: About how many people in the US live in fluoridated communities ? {{c1::210 million (75%)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the concentration of fluoride in school water fluoridation initiatives ? {{c1::~4.5ppm}}
fields.Extra: 45-50% reduction in caries!
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What fluoridation strategy is used in developing nations that do not have safe public drinking water ? {{c1::Salt fluoridation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the concentration of fluoride used in salt fluoridation schemes ? {{c1::200-350mg/kg salt}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is combining salt fluoridation and water fluoridation not recommended ? {{c1::prevent fluorosis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Fluoride supplements are only prescribed for what patient population ? {{c1::children with high caries risk in nonfluoridated areas}}
fields.Extra: Areas with < 0.6 ppm NaF
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of fluoride supplement would be prescribed to a patient ≤ 3 years old ? {{c1::Fluoride drops}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of fluoride supplement would be prescribed to a patient between 3 and 6 years old ? {{c1::Fluoride tablets and lozenges}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of fluoride supplement would be prescribed to a patient over 6 years old ? {{c1::Fluoride mouth rinse}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What concentration of sodium fluoride (NaF) in mouth rinses used weekly ? {{c1::0.2% NaF}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What concentration of sodium fluoride (NaF) in mouth rinses used daily ? {{c1::0.05% NaF}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: No supplemental fluoride is needed for which patient categories ? - {{c1::> 0.6ppm fluoride in drinking water}} - {{c1::< 6 months old}} - {{c1::> 16 years old}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Topical fluoride is better at remineralizing occlusal surfaces or smooth surfaces ? {{c1::Smooth surfaces}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Fluoride varnish has what concentration of fluoride ? {{c1::5% NaF by weight (22,600ppm)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Acidulated phosphate fluoride gel has a pH of {{c2::3.0}} and {{c1::1.23}}% fluoride.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What added benefit does stannous fluoride have over NaF ? {{c1::Antimicrobial}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 2 major drawbacks of stannous fluoride ? - {{c1::Astringent taste}} - {{c1::Extrinsic tooth staining}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the toxic dose of fluoride ? {{c1::5mg/kg}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the lethal dose of fluoride for an adult ? {{c1::5g}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What primary prevention measure is ideal for preventing occlusal caries ? {{c1::Sealants}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Sealants are recommended for which teeth ? {{c1::first and second permanent molars}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What appliance is used to protect athletes from dental trauma ? {{c1::Mouth guards}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Dental education should be paired with what other strategy to prevent oral disease ? {{c1::Behavior modification}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the main etiological factor behind caries and periodontal disease ? {{c1::Dental plaque}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Children under {{c1::6}} years old should be supervised when brushing with fluoride toothpaste
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does flossing help prevent caries ? {{c1::No}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does flossing help prevent periodontal disease ? {{c1::Yes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does the frequency of intake or quantity of sugar contribute more to caries ? {{c1::Frequency}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which antiplatelet medication decreases platelet activation by competing with ADP ? {{c1::Clopidogrel}}
fields.Extra: ADP is needed to recruit platelets to the endothelial lesion
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which anticoagulant functions by directly inhibiting thrombin (IIa) ? {{c1::Dabigatran (Pradaxa)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which anticoagulant functions by inhibiting factor Xa ? {{c1::Apixaban (Eliquis)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the biggest risk when using mercury ? {{c1::Inhalation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To clean a mercury spill a special vacuum system is used then {{c1::sulfur powder}} is applied to the floor
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the symptoms of acute mercury toxicity ? - {{c1::Hypotonia}} - {{c2::Alopecia}} - {{c3::Weight loss/GI disorders}} - {{c4::Exhaustion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Airborne particles greater than {{c1:: 50 microns }} are called {{c2:: splatter/spatter }} and fall within {{c3::3 feet}} of a patient's mouth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of airborne particles can carry blood-borne pathogens ? {{c1::Splatter/spatter}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Airborne particles less than 50 microns are called {{c2:: aerosols }} and remain floating in air for {{c3::several hours}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of airborne particles can only carry respiratory infections ? {{c1::Aerosols}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Hearing loss can be caused by noise ≥ {{c1::90 dB}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The EPA requires that water lines contain ≤ {{c1::500}} CFU of heterotrophic bacteria per milliliter of water
fields.Extra: CFU = colony forming unit (estimate of viable bacteria/fungi)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The Material Safety Data Sheet is published by whom ? {{c1::manufacturer}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The manfucturer published manual that details the hazards of particular chemical is called the {{c1::Material Safety Data Sheet}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does the color blue represent according to the NFPA ? {{c1::Health hazard}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does the color red represent according to the NFPA ? {{c1::Fire hazard}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does the color yellow represent according to the NFPA ? {{c1::Reactivity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does the color white represent according to the NFPA ? {{c1::type of required PPE (Specific hazard)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the minimum time needed for handwashing ? {{c1::15 seconds}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the minimum time needed to flush ultrasonic instruments between each patient ? {{c1::20-30 seconds}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An object or material likely to carry disease is termed a {{c1::fomite}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which forms of hepatitis are transmitted fecal-orally ? {{c1::A and E}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which form of hepatitis are transmitted via contaminated blood ? {{c1::B, C}}
fields.Extra: As well as other body fluids
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which form of hepatitis is most often a co-infection or superinfection of another form of hepatitis ? {{c1::D}}
fields.Extra: As well as other body fluids
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the risk of transmission of Hepatitis B after percutaneous injury ? {{c1::30%}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The virion of Hepatitis B is called a {{c1::Dane particle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is Hepatitis B an RNA or DNA virus ? {{c1::DNA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is Hepatitis C an RNA or DNA virus ? {{c1::RNA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the risk of transmission of Hepatitis C after percutaneous injury ? {{c1::1.8%}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is HIV an RNA or DNA virus ? {{c1::RNA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the risk of transmission of HIV after percutaneous injury ? {{c1::0.3%}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of test is used to detect HIV antibodies in blood ? {{c1::ELISA test}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the postexposure prophylaxis for HIV ? {{c1::Antivirals}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How often should health care workers receive a tuberculin skin test ? {{c1::Once per year}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How is tuberculosis transmitted ? {{c1::Inhalation of infected droplets}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the standard method of testing for tuberculosis ? {{c1::Tuberculin skin test}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Gloves should be worn whenever touching something that is contaminated with {{c1::body fluids}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How often should protective masks be changed ? {{c1::Between every patient}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The personnel most at risk of eye injury in the dental office is the {{c1::dentist}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How often should gowns be changed ? {{c1::Once per day}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which organisation is charged with protecting healthcare professionals from occupational hazards ? {{c1::OSHA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: OSHA mandates that all dental workers should be offered a free {{c1::Hepatitis B}} vaccine
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Where should clothing worn at work be washed ? {{c1::At work}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The EPA defined maximum exposure to mercury vapor is {{c1::0.1 μg/kg}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which organisation regulates transportation of dental waste from dental offices ? {{c1::EPA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The destruction of all life forms including bacteria, viruses, and spores is called {{c1::sterilization}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cold sterilization is done with what product ? {{c1::Glutaraldehyde solution}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Pressure sterilization is done at {{c1:: 121 }} °C at {{c2:: 15 }} PSI for {{c3:: 30 }} minutes
fields.Extra: 121°C for 30 mins or 132°C for 4 mins according to CDC guidelines https://www.cdc.gov/infectioncontrol/guidelines/disinfection/sterilization/steam.html
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does moist heat in pressure sterilization destroy bacteria ? {{c1::irreversible coagulation and denaturation}}
fields.Extra: Moist heat destroys microorganisms by the irreversible coagulation and denaturation of enzymes and structural proteins. (CDC)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Dry heat sterilization is done at {{c1:: 160 }} °C for {{c2:: 120 }} minutes
fields.Extra: 160°C for 120 mins or 170°C for 60 mins according to CDC guidelines https://www.cdc.gov/infectioncontrol/guidelines/disinfection/sterilization/other-methods.html
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which types of materials can be sterilized with dry heat ? {{c1::Glass and metal}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does dry heat sterilization destroy bacteria ? {{c1::Coagulation of proteins}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which gas can be used to sterilize pre-packaged items at low temperature ? {{c1::Ethylene oxide}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the difference between sterilization and disinfection ? {{c1::Disinfection does not destroy spores}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the difference between antisepsis and disinfection ? {{c1::antisepsis is used on living tissue while disinfection is done on inanimate objects}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The "benchmark" for disinfection efficacy is the ability to destroy which bacteria ? {{c1::Mycobacterium tuberculosis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long should disinfection solution be applied before being wiped off ? {{c1::10 minutes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does alcohol destroy bacteria ? {{c1::denatures proteins}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The tissue binding ability and long term action of chlorhexidine is due to its high {{c1::substantivity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the antiseptic mechanism of action of detergents ? {{c1::Helps loosen and remove microbes from surface with rinsing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the mechanism of action of quaternary ammonium compounds ? {{c1::Disrupts cell membrane}}
fields.Extra: = cationic surfactant
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What classification system is used to classify the risk of infection of reusable medical instruments ? {{c1::Spaulding classification system}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: According to the Spaulding classification system , critical risk instruments are those that contact {{c1::sterile tissue or the vascular system}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: According to the Spaulding classification system , semi-critical risk instruments are those that contact {{c1::mucosa}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: According to the Spaulding classification system , non-critical risk instruments are those that contact {{c1::skin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Critical infection risk instruments should be cleaned by {{c1::sterilization}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Semi-critical infection risk instruments should be cleaned by {{c1::high-level disinfection or ideally sterilization}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Non-critical infection risk instruments should be cleaned by {{c1::disinfection}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The monthly amount you pay per month to have insurance is called the {{c1::premium}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The predetermined rate that you pay at the time of care is called the {{c1::copayment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The amount you need to pay out of pocket before insurance kicks in is called the {{c1::deductible}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The percent of the charge that you pay after the deductible is termed {{c1::coinsurance}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The most you have to pay before insurance covers 100% of the bill is called the {{c1::out-of-pocket maximum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The list of maximum amounts an insurance plan will cover per procedure that allows dentists to charge more is called the {{c1::table of allowances}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The list of fees that the dentist has agreed upon and that insurance will cover in full is termed the {{c1::fee schedule}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In which payment plan is the dentist paid per procedure ? {{c1::Fee-for-service}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In which payment plan is the dentist paid based on the number of patients seen ? {{c1::Capitation plan}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In which payment plan is the dentist paid based on patient income and ability to pay ? {{c1::Sliding scale fee}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In which payment plan does the dentist charge the remaining balance between the total fee and what the insurance company covered ? {{c1::Balance billing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In which payment plan does the dentist get paid a predetermined fixed amount before treatment is provided ? {{c1::Prospective reimbursement}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which fraud involves the separating of dental procedures into component parts ? {{c1::Unbundling (done by dentist)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which fraud involves the combining of distinct dental procedures ? {{c1::Bundling (done by insurance)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which fraud involves reporting a more complex or higher cost procedure than what was performed ? {{c1::Upcoding (done by dentist)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which fraud involves changing a procedure code to a less complex or lower cost procedure than what was performed ? {{c1::Downcoding (done by insurance)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which fraud involves charging more than legally or ethically acceptable ? {{c1::Overbilling (done by dentist)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of insurance option limits coverage to medical providers who are under contract with them ? {{c1::HMO}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In what type of insurance option are dentists paid according to a capitation plan ? {{c1::HMO}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In which insurance type do providers agree to accept less than usual fees in exchange for a higher volume of patients ? {{c1::PPO}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In which type of managed care can any dentist see any patient ? {{c1::open panel}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In which type of managed care can dentists only see patients who are members of the managed care organization ? {{c1::Closed panel}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the principal agency for protecting the health of all Americans ? {{c1::Department of Health and Human Services}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To which government agency should elder abuse be reported to ? {{c1::Department of Health and Human Services}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The program that provides comprehensive early childhood education, nutrition, health, and parent involvment services to low-income families is called the {{c1:: Head Start program }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which government branch oversees the Head Start program ? {{c1::ACF (Administration for Children and Families)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which government branch oversees the medicare and medicaid, CHIP programs ? {{c1::CMS (Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Medicare serves what community ? {{c1::the elderly}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In what cases does Medicare cover dental care ? {{c1::Treatment is related to a medical condition}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which population does Medicaid serve ? {{c1::the destitute (low-income)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which Medicaid program requires states to take action to ensure children under 21 can access care ? {{c1::EPSDT (Early Periodic Screening Diagnostic and Treatment)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which population does CHIP serve ? {{c1::Children whose family's income is too high for Medicaid but too low for private insurance}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which program provides loan repayment for health professionals who work in underserved communities ? {{c1::National Health Service Corps (FQHC)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which law funds medical and dental care for people who have HIV/AIDS ? {{c1::Ryan White CARE Act}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which branch of the government is responsible for oral health surveillance, dental infection control , and general epidemiology ? {{c1::CDC}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which branch of the government is responsible for evaluating medical devices, drugs, and food based on efficacy and safety ? {{c1::FDA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which branch of the government is responsible for improving the health status of American Indians and Alaskan natives ? {{c1::IHS (Indian Health Service)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which branch of the government oversees biomedical and public health research ? {{c1::NIH (National Institutes of Health)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which branch of the government oversees quality and access to care research ? {{c1::AHRQ (Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which sterilization procedure is best at preserving cutting edges and least likely to corrode instruments ? {{c1::Dry heat sterilization}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 4 components of the periodontium ? - {{c1::Gingiva}} - {{c1::Alveolar bone}} - {{c1::Periodontal ligament}} - {{c1::Cementum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the predominant etiological factor for periodontal disease ? {{c1::Microbial plaque}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Loss of tooth structure caused by acidic foods/beverage or gastric acid is termed {{c1::erosion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Loss of tooth structure caused by mechanical wear of non-tooth structures is called {{c1::abrasion}}
fields.Extra: eg. hard bristle tooth brush
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Loss of tooth structure caused by mechanical wear of opposing teeth is termed {{c1::attrition}}
fields.Extra: eg. hard bristle tooth brush
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Loss of tooth structure in cervical areas due to tooth flexure is called {{c1::abfraction}}
fields.Extra: eg. hard bristle tooth brush
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Probing pocket depth is measured from the {{c1::gingival margin}} to the {{c1:: base of the pocket}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Clinical attachment loss is measured from the {{c1::CEJ}} to the {{c1:: base of the pocket}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The best measure of periodontal inflammation is {{c1::bleeding on probing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Suppuration from a periodontal pocket is indicative of the presence of what cells ? {{c1::neutrophils}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What hormones does the thyroid gland produce ? - {{c1::T3}} - {{c1::T4}} - {{c1::Calcitonin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which thyroid cells produce T3 and T4 ? {{c1::Follicular cells}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which thyroid cells produce calcitonin ? {{c1::C-cells/Parafollicular cells}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Primary endogenous hyperthyroidism is due to an increase of {{c1::T3/T4}} secretion at the {{c2::thyroid gland}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Secondary endogenous hyperthyroidism is due to an increase of {{c1::TSH}} secretion at the {{c2::Anterior pituitary}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Tertiary endogenous hyperthyroidism is due to an increase of {{c1::TRH}} secretion at the {{c2::hypothalamus}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which pathology is due to antibodies that target TSH receptors and stimulate the release of thyroid hormone (T3/T4) ? {{c1::Grave's disease}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Grave's disease is due to {{c2:: antibodies }} that target {{c3:: TSH receptors }} and stimulate the release of {{c1:: thyroid hormone (T3/T4) }} ?
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What manifestation of Grave's disease affects the eyes ? {{c1::Exophtalmos}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What medical emergency occurs when T3 and T4 are critically high ? {{c1::Thyrotoxic crisis/Thyroid storm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What clinical sign distinguishes thyrotoxic crisis from severe hyperthyroidism ? {{c1::Sudden fever}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some clinical signs of thyrotoxic crisis ? - {{c1::Sudden fever ++}} - {{c1::Loss of consciousness}} - {{c1::Atrial fibrillation}} - {{c1::Tachycardia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What course of action face to a patient with thyrotoxic crisis ? - {{c1::Activate EMS and monitor vitals}} - {{c1::Apply wet/ice packs}} - {{c1::IV hydrocortisone or oral dexamethasone}} - {{c1::IV glucose}} - {{c1::Administer propylthiouracil}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does propylthiouracil function to ease a thyrotoxic crisis ? {{c1::Makes it harder for the body to use iodine to produce T3/T4}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most comon cause of hypothyroidism in underdeveloped countries ? {{c1::Iodine deficiency}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most comon cause of hypothyroidism in developed countries ? {{c1::Hashimoto's thyroiditis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which pathology involves autoantibodies directed against the thyroid gland causing insufficient production of T3/T4 ? {{c1::Hashimoto's thyroiditis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Hashimoto's thyroiditis involves autoantibodies directed against the {{c1::thyroid gland}} causing insufficient production of T3/T4 ?
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What clinical sign may be found in both hypothyroidism and hyperthyroidism ? {{c1::Goiter}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some clinical sign and symptoms of hypothyroidism ? - {{c1::Weight gain}} - {{c1::Bradycardia}} - {{c1::Goiter}} - {{c1::Cold intolerance}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What manifestation of hypothyroidism affects bones ? {{c1::Wormian bones (extra bone between the sutures of the cranial vault)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Hypothyroidism in children is termed {{c1:: cretinism }} and manifests as stunted {{c2:: physical }} and {{c2:: mental }} growth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Cretinism is a result of {{c1::insufficient}} T3/T4 production in children
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which emergency involves critically low T3/T4 levels ?{{c1::Myxedematous coma}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What can precipitate a myxedematous coma in a dental setting ? {{c1::stress due to infection, surgery, trauma}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some clinical manifestations of myxedematous coma ? - {{c1::Bradycardia}} - {{c1::Severe hypotension}} - {{c1::Low body temperature}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What conduct faced with a patient with a myxedematous coma ? - {{c1::Activate EMS/Monitor vitals}} - {{c1::Cover with blankets}} - {{c1::IV levothyroxine and hydrocortisone}} - {{c1::IV glucose}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most ocmmon site of origin for metastasis to the thyroid gland ? {{c1::Kidney}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which pathology associates multiples neuromas , medullary thyroid cancer , and pheochromocytoma ? {{c1::Multiple endocrine neoplasia type 2 (MEN2)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the role of thyroid peroxidase ? {{c1::neutralizes Iodine to attach it to thyroglobulin to make T3/T4}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Propylthiouracil , Methimazole , and Carbimazole have what mechanism of action ? {{c1::Inhibit thyroid peroxidase}}
fields.Extra: Prevents the production of T3/T4
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Lyothyronine is a {{c1::T3}} hormone replacement
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Levothyroxine is a {{c1::T4}} hormone replacement
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What consideration when treating a patient with poorly controlled hyperthyroidism ? - {{c1::Avoid epinephrine (2 carpules maximum)}} - {{c1::Limit NSAIDs (limits circulating thyroid hormone)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What consideration when treating a patient with poorly controlled hypothyroidism ? {{c1::Avoid narcotics, barbiturates, sedatives}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The branching point between tooth roots is termed the {{c1::furcation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which factors favor furcation involvement ? {{c1::Short root trunk::Short/Long root trunk}} {{c1::Short roots::Short/Long roots}} {{c1::Narrow interradicular dimension::Narrow/Wide interradicular dimension}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which hard-tissue anatomic variation can predispose a tooth to furcation involvement ? {{c1::Cervical enamel projection}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which classification uses a Nabers probe to evaluate furcation involvement ? {{c1::Hamp classification}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which instrument is used to evaluate furcation involvement using the Hamp classification ? {{c1::Nabers probe}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does a Hamp classification class 0 signify ? {{c1::No furcation involvement}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does a Hamp classification class 1 signify ? {{c1::Horizontal involvement < 3mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does a Hamp classification class 2 signify ? {{c1::Horizontal involvement > 3mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does a Hamp classification class 3 signify ? {{c1::Through and through furcation involvement}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does a Glickman classification class 1 signify ? {{c1::Pocket formation into the flute }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does a Glickman classification class 2 signify ? {{c1::Pocket formation into the furca }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does a Glickman classification class 3 signify ? {{c1::Through and through furcation lesion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does a Glickman classification class 4 signify ? {{c1::Through and through furcation lesion that you can see through }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The normal distance from the CEJ to the alveolar crest is about {{c1::2}} mm
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Alveolar bone should be parrallel to the line connecting {{c1::CEJs}} of adjacent teeth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of bone loss is present in this lower right first molar ? {{c1::Vertical bone loss}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of bone loss is present in this upper right quadrant ? {{c1::Horizontal bone loss}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A 4 wall infrabony defect is termed a {{c1::circumferential defect}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A 3 wall infrabony defect is termed a {{c1::trough}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A 2 wall infrabony defect where the interseptal bone between two teeth is lost but the cortical plates remain is called a {{c1::crater}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the most common infrabony defects ? {{c1::2 wall defects}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A 1 wall infrabony defect is termed a {{c1::hemiseptal defect}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Miller Recession class {{c1::I}} is defined as : - Marginal recession that does not extend to the MGJ - No loss of interdental bone or soft tissue
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Miller Recession class {{c1::II}} is defined as : - Marginal recession does extend to the MGJ - No loss of interdental bone or soft tissue
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Miller Recession class {{c1::III}} is defined as : - Marginal recession that does extend to the MGJ - Interproximal bone/soft tissue loss present that prevents total root coverage
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Miller Recession class {{c1::IV}} is defined as : - Marginal recession that does extend to the MGJ - Interproximal bone/soft tissue loss present that prevents any root coverage
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which recessions can be 100% covered according to the Miller classification ? {{c1::Class I and II}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which recessions can be partially covered according to the Miller classification ? {{c1::Class III}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which recessions cannot be covered according to the Miller classification ? {{c1::Class IV}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What three descriptive features of the gums can help detect inflammation ? - {{c1::Color}} - {{c1::Contour}} - {{c1::Consistency}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Plaque-induced gingival diseases can be modified by {{c1::systemic factors}}, {{c1::medications}}, or {{c1::malnutrition}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Non plaque-induced gingival diseases can be in response to {{c1::infection}}, {{c1::allergies}}, or {{c1::trauma}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::Hyperthyroidism::Hypo/Hyper-thrydoidism}} leads to accelerated tooth eruption
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::Hyperthyroidism::Hypo/Hyper-thrydoidism}} may lead to a lingual thyroid
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some oral effects of propylthiouracial ? - {{c1::Sialoliths}} - {{c1::Oral ulcerations}} - {{c1::Necrotizing gingivostomatitis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::Hypothyroidism::Hypo/Hyper-thrydoidism}} leads to delayed eruption of teeth
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::Hypothyroidism::Hypo/Hyper-thrydoidism}} commonly leads to macroglossia
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::Hypothyroidism::Hypo/Hyper-thrydoidism}} more commonly leads to xerostomia
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Supragingival plaque is mostly composed of {{c1:: aerobic::(an)aerobic }} bacteria that are gram {{c2:: + }} near the tooth and gram {{c2:: - }} on the outer surface.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Subgingival plaque is mostly composed of {{c1:: anaerobic::(an)aerobic }} bacteria that are gram {{c2:: + }} near the tooth and more gram {{c2::-}} on the surface
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Subgingival plaque on the tooth is more gram {{c1::+}} coronally and more gram {{c1::-}} apically
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The components of supragingival plaque are derived from {{c1::saliva}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The components of subgingival plaque are derived from {{c1::gingival crevicular fluid}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Subgingival plaque associated with the epithelium is predominantly anaerobic gram {{c1::-}} bacteria
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 steps to dental plaque formation ? 1. {{c1::Pellicle formation}} 2. {{c1::Adhesion of bacteria}} 3. {{c1::Colonization and maturation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: After mechanical removal of plaque, how long does it take for the acquired pellicle to form ? {{c1::few seconds}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: After mechanical removal of plaque, how quickly do bacteria adhere and attach to the acquired pellicle ? {{c1::within minutes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Initial bacterial adhesion to the acquired pellicle is due to weak reversible {{c1::van der Waals}} and {{c1::electrostatic}} forces
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Bacterial attachment to the acquired pellicle is due to strong irreversible interaction between pellicle receptors and {{c1::adhesin molecules}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How quickly do secondary colonizers begin to colonize , signifying plaque maturation ? {{c1::24-48h}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The process where primary colonizers provide new receptors for the attachment of other bacteria is called {{c1::coadhesion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: As bacteria proliferate and the biofilm matures, there is a shift from {{c1:: facultative gram-positive }} bacteria to {{c2:: anaerobic gram-negative }} bacteria
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which bacteria belongs to the secondary colonizers and has a high capacity for binding both primary and secondary colonizers ? {{c1::Fusobacterium nucleatum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What elements of a biofilm permit the passage of nutrients ? {{c1::fluid channels}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The communication among bacteria in a biofilm is called {{c1::quorum sensing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does quorum sensing lead to in a biofilm ? {{c1::Promote growth of beneficial species and discourage growth of competing species}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Are planktonic bacteria or biofilm bacteria more resistant to antimicrobials ? {{c1::Biofilm}}
fields.Extra: This is why antibiotics for perio are useful only after debridement when the biofilm has been disorganized
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The {{c1:: red }} complex is associated with {{c2:: BOP }} and {{c2:: deeper pockets depths }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 bacteria in the red complex ? - {{c1::P. gingivalis}} - {{c1::T. denticola}} - {{c1::T. forsythia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the role of the orange complex ? {{c1::bridge the early colonizers and red complex species}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 major bacteria of the orange complex ? - {{c1::Fusobacterium nucleatum}} - {{c1::P. intermedia}} - {{c1::C. rectus}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which plaque hypothesis posited that more plaque means more disease regardless of the bacterial species ? {{c1::Non-specific hypothesis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which plaque hypothesis posited that only specific bacteria cause disease ? {{c1::Specific hypothesis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which plaque hypothesis posited that certain bacteria and host factors change the environment to favor pathogenic bacteria ? {{c1::Ecological hypothesis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which bacteria is highly associated with aggressive periodontitis ? {{c1::A. actinomycetemcomitans}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A. actinomycetemcomitans is highly associated with what type of periodontal condition ? {{c1::Aggressive periodontitis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: P. gingivalis is highly associated with what type of periodontitis ? {{c1::Chronic periodontitis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which bacteria is highly associated with chronic periodontitis ? {{c1::P. gingivalis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which bacteria is highly associated with NUG/NUP ? {{c1::T. Denticola}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: T. denticola is highly associated with what periodontal condition ? {{c1::NUP/NUG}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: P. intermedia is highly associated with what periodontal condition ? {{c1::Pregnancy gingivitis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which bacteria is highly associated with pregnancy gingivitis ? {{c1::P. intermedia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which bacteria is often found in root caries ? {{c1::Actinomyces}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which bacteria is found in coronal caries ? {{c1::S. mutans}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common oral bacteria that resides on the tongue ? {{c1::S. salivarius}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 2 types of bacteria often found around implants ? {{c1::Pseudomonas}} {{c1::Staphylococcus}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is calculus ? {{c1::mineralized dental plaque}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long does it take for plaque to mineralize into calculus ? {{c1::1-14 days}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is calculus a mechanical irritant to the gingival tissues ? {{c1::No, primary irritant is bacterial plaque that covers calculus}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Supragingival calculus is {{c1::white/yellow::color}} and mineralization occurs via {{c2:: saliva }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Subgingival calculus is {{c1::brown/black::color}} and mineralization occurs via {{c2:: GCF }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The soft white cheeselike accumulation of bacteria, salivary proteins, and epithelial cells is called {{c1::materia alba}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do extrinsic stains contribute to gingival inflammation ? {{c1::No}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Orange extrinsic stains on anterior teeth are usually due to {{c1::poor oral hygiene}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Brown extrinsic stains are usually due to {{c1::drinking dark colored beverage (or poor oral hygiene)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Dark brown or black extrinsic stains are usually due to {{c1::tobacco}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Yellow-brown extrinsic stains are often due to {{c1::CHX or stannous fluoride}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Thin black extrinsic stain lines on the cervical third of teeth are often due to {{c1::high consumption of iron (black due to chromogenic bacteria)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Green and yellow extrinsic stains usually on anterior teeth may be due to {{c1::chromogenic bacteria, poor oral hygiene}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Bluish-green extrinsic stains can be due to occupational exposure to {{c1::metallic dust}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of malocclusion has been found to contribute to plaque retention ? {{c1::crowding}}
fields.Extra: Interproximal hygiene and surfaces are harder to access
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What consequences of missing teeth lead to plaque retention ? {{c1::Mesial drift or extrusion of antagonist leads to food impaction}}
fields.Extra: Interproximal hygiene and surfaces are harder to access
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Are undercontoured or overcontoured restorations worse for gingival health ? {{c1::overcontoured}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which placement of crown margins are most associated with plaque accumulation , and gingival inflammation ? {{c1::subgingival margins}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: RPDs have what effect on the abutment teeth ? {{c1::increase mobility}}
fields.Extra: Mostly due to plaque accumulation and associated gingival inflammation
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What oral appliances can have a negative influence on periodontal health ? - {{c1::RPDs}} - {{c1::Orthodontic appliances}} - {{c1::Oral jewelry}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of tooth brushing can cause tooth abrasion and gingival recession ? {{c1::aggressive horizontal brushing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which cells are responsible for the destruction of periodontal tissues in periodontitis ? {{c1::Neutrophils}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep
