fields.Text: Through phagocytosis , neutrophils internalize bacteria and kill them using {{c1::myeloperoxidase}} and {{c1::oxygen radicals}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which proteinase is most involved in periodontal tissue destruction ? {{c1::MMP-8}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: MMP-8 is inhibited by what antimicrobials ? {{c1::tetracyclines}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Defective neutrophil chemotaxis may lead to what periodontal condition ? {{c1::Aggresssive periodontitis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which cells regulate the immune response via cytokines such as IL-8 ? {{c1::Macrophages}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: IL-8 has a distinct target specificity for what cell ? {{c1::neutrophils}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Degranulation of mast cells may be caused by antigens binding to which immunoglobulin ? {{c1::IgE}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which cells produce antibodies ? {{c1::Plasma cells (B lymphocytes)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which cells recognize and kill tumor and virally-infected cells ? {{c1::NK cells}}
fields.Extra: Can kill cells that do not have MHC Class I markers (those that are not part of the host)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which pro-inflammatory mediator leads to bone resorption ? {{c1::IL-1}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which pro-inflammatory mediator leads to macrophage activation ? {{c1::TNF-α}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of pro-inflammatory mediators leads to collagen destruction ? {{c1::MMP (matrix metalloproteinase)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 4 stages of gingival inflammation ? 1. {{c1::Initial lesion}} 2. {{c1::Early lesion}} 3. {{c1::Established lesion}} 4. {{c1::Advanced lesion (beginning of periodontitis)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The initial lesion (stage 1) of gingivitis appears within {{c1::2-4}} days of plaque accumulation
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The initial lesion (stage 1) of gingivitis is characterized by {{c1:: neutrophil }} infiltration, and increased production of {{c2:: GCF }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The early lesion (stage 2) of gingivitis is characterized by {{c1:: T lymphocyte }} infiltration, and loss of {{c2:: collagen }} due to neutrophils and macrophages
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: BOP is present starting at what histological stage of gingival inflammation ? {{c1::Early lesion (Stage 2)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The early lesion (stage 2) of gingivitis appears within {{c1::4-7}} days of plaque accumulation
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The established lesion (stage 3) of gingivitis appears within {{c1::14-21}} days of plaque accumulation
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The established lesion (stage 3) of gingivitis is characterized by {{c1:: B lymphocyte }} infiltration, and clinical changes in {{c2::color}}, {{c2::contour}}, and {{c2::consistency}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the short term goal of periodontal therapy ? {{c1::reduce inflammation by adressing causative factors}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the long term goal of periodontal therapy ? {{c1::reduce tissue damage and rehabilitate the patient}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What kind of treatment is administered during the preliminary phase of periodontal treatment ? {{c1::Treat emergencies}} {{c1::Extract hopeless teeth}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The non-surgical phase (phase 1) of periodontal therapy begins with {{c1::plaque control}} and {{c1::patient education}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the components of non-surgical periodontal therapy ? - {{c1::Plaque control and OHI}} - {{c1::Prophylaxis and SRP}} - {{c1::Removal of local factors}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Periodontal re-evaluation should occur {{c1::4}}-{{c1::8}} weeks after completion of phase I periodontal therapy
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why wait 4-8 weeks after phase I periodontal therapy to re-evaluate (charting) ? {{c1::allows time for healing and formation of connective tissue attachment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the objective of phase 2 periodontal therapy ? {{c1::Surgically reduce pockets and correct tissue defects}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the objective of phase 3 of periodontal therapy ? {{c1::Restore function (final restorations)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the objective of phase 4 of periodontal therapy ? {{c1::Maintain OHI and periodontal health}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During the first year of supportive periodontal therapy how often should patients be seen ? {{c1::every 3 months}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A risk {{c1::factor}} is causally associated with the disease
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A risk {{c1::determinant}} is an unchangeable characteristic that increases the likelihood of disease
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A risk {{c1::indicator}} is not causally associated with the disease but may influence it
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A risk {{c1::marker/predictor}} shows quantitative association with disease but doesn't alter the risk of the outcome
fields.Extra: "Direct alteration of the risk marker does not necessarily alter the risk of the outcome" Eg. Driving-while-intoxicated (DWI) history is a risk marker for pilots as epidemiologic studies indicate that pilots with a DWI history are significantly more likely than their counterparts without a DWI history to be involved in aviation crashes" (Wikipedia)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some risk factors of periodontitis ? - {{c1::Smoking}} - {{c1::Diabetes}} - {{c1::Pathogenic bacteria}} - {{c1::Microbial tooth deposits}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some risk determinants of periodontitis ? - {{c1::Genetics}} - {{c1::Age}} - {{c1::Gender}} - {{c1::Socioeconomic status}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some risk indicators of periodontitis ? - {{c1::HIV/AIDS}} - {{c1::Osteoperosis}} - {{c1::Infrequent dental visits}} - {{c1::Stress}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some risk markers of periodontitis ? - {{c1::History of periodontal disease}} - {{c1::BOP}} - {{c1::CAL}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The prediction of the outcome of a disease is called the {{c1::prognosis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why does vertical bone loss have a better prognosis than horizontal bone loss ? {{c1::potential for regenerative therapy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: For the same level of periodontal disease , do younger or older patients have a worse prognosis ? {{c1::younger (longer to live)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why does Parkinson's disease compromise periodontal prognosis ? {{c1::oral hygiene is more complicated}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which root feature of maxillary first premolars worsens periodontal prognosis ? {{c1::Mesial root concavity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most important factor in determining periodontal prognosis ? {{c1::CAL}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The removal of both supragingival and subgingival plaque and calculus is called {{c1::scaling}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The removal of embedded calculus and rough cementum is called {{c1::root planing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of instrument is used to remove supragingival calculus ? {{c1::Sickle scaler}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What shape is the cross section of a sickle scaler ? {{c1::Triangle which has two cutting edges}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of instrument is used to remove subgingival calculus ? {{c1::Curette}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What shaped cross section do curettes have ? {{c1::semicircle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How many cutting edges do universal curettes have ? {{c1::2}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How many cutting edges do Gracey curettes have ? {{c1::1}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which Gracey curettes are used for the anterior teeth ? {{c1::1-2 and 3-4}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which Gracey curettes are used for the anteriors and premolars ? {{c1::5-6}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which Gracey curettes are used for the facial and lingual of posteriors ? {{c1::7-8 and 9-10}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which Gracey curettes are used for the mesial of posteriors ? {{c1::11-12}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which Gracey curettes are used for the distal of posteriors ? {{c1::13-14}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Ultrasonic scalers are particularly useful for eliminating {{c1::tenacious}} calculus
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Ultrasonic scalers are contraindicated in which patients ? - {{c1::Pacemakers}} - {{c1::Infectious disease spread by aerosol}} - {{c1::At risk for respiratory disease}}
fields.Extra: Pacemakers no longer officially contraindicated.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::Magnetostrictive}} ultrasonics vibrate in a(n) {{c2::elliptical}} pattern
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::Piezoelectric}} ultrasonics vibrate in a(n) {{c2::linear}} pattern
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the mechanisms of action of ultrasonic scalers ? - {{c1::Lavage}} - {{c1::Cavitation}} - {{c1::Vibration}} - {{c1::Acoustic turbulence}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How should an exploratory stroke with a probe or explorer be ? {{c1::light, feeling stroke}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How should a scaling stroke with a sickle scaler be ? {{c1::short, strong pull}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How should a root planing stroke with a curette be for final smoothing ? {{c1::light to moderate pull stroke}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How should the stroke of an ultrasonic instrument be in a pocket ? {{c1::intermittent strokes with tipe parallel to the tooth in constant motion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The angulation of a curette blade when inserting should be {{c1::0}} degrees, and when scaling and root planing the angulation should be {{c1::45-90}} degrees
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What tool is used after scaling to remove extrinsic stains and residual plaque ? {{c1::prophy cup or brush}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A prophy jet delivers a slurry of water and {{c1::sodium bicarbonate}} to remove extrinsic stains and soft deposits
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of flap thickness is used in mucogingival surgery ? {{c1::Split or partial thickness flap}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A full thickness flap cuts through which tissue types ? {{c1::Gingiva (mucosa), submucosa, periosteum}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A partial thickness flap cuts through which tissue types ? {{c1::Gingiva (mucosa), submucosa}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Another name for a full thickness flap is {{c1::mucoperiosteal}} flap
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Whenever a mucoperiosteal flap is elevated, about how much bone resorption should be expected ? {{c1::1 mm }}
fields.Extra: Due to surface necrosis
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A modified Widman flap involves what 3 incisions ? - {{c1::Internal/reverse bevel incision}} - {{c1::Sulcular/crevicular incision}} - {{c1::Interdental incision}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A {{c2:: sulcular/crevicular }} incision is done by incising through the base of the {{c1:: sulcus/pocket }} to the {{c1:: alveolar crest }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An internal/reverse bevel incision removes the {{c1::pocket lining}} while preserving outer gingiva
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An {{c2:: interproximal/interdental }} incision removes the remaining {{c1::collar of tissue}} after internal bevel and crevicular incision
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common periodontal surgery used to provide access to the subgingival areas for debridement when there is sufficient keratinized tissue (≥ 3mm)? {{c1::Modified Widman flap}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When periodontal surgery is indicated but there is little keratinized tissue width (< 3mm) what type of surgery is indicated ? {{c1::Apically repositioned flap}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why chose an apically repositioned flap over a modified Widman flap ? {{c1::Insufficient keratinized tissue width (< 3mm)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the purpose of a periodontal pack ? {{c1::Protect the wound and minimize discomfort and post-op bleeding}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the main ingredient in most periodontal packs ? {{c1::ZOE}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do periodontal packs enhance wound healing ? {{c1::No!}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long are periodontal packs left in place after surgery ? {{c1::1 week}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The excision of gingiva to eliminate suprabony pockets or gingival enlargements is called {{c1::gingivectomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The excision of gingiva to reshape tissue deformities in the absence of periodontal pockets is called {{c1::gingivoplasty}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Healing after gingivectomy/gingivoplasty is by {{c1::secondary}} intention
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of surgery is performed for pocket reduction distal to terminal molars ? {{c1::Distal wedge}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Distal wedges in the maxilla are done with a mucoperiosteal flap with {{c1::parallel}} incisions
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Distal wedges in the mandible are done with a mucoperiosteal flap with {{c1::V-shaped}} incisions
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of mucoginginval surgery is used to widen the band of keratinized tissue ? {{c1::Free gingival graft}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of mucoginginval surgery is used to obtain root coverage ? {{c1::Connective tissue graft}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the objective of a free gingival graft ? {{c1::Widen band of keratinized tissue}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the objective of a connnective tissue graft ? {{c1::Gain root coverage}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The complete removal of a frenum is termed {{c1::frenectomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The incision of the frenum (mostly the lingual frenum) is termed {{c1::frenotomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of surgery is done to deepen the vestibule ? {{c1::Vestibuloplasty}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The ideal thickness of a free gingival graft is about {{c1::1-1.5mm}}
fields.Extra: Should be thin enough to permit diffusion of fluid from the recipient site but not so thin as to necrose
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Where is the most common donor site for both free gingival grafts and connective tissue grafts ? {{c1::Palate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of bony architecture is shown ? {{c1::Positive architecture}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of bony architecture is shown ? {{c1::Flat architecture}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of bony architecture is shown ? {{c1::Negative architecture}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The removal of supporting bone that is in contact with the PDL is termed {{c1::ostectomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The removal of non-supporting bone that is not contact with the PDL is termed {{c1::osteotomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of osseous surgery is done when insufficient ferrule height is available on a tooth ? {{c1::Clinical crown lengthening}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Healing with complete restoration of architecture and function is termed {{c1::regeneration}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Healing with incomplete restoration of architecture and function is termed {{c1::repair}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Formation of epithelial and connective tissue attachment after flap surgery is called {{c1::reattachment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Formation of epithelial and connective tissue attachment after periodontal regeneration is called {{c1::new attachment}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Guided tissue regeneration attempts to regenerate which tissues ? {{c1::Bone, cementum, PDL}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: From fastest to slowest, which cells repopulate a wound area after dentoalveolar surgery ? 1. {{c1::Epithelial cells}} 2. {{c1::Connective tissue cells}} 3. {{c1::PDL cells}} 4. {{c1::Bone cells}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1:: EDTA }} and {{c1:: citric acid }} are {{c2:: chelating }} agents that expose collagen fibrils through demineralization and may improve new attachment
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A bone graft from oneself is termed an {{c1::autograft}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A bone graft from another human (cadaver) is termed an {{c1::allograft}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A bone graft from another species is termed a {{c1::xenograft}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A bone graft made of synthetic or inorganic materials is termed an {{c1::alloplast}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A bone graft that acts as a scaffold is termed {{c1::osteoconductive}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A bone graft that contains growth factors and converts neighboring cells into osteoblasts is termed {{c1::osteoinductive}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A bone graft that makes bone and provides osteoblasts is termed {{c1::osteogenic}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of surgery is most common for 1 and 2-wall defects ? {{c1::resection}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of surgery is most common for 3 and 4-wall defects ? {{c1::regeneration}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of defect is ideal for regenerating infrabony defects ? {{c1::narrow 3-wall defects}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of furcation defect is ideal for regenerating furcation defects ? {{c1::Hamp class II buccal upper or buccal/lingual }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When they are indicated, when are antibiotics administered during periodontal therapy ? {{c1::Adjunct to mechanical debridement during phase I}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: In which periodontitis cases is the adjunct use of systemic antibiotics indicated ? {{c1::aggressive periodontitis (stage III grade C)}}
fields.Extra: (Sanze et al, 2020)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why are tetracyclines an interesting antimicrobial for periodontal therapy ? {{c1::Concentrates in GCF}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common adjunctive antibiotic prescription to periodontal debridement ? {{c1::Amoxicillin + Metronidazole for 14 days}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Is dose or duration more important when prescribing adjunctive antibiotics for periodontal debridement ? {{c1::Duration (14 days)}}
fields.Extra: Because it takes 2 weeks for JE to form after SRP (??)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When are local delivery antibiotics/antiseptics indicated in periodontal therapy ? {{c1::localized residual PD ≥5mm with inflammation still present following conventional therapies}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the active ingredient in Arrestin ? {{c1::Minocycline}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the active ingredient in Atridox ? {{c1::Doxycycline}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the active ingredient in PerioChip ? {{c1::Chlorhexidine gluconate}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the objective of host modulation therapy in periodontal therapy ? {{c1::downregulate tissue destruction by the host}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of periodontitis might indicate the use of host modulation therapy in periodontal therapy ? {{c1::chronic periodontitis}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Host modulation therapy would be used at what point in periodontal therapy ? {{c1::adjunct to mechanical debridement (phase I)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which periodontal host modulation therapy works by inhibiting prostaglandins ? {{c1::NSAIDs}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which periodontal host modulation therapy works by inhibiting osteoclasts ? {{c1::Bisphosphonates}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which periodontal host modulation therapy works by inhibiting MMPs ? {{c1::Subantimicrobial dose doxycycline}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Emdogain is composed of {{c1::enamel matrix proteins}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Occlusion that exceeds the reparative capacity of the attachment is termed {{c1::traumatic occlusion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::Primary}} occlusal trauma is caused by excessive forces on a normal periodontium
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::Secondary}} occlusal trauma is caused by excessive forces on a reduced periodontium
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The vibration of periodontally compromised teeth upon closing is termed {{c1::fremitus}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 2 main types of occlusal therapy used as periodontal treatment adjuncts ? - {{c1::Coronoplasty}} - {{c1::Bite guard}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When is splinting of mobile periodontally compromised teeth indicated ? {{c1::To improve patient comfort and function}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of furcation correction involves removing tooth structure from the furcation to facilitate oral hygiene ? {{c1::Furcation plasty}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of furcation correction involves removing bone from the furcation to facilitate oral hygiene ? {{c1::Tunneling}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of furcation correction involves removal of a tooth root while leaving the crown intact ? {{c1::Root amputation/resection}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What type of furcation correction involves removal of a tooth root and half the crown ? {{c1::Hemisection/premolarization}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the "best" method of tooth brushing ? {{c1::Bass method}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: About how often should a toothbrush be replaced ? {{c1::every 3 months}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What shape should floss make when used against the side of the tooth ? {{c1::C-shape}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do home water irrigation systems remove plaque from teeth ? {{c1::No! Only reduces bacterial load on gingiva and flush out food debris}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Periodontitis is most common in which ethnic group ? {{c1::males of African descent}}
fields.Extra: Chronic periodontitis++
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How is an intact periodontium defined ? {{c1::No attachment loss or bone loss}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How is a reduced periodontium defined ? {{c1::Attachment loss or bone loss present, but currently stable }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The case definition of periodontal health is defined as {{c1:: BOP < 10% of sites }} and {{c2:: PD 3mm or below }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The case definition of gingivitis is defined as {{c1:: BOP > 10% of sites }} and {{c2:: PD of 3mm or less }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The case definition of periodontitis is defined as {{c1:: interdental CAL detectable at ≥2 non-adjacent teeth}}, or {{c2:: buccal/lingual CAL of ≥3mm with pocketing of >3mm is detectable at ≥2 teeth}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What three components make up the staging component of a periodontitis diagnosis ? - {{c1::severity}} - {{c1::complexity}} - {{c1::extent}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Initial staging of periodontitis should be determined using {{c1::clinical attachment loss}}, which if unavailable can be replaced by {{c1::radiographic bone loss}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does tooth loss due to periodontitis affect the severity of periodontitis when staging ? {{c1::≤4 teeth lost is automatic stage III, anything above is stage IV}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A patient who has lost 4 teeth due to periodontitis is automatically a stage {{c1::III}} patient
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A patient who has lost 5 teeth due to periodontitis is automatically a stage {{c1::IV}} patient
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Starting at what interdental CAL is a patient automatically a stage III or IV ? {{c1::5+ mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Starting at what radiographic bone loss is a patient automatically a stage III or IV ? {{c1::> 33% bone loss}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Starting at what probing depth is a patient automatically a stage III or IV ? {{c1::≥6 mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A patient who has fewer than {{c1::20}} remaining teeth is automatically at stage IV
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Furcation involvements of class {{c1::II or III}} is an automatic minimum stage III or IV
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Vertical bone loss involvements of {{c1::≥3mm}} is an automatic stage III or IV
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The extent of periodontitis is divided into what categories ? - {{c1::Localized (<30% of teeth involved)}} - {{c1::Generalized (≥30% of teeth involved)}} - {{c1::Molar/incisor pattern (classic localized aggressive periodontitis)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What markers serve as direct evidence of periodontitis progression to establish grade ? {{c1::RBL or CAL }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: CAL or RBL of < 2mm over the last 5 years is categorized as grade {{c1::B}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: CAL or RBL of ≥ 2mm over the last 5 years is categorized as grade {{c1::C}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What markers serve as indirect evidence of periodontitis progression to determine grade ? {{c1::%bone loss/age, or case phenotype}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How is case phenotype used in determining periodontitis grade ? {{c1::Concordance of biofilm deposits with tissue destruction}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A grade B %bone loss/age is between {{c1::0.25}} and {{c1::1}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What risk factors are used as grade modifiers for periodontitis ? {{c1::Smoking and Diabetes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How many cigarettes/day puts a periodontitis patient in grade B ? {{c1::< 10/day}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How many cigarettes/day puts a periodontitis patient in grade C ? {{c1::≥ 10/day}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What HbA1c value puts a periodontitis patient in grade C ? {{c1::≥ 7%}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What HbA1c value puts a periodontitis patient in grade B ? {{c1::< 7%}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Smokers and diabetics are at minimum which periodontitis grade ? {{c1::Grade B}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The soft tissue around implants is called {{c1::peri-implant mucosa (not gingiva!)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The bone pattern of the trabeculae and interdental septa as seen in {{c2:: sickle cell anemia }} is described as a {{c1:: stepladder} } appearance
fields.Extra: - Bone pattern may have fewer but coarser trabeculae - SSA causes hyperplasia of bone marrow at the expense of cancellous bone (Oral Radiology Principles and Interpretation 2018)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Why is antibiotic prophylaxis no longer recommended for patients with prosthetic joints ? {{c1::Current evidence fails to demonstrate an association between dental procedures and prosthetic joint infection}}
fields.Extra: The 2014 Panel judged that the current best evidence failed to demonstrate an association between dental procedures and prosthetic joint infection (PJI). The 2014 Panel also presented information about antibiotic resistance , adverse drug reactions , and costs associated with prescribing antibiotics for PJI prophylaxis.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The {{c1:: chief }} cells in the parathyroid glands produce {{c2:: parathyroid hormone }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What cells are found interspersed betweeen chief cells in the parathyroid glands and have an unknown function ? {{c1::Oxyphil cells}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How many parathyroid glands are there ? {{c1::4}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What 2 major types of cells are found in the parathyroid glands ? - {{c1::Chief cells}} - {{c1::Oxyphil cells}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What effect does parathyroid hormone (PTH) have on bone ? {{c1::Increases remodelling/breakdown by stimulating osteoblasts}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Parathyroid hormone (PTH) {{c2::increases::decreases/increases}} {{c1::calcium}} reabsorption and {{c2::decreases::decreases/increases}} {{c1::phosphate}} reabsorption by the kidneys
fields.Extra: - Phosphate ions form water-insoluble salts with calcium - Decreasing phosphate concentration increases the amount of calcium that is ionized (available)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does parathyroid hormone (PTH) promote absorption of calcium in the intestines ? {{c1::Upregulates the activation of Vitamin D (in the kidneys) which increases the absorption of calcium in the intestines }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Parathyroid hormone (PTH) upregulates the activation of vitamin D in the {{c1::kidneys}} which increases absorption of calcium in the {{c1::intestines}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What effect does PTH have on serum calcium levels ? {{c1::Increases serum Ca 2+ }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What effect does calcitonin have on serum calcium levels ? {{c1::Decreases serum Ca 2+ }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What causes primary hyperparathyroidism ? {{c1::Parathyroid gland tumor}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are 2 causes of secondary hyperparathyroidism ? {{c1::Vit D deficiency or chronic renal failure}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What causes tertiary hyperparathyroidism ? {{c1::Chronic secondary hyperparathyroidism due to hyperplasia of the glands}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some general signs/symptoms of hyperparathyroidism ? - {{c1::Kidney stones }} - {{c2::Abdominal groans }} - {{c3::Painful bones }} - {{c4::Psychiatric moans (fatigue/depression/psychosis)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Hyperparathyroidism can lead to what radiographic appearance of bone due to loss of trabecular bone ? {{c1::salt and pepper appearance}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most common cause of hypoparathyroidism ? {{c1::Damage or removal during thyroidectomy}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are 2 common general signs of hypoparathyroidism ? - {{c1::Paresthesia}} - {{c1::Tetany (muscle spasms)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::Hypo::Hypo/Hyper}} parathyroidism may lead to a radiopacity in the jaws and in the skull by the basal ganglia
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are some oral manifestations of hypoparathyroidism ? - {{c1::Late eruption of teeth}} - {{c2::Dilaceration of roots}} - {{c3::Increased radiopacity in the jaws}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the chemical formula for apatite ? {{c1::Ca 5 (PO 4 ) 3 X}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the chemical formula for hydroxyapatite ? {{c1::Ca 10 (PO 4 ) 6 (OH) 2 }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How long does a pregnancy typically last ? {{c1::40 weeks}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A baby is termed preterm if it is born before {{c1::37}} weeks
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A baby is termed viable if it is born after {{c1::25}} weeks
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: During which trimester is the baby most at risk of malformation ? {{c1::1st trimester}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What effect does pregnancy have on iron concentration ? {{c1::Decreases}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What effect does pregnancy have on platelet count ? {{c1::Decreases PC}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What effect does pregnancy have on coagulation factor concentration ? {{c1::Increases}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What effect does pregnancy have on white blood cell count ? {{c1::Increases}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What effect does pregnancy have on lung capacity ? {{c1::Decreases}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Gestational diabetes is due to insulin {{c1::resistance}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: High blood pressure associated with damage to another organ system in a pregnant person is termed {{c1::preeclampsia}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the safest analgesic during pregnancy? {{c1::Acetaminophen}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When can NSAIDs be used during pregnancy ? {{c1::1st and 2nd trimesters only}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which antibiotic family should not be used during pregnancy ? {{c1::Tetracyclines}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Generally, can benzodiazepines be used for sedation for pregnant patients ? {{c1::No!}}
fields.Extra: Zolpidem (Ambien) can be preferentially used
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the safest local anesthetic that can be used during pregnancy ? {{c1::Lidocaine}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When the gravid uterus compresses the aorta and inferior vena cava in a pregnant patient, she is at risk of {{c1::supine hypotensive syndrome}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the ideal chair position for a pregnant patient ? {{c1::Reclined}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What precautions should be taken when taking radiographs on a pregnant patient ? {{c1::Lead apron and thyroid collar}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: At what point in pregnancy should elective care be ideally deferred ? {{c1::1st trimester and second half of 3rd trimester}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When should nitrous oxide sedation be avoided during pregnancy ? {{c1::1st semester}}
fields.Extra: In 2nd and 3rd trimester, limit time to 30 minutes and follow with at least 50% oxygen
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Tetracyclines are contraindicated during what trimesters of pregnancy ? {{c1::2nd and 3rd (teeth are forming)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the etiology of pregnancy gingivitis ? {{c1::plaque, but accentuated by hormones}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which red, soft tissue tumor on the interdental gingiva is common in pregnant patients ? {{c1::Pyogenic granuloma}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Naturally occuring hydroxyapatite found in enamel and dentin is actually {{c1::carbonate}} -substituted hydroxyapatite
fields.Extra: Tooth = 85% HAP by volume, 95% HAP by weight
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which part of the enamel rod/prism has the least mineral content making it more susceptible to decay ? {{c1::Tail}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What shape is an enamel prism/rod ? {{c1::Keyhole shaped}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Carbonate substitution {{c1::increases::decreases/increases}} solubility of hydroxyapatite making it {{c2::more::less/more}} susceptible to decay
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The process of sugar {{c1:: fermentation }} by cariogenic bacteria leads to the production of {{c2:: lactic }} acid .
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The free floating H + ions released by lactic acid in the mouth complexes with ions in saliva to form {{c1:: phosphoric }} acid
fields.Extra: Drives the whole reaction to the right --> DEMINERALIZATION
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the average pH of saliva ? {{c1::6.7}}
fields.Extra: 6.7 - 7.3 is normal (7 could also be an acceptable answer)
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the critical pH of enamel ? {{c1::5.5}}
fields.Extra: Enamel : 5.5 Fluorapatite : 4.5 Dentin : 6.5 Root dentin : 6.7
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the critical pH of fluorapatite ? {{c1::4.5}}
fields.Extra: Enamel : 5.5 Fluorapatite : 4.5 Dentin : 6.5 Root dentin : 6.7
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the critical pH of dentin ? {{c1::6.5}}
fields.Extra: Enamel : 5.5 Fluorapatite : 4.5 Dentin : 6.5 Root dentin : 6.7
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the critical pH of root dentin ? {{c1::6.7}}
fields.Extra: Enamel : 5.5 Fluorapatite : 4.5 Dentin : 6.5 Root dentin : 6.7
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Whic major component of saliva acts as a buffer ? {{c1::bicarbonate (HCO 3 - )}}
fields.Extra: Binds H + to form carbonic acid
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 3 mechanisms of action of fluoride ? - {{c1::Remineralizes tooth structure}} - {{c2::Decreases enamel solubility}} - {{c3::Interferes with the metabolic activity of cariogenic bacteria}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Caries is the result of the interaction between {{c1::biofilm}}, {{c1::sugar}}, and the {{c1::host}} over {{c2::time}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The interaction between biofilm, sugar, the host, over time is modeled by the {{c1::modified Keyes-Jordan}} diagram
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which shape are pit and fissure caries as they progress ? {{c1::Inverted V-shape (starts narrow, gets wider)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which shape are smooth surface caries as they progress ? {{c1::V-shape (starts wide, gets narrow)}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of dentin is wet, soft, mushy, and necrotic ? {{c1::Infected dentin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of dentin is dry, leathery, demineralized but not invaded by bacteria ? {{c1::Affected dentin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of carious dentin is OK to leave when close to the pulp ? {{c1::Affected dentin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To allow remineralization of a lesion, the surface must be {{c1::intact}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: At what point does a carious lesion become irreversible ? {{c1::Once cavitation occurs}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: About how long do it take for an enamel cavitation due to caries ? {{c1::1-2 years}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Does dentin demineralization occur before or after enamel cavitation ? {{c1::Before!}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An ICDAS 1 lesion is also termed {{c1::incipient}} caries
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An ICDAS 3 lesion is also termed {{c1::cavitated}} caries
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Caries that covers one surface of a tooth is termed {{c1::simple}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Caries that covers two surfaces of a tooth is termed {{c1::compound}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Caries that covers three or more surfaces of a tooth is termed {{c1::complex}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Caries that occur at the junction of the tooth and a restoration is termed {{c1::secondary}} caries
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Caries that remain in a completed tooth prep is termed {{c1::residual}} caries
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of caries rate damages tooth structure rapidly , is light colored, soft, and infectious ? {{c1::Acute/rampant caries}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of caries rate describes demineralized tooth structure that is almost remineralized , discolored and fairly hard ? {{c1::Chronic/slow caries}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of caries is described as hard , brown/black ? {{c1::Arrested caries}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which bacteria is most responsible for enamel caries ? {{c1::Streptococcus mutans}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which enzyme produced by S. mutans converts sucros to glucans/fructans which help the bacteria stick to the tooth ? {{c1::Glucosyltransferase}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does glucosyltransferase increase the virulence of S. mutans' cariogenic potential ? {{c1::Converts sucrose to glucans/fructans which help bacteria stick to the tooth}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: S. mutans is {{c1::aciduric}}, which means it tolerates acid well
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which primary type of bacteria are responsible for dentinal caries ? {{c1::Lactobacillus}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which primary type of bacteria are responsible for root caries ? {{c1::Actinomyces}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which component of saliva helps agglutinate bacteria to help eliminate them through swallowing ? {{c1::Glycoproteins}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which component of saliva helps destroy cell walls ? {{c1::Lysozyme}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which component of saliva binds iron which inhibits certain bacterial enzymes ? {{c1::Lactoferrin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which component of saliva inactivates certain bacterial enzymes ? {{c1::Lactoperoxidase}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which immunologic component of saliva is against targeted bacteria ? {{c1::sIgA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do incipient caries (ICDAS 1) partially disappear by wetting ? {{c1::Yes}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Do hypocalcifications partially disappear by wetting ? {{c1::No}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To identify incipient caries should the teeth be dry or wet ? {{c1::Dry}}
fields.Extra: Presence of water fills and masks the rugosities of the active lesion
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does the tactile feedback of caries feel like when using an explorer ? {{c1::A stick (softness)}}
fields.Extra: Debate over its use as there is the possibility of making a reversible lesion into an irreversible lesion
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Caries are always {{c1::smaller::smaller/larger}} radiographically than clinically
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To be detected radiographically , what percentage of mineral loss is needed ? {{c1::30-40%}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which x-ray is standard for caries detection in anterior teeth ? {{c1::Periapical}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which x-ray is standard for caries detection in posterior teeth ? {{c1::Bitewing}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When using transillumination to detect caries , where do you position the light ? {{c1::Contact areas}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which types of caries can be most easily detected using transillumination ? {{c1::Interproximal caries}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Transillumination can be used to detect interproximal caries as well as {{c1::fractures}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The bluish hue surrounding amalgam is often due to {{c1::corrosion}} products and does not classify as defective
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: An amalgam with a marginal gap or ditch of > {{c1::0.5}}mm is judged carious or caries-prone
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The chemical loss of tooth structure due to acidic foods/beverages or gastric acid is called {{c1::erosion}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The loss of tooth structure due to mechanical wear from non-tooth surfaces is called {{c1::abrasion}}
fields.Extra: eg. hard bristle toothbrush
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The loss of tooth structure due to mechanical wear from natural tooth surfaces is called {{c1::attrition}}
fields.Extra: not from restorations
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The loss of tooth structure in {{c1:: cervical }} areas due to tooth {{c2:: flexure }} is called {{c3:: abfraction }}
fields.Extra: eg. hard bristle toothbrush
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Acute pain when consuming cold or acidic foods due to exposed dentinal tubules in the root surfaces is called {{c1::hypersensitivity}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1:: Hydrodynamic theory }} postulates that pain results from dentinal fluid movement that stimulates {{c2:: mechanoreceptors }} near the predentin
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which material can be used to occlude dentinal tubules to reduce detinal hypersensitivity ? {{c1::GLUMA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the traditional order of treatment planning phases ? 1. {{c1::Urgent phase}} 2. {{c1::Control phase}} 3. {{c1::Reevaluation phase}} 4. {{c1::Definitive phase}} 5. {{c1::Maintenance phase}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the criteria for high caries risk ? - {{c1::2+ active caries}} - {{c2::Large number of restorations}} - {{c3::Poor dietary habits (sugar++)}} - {{c4::Low salivary flow}} - {{c5::Poor oral hygiene}} - {{c6::Low fluoride exposure}} - {{c7::Unusual tooth morphology}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: One of the criteria for high caries risk is the presence of {{c1::2}} or more active caries
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Incipient smooth-surface lesions should ideally be treated how ? {{c1::remineralization}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Caries-prone pits and fissures should ideally be treated how ? {{c1::Sealants}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which part of the instrument does B represent ? {{c1::Shank}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which part of the instrument does C represent ? {{c1::Handle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The ability to look at reflected oral structures through a dental mirror is termed {{c1::indirect vision}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The 3rd number in the cutting instrument formula refers to the {{c1::blade length}}

fields.Text: The 4th number in the cutting instrument formula refers to the {{c1::blade angle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which hand instrument is used to remove supragingival calculus ? {{c1::sickle scaler}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which hand instrument is used to remove subgingival calculus ? {{c1::curette}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of hand instrument is most useful for remobing infected dentin ? {{c1::Spoon excavator}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of hand instrument can be used to refine enamel margins on a caries prep ? {{c1::enamel hatchet}}

fields.Text: Does a short or large working radius increase control and accuracy when using hand instruments ? {{c1::Short working radius}}

fields.Text: A low-speed handpiece operates at < {{c1::12,000}} rpm
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A medium-speed handpiece operates at {{c1::12,000}}-{{c1::200,000}} rpm
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: A high-speed handpiece operates at > {{c1::200,000}} rpm
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the best instrument to remove affected dentin in proximity to the pulp ? {{c1::large round bur on low-speed hand piece}}

fields.Text: The pedal that controls the speed of a rotary handpiece is called a {{c1::rheostat}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1:: Carbide }} burs are better for {{c2:: end }}-cutting
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1:: Diamond }} burs are better for {{c2:: side }}-cutting
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which GV Black classification refers to pits and fissures caries ? {{c1::Class I }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which GV Black classification refers to caries on proximal surfaces of posterior teeth ? {{c1::Class II }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which GV Black classification refers to caries on only proximal surfaces of anterior teeth ? {{c1::Class III}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which GV Black classification refers to caries on proximal surfaces of anterior teeth and the incisal edge ? {{c1::Class IV}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which GV Black classification refers to caries on the cervical third of teeth ? {{c1::Class V}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which GV Black classification refers to caries on only the incisal edge of anterors or cups of posteriors ? {{c1::Class VI}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The area where the cavity preparation meets the original tooth surface is referred to as the {{c1::cavosurface margin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The yellow highlighted section represents which part of the preparation ? {{c1::Cavosurface margin}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The junction of 2 walls in a cavity preparation is referred to as a {{c1::line angle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The junction of 3 walls in a cavity preparation is referred to as a {{c1::point angle}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 4 steps of the initial tooth preparation according to GV Black ? 1. {{c1::Outline}} form 2. {{c1::Primary resistance}} form 3. {{c1::Primary retention}} form 4. {{c1::Convenience}} form
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When preparing teeth with caries, the outline form preparation should extend {{c1::0.2}}mm into dentin
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When preparing teeth with caries, the outline form preparation should extend the gingival floor {{c1::0.5}}mm around caries
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Dentin without denting underneath it is referred to as {{c1::unsupported enamel}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Primary {{c1::resistance}} form preents the tooth or restoration from fracturing under occlusal forces.
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Primary resistance form requires internal line angles that are what shape ? {{c1::rounded}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To prevent occlusal displacement of non-retentive materials, primary retention form involves making cavity walls {{c1::convergent}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To prevent proximal displacement of non-retentive materials, primary retention form involves making cavity walls {{c1::dovetail}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the purpose of convenience form when removing caries ? {{c1::Improve access and visibility}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which type of carious dentin must be removed and cannot be left behind ? {{c1::Infected dentin}}
fields.Extra: Demineralized dentin, with bacteria, no bonding potential
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What additional therapy may be indicated when deep caries excavation is close to the pulp ? {{c1::indirect pulp cap}}
fields.Extra: Guidelines for capping are changing, specifically indirect pulp caps are becoming outdated
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What additional therapy is indicated when a < 1mm pulp exposure occurs and the patient is preoperatively asymptomatic ? {{c1::Direct pulp cap}}
fields.Extra: Guidelines for capping are changing, specifically indirect pulp caps are becoming outdated
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What additional therapy is indicated when a > 1mm pulp exposure occurs and the patient is preoperatively symptomatic ? {{c1::Root canal therapy}}
fields.Extra: Guidelines for capping are changing, specifically indirect pulp caps are becoming outdated
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: How does desensitizer work ? {{c1::occludes dentinal tubules by cross-linking tubular proteins}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the 2 main ingredients in GLUMA ? {{c1::Glutaraldehyde}} and {{c1::HEMA}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are 2 the most commonly used materials used as liners ? {{c1::CaOH and RMGI}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the main purpose of a base when a metal restoration is placed ? {{c1::Thermal protection}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the most commonly used material that serves as a base ? {{c1::RMGI}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: When making an amalgam preparation what type of bur is preferred to smooth cavity walls ? {{c1::Carbide bur}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The ideal cavosurface angle for amalgam is {{c1::90 degrees}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The ideal minimum restorative thickness of amalgam is {{c1::1.5-2mm}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: To create rough walls when using composite to restore a tooth which type of burs should be used ? {{c1::Coarse diamond bur}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which prep feature refers to the beveled shoulder around a capped cusp for a gold onlay ? {{c1::Collar}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Which prep feature refers to the feather-edge margin when prepping a gold onlay ? {{c1::Skirt}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Dental amalgam is a combination of {{c1::mercury}} and some kind of {{c2::silver}} alloy
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What are the most commonly found metals in the metal alloy component of amalgam ? {{c1::Silver}} {{c1::Tin}} {{c1::Copper}} {{c1::Zinc}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The {{c1::Eame's}} ratio describes the 1:1 ratio of mercury to metal alloy in dental amalgam
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The mixing of amalgam components within the capsule is called {{c1::trituration}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What happens when amalgam is triturated ? {{c1::mercury coats the alloy particles }}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Unreacted silver-tin (metal alloy) in triturated amalgam is referred to as the {{c1::γ}} phase
fields.Extra: γ= gamma
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The strong silver-mercury matrix in triturated amalgam is referred to as the {{c1::γ 1 }} phase
fields.Extra: γ= gamma
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The weak tin-mercury in triturated amalgam is referred to as the {{c1::γ 2 }} phase
fields.Extra: γ= gamma
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What should well-triturated amalgam look like ? {{c1::shiny and smooth}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does over-triturated amalgam look like ? {{c1::warm, wet, soft, sets too quickly}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What does under-triturated amalgam look like ? {{c1::dry, dull, crumbly, sets too quickly}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Peak strength in amalgam occurs how long after trituration ? {{c1::24h}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: {{c1::High}}-copper alloys in amalgam results in only γ, and γ 1 phases
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: Amalgam with {{c1::admixed::shape}} alloy particles results in amalgam that requires more condensation force but results in better proximal contacts

fields.Text: What is the most toxic way of ingesting mercury ? {{c1::inhalation}}
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: The most toxic form of mercury is {{c1::methylmercury}} which is the organic form
fields.Source: Made by thefrenchdentiste and adapted for Booster Prep

fields.Text: What is the form of mercury present in dental amalgam before trituration ? {{c1::elemental, liquid metal}}

fields.Text: What is the form of mercury present in dental amalgam after trituration ? {{c1::mercury salts}}
